Subject: Chemistry
Title: Corrosion Of Nickel-Based Alloys
Author: Sridhar
Description: Corrosion of Nickel-Based Alloys provides a comprehensive overview of the history, metallurgy, and corrosion performance of nickel-based alloys. Blending fundamental scientific principles with practical industrial applications, the book explores corrosion mechanisms, alloy selection, and modeling approaches used across industries such as nuclear, oil and gas, aerospace, marine, and chemical engineering. An essential resource for engineers, researchers, consultants, and students working with corrosion-resistant materials.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: The Web Of Knowledge: Encyclopedias And Authority In The Digital Age
Author: Alevizou
Description: The Web of Knowledge explores the evolution of encyclopedias in the digital age, with a special focus on Wikipedia and online knowledge cultures. Blending historical analysis with contemporary case studies, the book examines how technology, media, and public participation have transformed the creation, governance, and accessibility of knowledge. An insightful resource for students and scholars of digital media, information science, and cultural studies.

Subject: Biology
Title: Flow Cytometry: First Principles, Third Edition
Author: Givan
Description: Flow Cytometry: First Principles is a comprehensive introduction to the principles, instrumentation, and applications of flow cytometry. Covering topics from cell analysis and sorting to clinical diagnostics and molecular biology, the book explains both fundamental concepts and advanced techniques in a clear, accessible manner. Richly illustrated and updated with modern applications, it serves as an essential resource for scientists, clinicians, students, and researchers working in biomedical and life sciences.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Virtual And Augmented Reality - Fundamentals And Applications
Author: Bhowmik
Description: Virtual and Augmented Reality: Fundamentals and Applications provides a comprehensive introduction to the principles, technologies, and real-world applications of virtual and augmented reality. Covering topics such as immersive system design, human sensory perception, AI-driven interaction, 3D graphics, spatial audio, and motion tracking, the book bridges foundational theory with practical implementation. Featuring case studies across healthcare, education, entertainment, tourism, and industry, it is an essential resource for students, researchers, engineers, and professionals working in immersive technologies.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Deep Learning In Quantitative Finance
Author: Green

Drawing on his experience scaling tech companies to over 100 million users, Colin Hodge shares practical frameworks for improving user acquisition, retention, engagement, branding, and customer loyalty through mindset development and psychology-driven product design.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Beyond Distinction: How Leaders Transcend The Turbulence Of An Ai-Transformed World
Author: Mckain
Description: Beyond Distinction explores how leaders can build organizations that achieve lasting competitive advantage in an AI-driven business world. Scott McKain provides practical strategies for creating meaningful customer relationships, attracting top talent, overcoming competitor imitation, and embedding distinction into company culture to drive long-term growth and relevance.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Working Scared: Blueprints For Employees And Leaders To Succeed During Turbulent Times
Author: Silverman
Description: Working Scared provides practical strategies for employees and leaders navigating workplace uncertainty in today’s rapidly changing business environment. Covering challenges such as AI transformation, hybrid work, economic instability, restructuring, and difficult leadership, the book combines research, real-world case studies, and actionable frameworks to help professionals build resilience, adaptability, and long-term career success.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Membrane Technology For Environmental Remediation - From Principles To Applied Solutions
Author: Shen
Description: Membrane Technology for Environmental Remediation provides a comprehensive guide to advanced membrane science and its applications in water treatment, pollution control, and sustainable resource recovery. Covering membrane design, AI-enabled system optimization, anti-fouling strategies, catalytic and smart membranes, and scalable remediation technologies, the book equips engineers, researchers, and environmental professionals with practical solutions for modern environmental challenges.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Brainstorm: A Guide To Neurodivergent Talent And The Future Of Work
Author: Thompson
Description: Brainstorm: A Guide to Neurodivergent Talent and the Future of Work explores how organizations can create more inclusive, innovative, and high-performing workplaces by embracing neurodiversity. Combining practical strategies, real-world insights, and workplace frameworks, Dave Thompson shows leaders and teams how to rethink hiring, management, communication, and workplace culture to unlock the full potential of neurodivergent talent.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Cheers To Monday: The Surprisingly Simple Method To Un-Stress Your Work And Transform Your Life
Author: Leneker
Description: Cheers to Monday challenges the modern culture of stress and burnout by showing that chronic stress is not a sign of success, but a barrier to wellbeing and performance. Drawing on leadership coaching, real-world experience, and science-backed strategies, Amy Leneker provides practical tools to help professionals reduce stress, regain energy and focus, and create healthier, more joyful approaches to work and leadership.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Great Leaders Care: Building Psychologically Safe And Resilient Teams
Author: Cowan
Description: Great Leaders Care presents a practical and evidence-based approach to leadership that prioritizes wellbeing, resilience, and high performance. Drawing on leadership research, mental health advocacy, and real-world executive insights, Graeme Cowan shows leaders how to build caring workplace cultures, prevent burnout, strengthen psychological safety, and create teams that thrive both personally and professionally.

Subject: For Dummies
Title: Passive Income For Dummies
Author: Mladjenovic
Description: Passive Income For Dummies provides practical, beginner-friendly strategies for building multiple streams of passive income through investments, digital products, affiliate marketing, subscriptions, and other income-generating opportunities. Drawing on decades of experience, Paul Mladjenovic offers step-by-step guidance, automation tips, and risk-management advice to help readers create greater financial security and long-term wealth.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Data Science First: Using Language Models In Ai-Enabled Applications
Author: Hawkins
Description: Data Science First explores how data scientists can effectively integrate language models into real-world AI applications while maintaining reliability, accuracy, and business value. Combining practical workflows, implementation patterns, case studies, and evaluation methods, John Hawkins demonstrates how generative AI can enhance traditional data science across industries such as banking, telecom, education, and insurance.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Warren Buffett Ceo: Secrets From The Berkshire Hathaway Managers, 25Th Anniversary Edition
Author: Miles
Description: The Warren Buffett CEO, 25th Anniversary Edition, offers an in-depth look at the evolution of Berkshire Hathaway, exploring its extraordinary growth, leadership culture, and succession from Warren Buffett to Greg Abel. Through executive insights, business case studies, and lessons from Berkshire’s insurance, railroad, energy, and manufacturing operations, the book reveals the strategies, leadership principles, and culture behind one of the world’s most successful conglomerates.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Data Makes The World Go 'Round: The Data, Tech, And Trust Behind Ai Success
Author: Halper
Description: Data Makes the World Go ‘Round provides a practical roadmap for organizations seeking to implement AI successfully through strong data, technology, and governance foundations. Combining real-world case studies, leadership insights, and actionable strategies, Fern Halper explains how businesses can build scalable data architectures, operationalize AI, manage risk and ethics, and align AI initiatives with organizational goals and measurable outcomes.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Making Ai Work For People - A Framework For Designing And Building Impactful Ai-Powered Applications
Author: Ibrahim
Description: Making AI Work for People presents a human-centered framework for designing AI applications that are ethical, trustworthy, and impactful. Through the PRESS framework, Asmaa Ibrahim guides software engineers, product managers, and designers in building AI-powered solutions that prioritize people, explainability, safety, responsibility, and long-term value while solving real-world business and user challenges.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Leading Beyond Fear: The Formula For Psychological Safety, Adaptability, And Growth
Author: Pretty
Description: Leading Beyond Fear is a practical leadership guide focused on building psychological safety, strong workplace culture, and high-performing teams. Neil Pretty provides actionable strategies to help leaders develop confidence, influence, adaptability, and authentic leadership skills while fostering trust, collaboration, innovation, and growth in modern organizations.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Untangling Ai - Driving Business Success Through Enterprise Automation And Ai Agents
Author: Kesby
Description: Untangling AI provides a practical roadmap for organizations looking to adopt AI and enterprise automation at scale. Matt Kesby explains how leaders can integrate AI agents into business operations, automate workflows, build AI-ready teams, and create effective strategies around technology, execution, ethics, and communication to drive long-term business transformation and efficiency.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Valuable And Visible: Redefining Personal Branding By Leading With Impact Over Image
Author: Errecarte
Description: Valuable & Visible redefines personal branding by showing professionals how to stand out through clarity, contribution, and memorable ideas rather than self-promotion. Vanessa Errecarte provides practical strategies to help readers build trust, communicate expertise effectively, create meaningful visibility, and become recognized for a distinct point of view that attracts opportunities and long-term success.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Warren Buffett Portfolio: Mastering The Power Of The Focus Investment Strategy, 25Th Anniversary Edition, Wiley Investment Classics
Author: Hagstrom
Description: The Warren Buffett Portfolio, 25th Anniversary Edition, explores the principles of focus investing through the proven strategies of Warren Buffett and insights from Charlie Munger. Robert G. Hagstrom combines investment psychology, probability theory, market behavior, and practical portfolio management to show investors how concentrated, business-driven investing can create long-term wealth and outperform traditional diversification strategies.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Good Work: Transform Your Work From The Inside Out
Author: Kathryn Page
Description: Good Work explores how modern workplaces can move beyond burnout, disconnection, and outdated work systems to create healthier, more meaningful environments. Drawing on psychological research and real-world examples, Dr. Kat Page provides practical strategies for building connected teams, improving workplace wellbeing, fostering honest communication, and creating sustainable cultures where people and organizations can thrive together.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: The Pricing Playbook: A Practical Guide To Revenue Growth Management In The Age Of Ai
Author: Reinhardt
Description: The Pricing Playbook provides a practical framework for building effective pricing and revenue growth strategies in the age of AI. Ingo Reinhardt combines pricing theory, behavioral economics, and real-world case studies to help business leaders understand value pricing, price elasticity, and AI-driven decision-making while creating data-informed strategies that drive sustainable growth and profitability.

Subject: General
Title: Aquatic Pollution: An Introductory Text
Author: Laws
Description: Aquatic Pollution: An Introductory Text, Fifth Edition, provides a comprehensive overview of pollution affecting freshwater and marine ecosystems. Covering topics such as climate change impacts, ocean acidification, persistent organic pollutants, nutrient loading, oil spills, and urban runoff, the book combines updated research, real-world case studies, and environmental science fundamentals to help students, researchers, policymakers, and practitioners understand and address aquatic pollution challenges.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Robot-Proof: When Machines Have All The Answers, Build Better People
Author: Ming
Description: Robot-Proof explores how people, businesses, and societies can thrive in an AI-driven world by focusing on uniquely human strengths. Dr. Vivienne Ming combines insights from artificial intelligence, psychology, and human behavior to examine the future of work, education, teamwork, and problem-solving, showing how adaptability, creativity, and human-centered thinking can help individuals remain valuable in an increasingly automated future.

Subject: General
Title: Laser Materials Processing And Manufacturing Techniques
Author: Bahadur
Description: Laser Materials Processing provides a comprehensive introduction to the principles, technologies, and industrial applications of laser-based manufacturing. Covering techniques such as cutting, welding, drilling, surface treatment, micromachining, and additive manufacturing, the book explores laser-material interactions, process optimization, and real-world industrial use cases, making it a valuable resource for engineers, researchers, and students in manufacturing and industrial engineering.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Adversarial Machine Learning: Mechanisms, Vulnerabilities, And Strategies For Trustworthy Ai
Author: Edwards
Description: Adversarial Machine Learning is a practical and comprehensive guide to securing AI systems against modern adversarial threats. Covering the full machine learning lifecycle, the book explains attacks such as evasion, poisoning, backdoor, privacy, and generative AI exploits, while also exploring defence strategies including adversarial training, certified defences, and privacy-preserving techniques. Designed for developers, security professionals, and AI leaders, it provides both technical depth and real-world guidance for building resilient and trustworthy AI systems.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: A Leader Worth Following: How To Master Yourself, Influence Others, And Create Exceptional Experiences
Author: Granger
Description: A Leader Worth Following presents a modern, human-centered approach to leadership built on emotional intelligence, empathy, communication, and psychological insight. Dr. Benjamin Granger combines workplace psychology and experience management research to help leaders build trust, strengthen relationships, improve influence, and inspire high performance through compassionate and effective leadership practices.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Capitalism For All: Inclusive Economics And The Future-Proofing Of America
Author: Bryant
Description: Capitalism For All explores how inclusive economics can rebuild prosperity, strengthen the middle class, and future-proof society in an era of rapid technological change. Drawing on decades of experience in financial empowerment and community development, John Hope Bryant presents practical strategies for expanding economic opportunity, leveraging AI and technology responsibly, and creating a more equitable form of capitalism that benefits businesses, communities, and society as a whole.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: The Ai Illusion: Why Machines Aren'T Creative
Author: Julia
Description: The AI Illusion examines the realities and limitations of generative AI, challenging the hype surrounding machine creativity and artificial general intelligence. Drawing on decades of experience in the tech industry, Luc Julia explains how AI systems work, why they rely on recombining existing data rather than true understanding, and the societal, economic, and environmental impacts of AI adoption while advocating for more responsible and realistic approaches to AI development.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Humanity's Edge: How Self-Leadership And Accountability Build High-Performing Teams In The Age Of Ai
Author: Solanki
Description: Humanity’s Edge explores how self-leadership, accountability, and authenticity can help leaders build high-performing teams in an AI-driven workplace. Drawing on psychology, executive coaching, and real-world business experience, Baiju Solanki provides practical strategies for developing self-awareness, empowering teams, strengthening workplace culture, and leading confidently through hybrid work and technological change.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Managing Operational Risk In A Changing World
Author: Cagan
Description: Managing Operational Risk in a Changing World provides a practical guide to addressing modern operational risks in an increasingly complex business environment. Combining decades of industry experience with real-world case studies and expert interviews, the book explores emerging challenges such as cyber threats, AI, ESG, climate risk, geopolitical events, and evolving regulations, offering actionable frameworks and strategies for risk professionals, students, and business leaders.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Artificial Intelligence And Computational Modeling In Heat Transfer And Fluid Dynamics
Author: Awasthi
Description: Artificial Intelligence in Heat Transfer and Fluid Dynamics explores how AI and machine learning are transforming thermal sciences by enhancing traditional computational methods used in heat transfer and fluid dynamics. Covering topics such as AI-driven optimisation, neural network modelling, predictive maintenance, turbulence analysis, microfluidics, and real-time flow control, the book combines theory, practical applications, and industrial case studies to help engineers and researchers solve complex thermal and fluid engineering challenges more efficiently.

Subject: General
Title: Immigration: How The Past Shapes The Present
Author: Foner
Description: Immigration: How the Past Shapes the Present examines how America’s immigration history continues to influence modern immigration debates, policies, and social attitudes. Drawing on historical and contemporary research, the book explores migration patterns, public perceptions, and the roots of modern nativism, offering valuable insights into how past immigration experiences shape present-day society and future challenges.

Subject: Economics
Title: Beyond Generation Rent: Political Economy, Inequality, And The Private Rental Sector
Author: Byrne
Description: Beyond Generation Rent explores the growing inequalities within modern housing systems and the global rise of private renting. Drawing on international research and case studies, Michael Byrne examines issues such as financialization, landlord power, evictions, declining homeownership, and housing insecurity, while highlighting the political and social challenges shaping the future of housing and tenant rights.

Subject: Economics
Title: Beyond Generation Rent: Political Economy, Inequality, And The Private Rental Sector
Author: Byrne
Description: Beyond Generation Rent provides a critical examination of the inequalities shaping modern housing systems and the global growth of private renting. Through international case studies and political-economic analysis, Michael Byrne explores landlordism, housing financialization, evictions, declining homeownership, and tenant activism, highlighting the urgent challenges of housing justice and affordability in contemporary societies.

Subject: General
Title: The German-Russian Century - History Of A Tangled Relationship
Author: Creuzberger
Description: The German-Russian Century explores the complex and deeply influential relationship between Germany and Russia throughout the twentieth century and beyond. Historian Stefan Creuzberger examines how revolution, war, fascism, communism, the Cold War, and modern geopolitical tensions shaped both nations and the wider world, offering a detailed historical analysis of one of the most consequential international relationships in modern history.

Subject: General
Title: Women'S Liberation: Gender Inequality From Suffrage To Austerity
Author: Pat Thane
Description: Women’s Liberation examines the long struggle for gender equality in Britain and the social, political, and economic forces that have shaped it over the past century. Through issues such as employment, education, political representation, health, and violence against women, Pat Thane explores how gender inequality intersects with class, race, disability, and income, offering a thoughtful analysis of both the progress achieved and the challenges that remain.

Subject: General
Title: The Great Realignment: Why The New Right Is Here To Stay
Author: Davies
Description: The Politics of Realignment explores the global political shifts reshaping modern democracies, from the rise of the hard right to the weakening of traditional political alliances. Steve Davies argues that politics is moving away from class- and economy-based divisions toward conflicts centered on national identity, sovereignty, and cosmopolitanism, offering a wide-ranging analysis of the forces transforming political systems across countries such as the USA, Germany, Argentina, and India.

Subject: General
Title: Same Player Shoots Again - A Biography Of The Pinball Machine
Author: Bernard
Description: The Age of Pinball reflects on the rise and disappearance of pinball machines as symbols of a lost era of public entertainment and shared leisure culture. Blending personal memories with cultural analysis, Andreas Bernard explores how changing technology, digital entertainment, and the blurring of work and leisure have transformed social spaces, gaming, and the meaning of free time in modern life.

Subject: General
Title: The Unknowable Body
Author: Moore
Description: The Unknowable Body is a deeply personal and reflective exploration of illness, identity, and the limits of medical understanding. Drawing from her experiences with cancer treatment and family caregiving, Lisa Jean Moore examines how bodies resist clear definition through themes of medical uncertainty, gender, emotion, technology, and embodiment, offering a thoughtful perspective on what it means to live in bodies that are constantly changing and never fully knowable.

Subject: General
Title: Speech Begins After Death: In Conversation With Claude Bonnefoy
Author: Foucault
Description: Speech Begins after Death presents a rare and personal conversation between philosopher Michel Foucault and critic Claude Bonnefoy about Foucault’s relationship with writing, memory, and intellectual life. Through this reflective and autobiographical dialogue, Foucault discusses his literary influences, personal history, and the deeper meaning of writing, offering unique insight into the mind and creative process of one of the twentieth century’s most influential thinkers.

Subject: General
Title: Prompt Thinking: A Critique Of Generative Reason
Author: Xun
Description: Prompt Thinking examines how generative AI is reshaping human thought and intellectual practice. Blending philosophy, technology, and critical reflection, the book explores prompting as a new form of dialogue with artificial intelligence, offering a thoughtful framework for engaging with AI in ways that expand creativity, awareness, and critical thinking while preserving human autonomy.

Subject: Physics
Title: Towards A Unification Of The Laws Of Physics In Classical Fields Theory
Author: Caltagirone
Description: Towards a Unification of the Laws of Physics in Classical Fields Theory presents a theoretical framework aimed at unifying the fundamental laws of physics through discrete mechanics. Revisiting concepts from classical mechanics, relativity, and electromagnetism, the book proposes a single law of motion based on local reference frames, discrete time, and proper acceleration, offering a new perspective on understanding physical phenomena across multiple fields of physics.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Generalized Fractional Brownian Motion
Author: Zili
Description: Generalized Fractional Brownian Motion introduces the Zili generalized fractional Brownian motion (ZgfBm), an advanced stochastic process designed to model complex random phenomena with greater flexibility than traditional fractional models. Covering foundational theory, parameter estimation, stochastic calculus, and applications to stochastic equations, the book provides a rigorous and comprehensive framework for researchers and students working in probability, mathematics, and stochastic analysis.

Subject: General
Title: Scaling Laws And Urban Systems
Author: Vacchiani-Marcu
Description: Scaling Laws and Urban Systems explores how the size of cities shapes economic activity, social inequality, innovation, resource use, and environmental impact. Examining scaling laws in urban development, the book explains how cities grow and adapt through sublinear and superlinear relationships, offering valuable insights into sustainability, ecological transition, and urban policy in an increasingly urbanized world.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Nanosatellites, Cubesats Of The Newspace Era For Space Observation 2 - Designing And Operating Cubesats
Author: Dahoo
Description: Nanosatellites, CubeSats of the NewSpace Era for Space Observation 2 provides a detailed exploration of the complete CubeSat lifecycle, from design and simulation to testing and orbital operations. Combining aerospace engineering, reliability optimisation, and space environment modelling, the book also examines the role of optics and light-based technologies in space observation, including modern developments in quantum optics and photonic science.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Autonomous Vehicles - Aerospace And Automotive Applications
Author: Krysinski
Description: Autonomous Vehicles provides a comprehensive exploration of the technologies powering self-driving systems in the automotive and aerospace industries. The book examines the integration of artificial intelligence, mechatronics, sensors, control systems, and data processing techniques used for autonomous driving and piloting, while also addressing critical industrial, legal, societal, and safety challenges through real-world applications and industry-focused case studies.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Digital Strategies For Supply Chains - From Design To Deployment
Author: Oruezabala
Description: Digital Strategies in Supply Chains examines how digital transformation is reshaping modern supply chain management through improved logistics optimization, operational traceability, collaboration, and sustainability. Using real-world industrial and service-sector case studies, the book explores practical digital strategies that help organizations enhance competitiveness, reduce costs and lead times, and meet evolving environmental and operational demands.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Next-Gen Supply Chains - Ai, Automation, And Sustainability In A Disrupted World
Author: Bhambri
Description: Next-Gen Supply Chains offers a forward-looking guide to transforming global supply networks through the integration of generative AI, blockchain, IoT, and autonomous systems. Combining practical implementation strategies with real-world case studies from companies like Amazon and Lenovo, the book explores how organizations can build resilient, efficient, sustainable, and ethically governed supply chains while developing the human capabilities needed to thrive in an increasingly technology-driven environment.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Hidden Faces Of Innovation
Author: Gilbert
Description: The Hidden Faces of Innovation explores the deeper dynamics, tensions, and paradoxes that shape innovation in modern organizations. Blending managerial analysis with reflections on technological, structural, and cultural change, the book examines topics such as creativity, remote work, digital transformation, organizational agility, and innovation management, offering a thoughtful perspective on how innovation truly develops in contemporary workplaces.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Context-Based Modeling Of Activity In Real-World Projects
Author: Brézillon
Description: Context-based Modelling of Activity in Real-World Projects presents a comprehensive framework for understanding and modelling context in human and group activities across real-world applications. Based on 25 years of research, the book introduces the Contextual-Graphs (CxG) formalism and explores how context, reasoning, and knowledge interact to support intelligent decision-making, laying the groundwork for future context-aware AI assistant systems.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Potential-Ize: How Leaders Unlock Human Potential In The Age Of Ai
Author: Bryant
Description: In Potential-ize, renowned self-leadership expert Andrew Bryant presents a powerful framework for unlocking human potential in the age of AI. Through his practical IGNITE model, the book explores how individuals and organizations can develop self-leadership, resilience, creativity, and meaningful human-AI collaboration. Filled with inspiring stories, leadership insights, and actionable strategies, Potential-ize helps readers transform challenges into growth opportunities while building cultures that encourage learning, belonging, and continuous development. Ideal for leaders, professionals, entrepreneurs, and teams seeking to thrive in a rapidly changing world.

Subject: General
Title: Love Notes: A Small Book With A Big Heart
Author: Cope
Description: Love Notes: A Small Book with a Big Heart is an uplifting and heartfelt collection of stories, reflections, quotes, and illustrations that celebrates the power of love, kindness, and human connection in a fast-paced modern world. Written by Andy Cope, Hannah Knowles, and illustrator Amy Bradley, the book encourages readers to rediscover joy, compassion, and authentic happiness through simple yet meaningful insights. Blending wisdom, humor, and creativity, it offers gentle reminders to focus less on ourselves and more on caring for others, while inspiring readers to share the book and its message of love with someone who needs it most.

Subject: General
Title: The Iliad - The Epic Classic
Author: Homer
Description: The Iliad is one of the most influential works of classical literature, set during the final phase of the Trojan War and centered on the legendary warrior Achilles. Through intense battles, powerful emotions, and divine intervention, the epic explores timeless themes of heroism, pride, friendship, revenge, fate, and mortality. Featuring iconic characters such as Hector, Agamemnon, and Patroclus, this modern translation by Ian Johnston brings Homer’s masterpiece to contemporary readers while preserving its dramatic depth and poetic grandeur.

Subject: General
Title: The Odyssey - The Epic Classic
Author: Homer
Description: The Odyssey is a timeless epic adventure that follows Odysseus, the king of Ithaca, on his perilous ten-year journey home after the Trojan War. Battling mythical creatures, divine wrath, and powerful temptations, Odysseus must rely on his intelligence, courage, and resilience to reunite with his family. Alongside unforgettable episodes involving the Cyclops, Sirens, Circe, and the Underworld, the poem explores enduring themes of loyalty, identity, perseverance, hospitality, and the meaning of home. Featuring a modern translation and introduction by Ian Johnston, this classic masterpiece remains essential reading for lovers of mythology, epic storytelling, and world literature.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Multiscale Structural Mechanics: Top-Down Modeling Of Composite Structures Using Mechanics Of Structure Genome
Author: Yu
Description: Multiscale Structural Mechanics: Top-Down Modelling of Composite Structures Using Mechanics of Structure Genome presents a comprehensive and unified framework for modelling composite materials and structures using the innovative Mechanics of Structure Genome (MSG) approach. Written by Wenbin Yu, the book bridges micromechanics and structural mechanics to create accurate yet computationally efficient multiscale models for beams, plates, shells, and 3D solids. Covering foundational concepts such as vectors, tensors, continuum mechanics, and variational methods, it also explores stress and failure analysis of composite laminates and the behavior of anisotropic and heterogeneous materials. Designed for graduate students, researchers, and practicing engineers in aerospace, mechanical, and materials engineering, the book serves as an essential reference for advanced composite structure modeling and analysis.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Nonlinear, Parametric And Rotordynamic Vibrations
Author: Palazzolo
Description: Introduction to Engineering Nonlinear and Parametric Vibrations with MATLAB and MAPLE is a comprehensive textbook that equips readers with the theoretical foundations, analytical methods, and computational tools required to study and solve complex nonlinear and parametric vibration problems in engineering. The book introduces essential mathematical concepts, numerical techniques, and simulation methods while integrating extensive practical examples using MATLAB and Maple. It covers topics such as nonlinear autonomous systems, limit cycles, stability analysis, parametric and autoparametric vibrations, Floquet and Mathieu theory, numerical continuation methods, and chaotic dynamics including Lyapunov exponents and homoclinic chaos. Supported by companion MATLAB and MAPLE codes, the text is designed for upper-level undergraduate students, graduate researchers, and practicing vibration engineers seeking a modern, application-oriented guide to nonlinear vibration analysis.

Subject: Health
Title: Facial Aesthetics: Concepts And Clinical Diagnosis 2E
Author: Naini
Description: Facial Aesthetics: Concepts and Clinical Diagnosis is a comprehensive guide to the science and art of facial aesthetics, combining historical concepts of beauty with modern clinical diagnosis and treatment planning. Covering facial proportions, expressions, smile aesthetics, and craniofacial analysis, the book provides a step-by-step, evidence-based approach for evaluating facial deformities using both 2D and 3D diagnostic methods. Designed for aesthetic dentists, orthodontists, oral and maxillofacial surgeons, and plastic surgeons, it serves as a practical reference for understanding and improving facial harmony and aesthetics.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Mobile Edge Computing And Communications: Driving Forces, Technology Foundation, And Application Areas
Author: Ding
Description: Mobile Edge Computing and Communications provides a practical and comprehensive introduction to mobile edge computing (MEC), covering its architecture, deployment, integration methods, and operational lifecycle in 5G and future mobile networks. Written by industry experts, the book explores emerging edge applications, communication technologies, and the challenges and opportunities shaping the future of distributed computing. Designed for mobile network operators, ICT developers, researchers, and students, it serves as an essential guide to understanding how MEC is transforming modern computing and communications.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Distillation Diagnostics - An Engineer'S Guidebook
Author: Kister
Description: Distillation Diagnostics is a practical guide to diagnosing and troubleshooting distillation and absorption tower problems, written by renowned industry expert “The Tower Doctor.” Drawing on over 40 years of real-world experience, the book explains how to use and interpret diagnostic tools such as gamma scans, thermal scans, pressure drop analysis, and column testing to identify root causes of tower malfunctions. Packed with case studies, troubleshooting strategies, and cost-effective solutions, it serves as an essential resource for plant operators, chemical engineers, and process professionals seeking to improve efficiency, reduce failures, and optimize plant performance.

Subject: Science
Title: Hoffbrand'S Postgraduate Haematology
Author: Mead
Description: Hoffbrand’s Postgraduate Haematology is a comprehensive and authoritative reference on blood and bone marrow disorders, widely trusted by generations of haematology trainees and clinicians since 1972. The fully updated eighth edition covers the pathogenesis, diagnosis, laboratory features, and treatment of a broad range of blood diseases, while incorporating recent advances in areas such as genome editing, next-generation sequencing, and modern diagnostic tools. Richly illustrated and extensively referenced, it serves as an essential resource for postgraduate students, residents, and healthcare professionals seeking both foundational knowledge and the latest developments in haematology.

Subject: Science
Title: Bladder Pathology 2E
Author: Cheng
Description: Bladder Pathology is a comprehensive, evidence-based guide to the diagnosis and clinical management of bladder diseases, designed for pathologists, urologists, oncologists, and related medical professionals. The fully updated second edition covers bladder anatomy, benign and malignant conditions, treatment-related changes, and modern diagnostic approaches, with a strong focus on applying scientific evidence in real clinical practice. Featuring more than a thousand detailed color illustrations, the book serves as an essential reference for accurate diagnosis, treatment planning, and understanding the latest developments in bladder pathology.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Molecular Basis Of Oxidative Stress: Chemistry, Toxicology, Disease Pathogenesis, Diagnosis, And Therapeutics, 2Nd Edition
Author: Villamena
Description: Molecular Basis of Oxidative Stress is a detailed and up-to-date reference exploring the molecular mechanisms of oxidative stress and its role in diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disorders, neurodegenerative conditions, COPD, and kidney and eye diseases. The second edition explains the chemistry of free radicals and reactive oxygen species (ROS), oxidative damage to DNA, proteins, and lipids, antioxidant defense systems, biomarkers, and emerging therapeutic strategies. Combining foundational redox biology with the latest research, the book is an essential resource for toxicologists, biochemists, pharmacologists, and medical researchers studying disease pathogenesis and treatment.

Subject: Science
Title: Sustainable 3D Printing For Innovative Biopolymer Production And Applications
Author: Sharma
Description: 3D-Printed Biopolymers explores the latest advancements in sustainable biopolymer materials and their applications through 3D printing technologies. Covering areas such as tissue engineering, water purification, energy storage, packaging, and biomedical innovation, the book highlights how green biopolymers, nanotechnology, and material functionalization support sustainability and the circular economy. With contributions from leading researchers, it provides a multidisciplinary overview of processing techniques, material synthesis, current challenges, and future opportunities in biopolymer science and manufacturing.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Organic Reaction Mechanisms 2021
Author: Moloney
Description: Organic Reaction Mechanisms 2021 is the 57th volume in the renowned series that reviews and summarizes significant research published in 2021 on organic reaction mechanisms. Covering a wide range of topics including oxidation and reduction, substitution reactions, cycloadditions, radicals, carbocations, carbanions, molecular rearrangements, and transition metal coupling, the book provides chemists and researchers with a reliable and detailed overview of contemporary developments in mechanistic organic chemistry.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Five Dysfunctions of a Team: A Leadership Fable, 20th Anniversary Edition
Author: Patrick M. Lencioni
Description: The Five Dysfunctions of a Team is a bestselling leadership classic that combines storytelling with practical management insights to explore why teams struggle to work effectively. Through the story of CEO Kathryn Petersen and her dysfunctional executive team, Patrick Lencioni introduces five common obstacles that undermine teamwork, including lack of trust, fear of conflict, and avoidance of accountability. Blending an engaging business fable with actionable leadership strategies, the book offers managers and executives a powerful framework for building cohesive, high-performing teams.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Thermal Explosion: Theory And Application
Author: Novozhilov
Description: Thermal Explosion provides a comprehensive overview of the theory, mechanisms, hazards, and prevention strategies related to thermal explosions. Covering mathematical models, combustion dynamics, fire flashover, porous systems, and modern applications such as lithium-ion batteries and biofuels, the book connects foundational theory with practical engineering solutions. It serves as an essential reference for researchers, engineers, and graduate students working in combustion, safety engineering, and thermal sciences.

Subject: General
Title: Novel Drug Delivery Systems - Fundamentals And Applications
Author: Hamidi
Description: Novel Drug Delivery Systems offers a comprehensive and accessible overview of modern drug delivery technologies designed to improve therapeutic effectiveness, affordability, and patient outcomes. Covering foundational principles, administration routes, market trends, and future innovations, the book bridges academic research with pharmaceutical industry applications. Written in a clear and practical style, it serves as a valuable resource for researchers, students, healthcare professionals, pharmaceutical executives, and policymakers interested in advanced drug delivery systems.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Introduction To Extended Reality (Xr) Technologies
Author: Vohra
Description: A practical and accessible guide to augmented, virtual, and mixed reality technologies, Introduction to Extended Reality (XR) Technologies explores how XR is transforming industries such as healthcare, education, aviation, tourism, and entertainment. Covering the fundamentals of AR, VR, and MR along with real-world applications, the book combines theoretical concepts with practical insights, making it an essential resource for engineers, IT professionals, healthcare practitioners, and students interested in the future of immersive technologies.

Subject: General
Title: Nanotechnology For Sustainable Food Packaging
Author: Anandharamakris
Description: A comprehensive guide to the latest innovations in sustainable food packaging, Nanotechnology for Sustainable Food Packaging explores how nanotechnology is transforming biodegradable and eco-friendly packaging solutions. The book covers advanced materials, intelligent and active packaging systems, bio-based nanocomposites, safety regulations, and sustainability practices while highlighting real-world applications and future commercialization opportunities. Ideal for researchers, students, and food industry professionals, it provides practical insights into improving food shelf life, packaging performance, and environmental sustainability through cutting-edge nanotechnology.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Medical Toxicology Of Occupational And Environmental Exposures: Metals And Metalloids, Volume 1
Author: Barceloux
Description: Medical Toxicology of Occupational and Environmental Exposures is a comprehensive and evidence-based reference covering the clinical, diagnostic, and monitoring aspects of toxic exposures related to metals, radiation, and chemical carcinogens. Written in a clear and standardized format, the book helps clinicians, researchers, and regulatory professionals quickly access critical information on exposure sources, dose-response effects, health surveillance, diagnosis, and treatment. Volume 1 focuses specifically on metals and metalloids, offering detailed clinical guidance and toxicological insights essential for occupational and environmental health practice.

Subject: General
Title: Understanding The Religions Of The World: An Introduction, 2Nd Edition
Author: Deming
Description: A visually rich and accessible introduction to the world’s major religions, Understanding the Religions of the World explores how faith traditions shape human beliefs, cultures, and societies. Covering Judaism, Hinduism, Buddhism, Christianity, Islam, Chinese religions, indigenous traditions, and more, the book combines contemporary scholarship, field-based insights, images, maps, and discussion tools to help readers understand how religions influence human aspirations, identity, and everyday life. Ideal for students and anyone interested in comparative religion, cultural studies, and global belief systems.

Subject: General
Title: The Handbook Of Social And Political Conflict
Author: Samoilenko
Description: A comprehensive and interdisciplinary guide to understanding modern conflict, The Handbook of Social and Political Conflict examines the causes, dynamics, and consequences of social and political tensions through perspectives from sociology, political science, psychology, communication, and conflict studies. Covering topics such as political polarization, cancel culture, information warfare, media influence, AI, and peacebuilding, the book combines cutting-edge research with practical conflict resolution strategies. Ideal for students, researchers, policymakers, and professionals, it offers valuable insights into navigating and addressing the complex conflicts shaping today’s world.

Subject: Health
Title: Recognizing And Correcting Developing Malocclusions - A Problem-Oriented Approach To Orthodontics, Second Edition
Author: Araújo
Description: An evidence-based guide to early orthodontic intervention, Recognizing and Correcting Developing Malocclusions, Second Edition helps clinicians identify, assess, and manage developing dental and skeletal irregularities in children. Covering Class I, II, and III malocclusions, eruptive deviations, missing teeth, dental trauma, and sleep apnea, the book combines comprehensive research with practical treatment protocols, detailed case analyses, and modern diagnostic approaches. Richly illustrated with clinical photographs, tables, and 3D visuals, it serves as an essential resource for orthodontists, pediatric dentists, residents, and clinicians involved in mixed-dentition treatment planning.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Condition-Based Maintenance And Residual Life Prediction
Author: Singh
Description: Condition-Based Maintenance and Residual Life Prediction provides a practical overview of predictive maintenance techniques using vibration data analysis to monitor machine health, detect faults, and estimate equipment life. Covering issues such as shaft cracks, misalignment, and vibration-based diagnostics, the book presents modelling approaches and maintenance strategies that help improve reliability, reduce downtime, and support efficient condition-based maintenance in industrial systems.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Electric Machinery And Drives: An Electromagnetics Perspective
Author: Demerdash
Description: Electric Machinery and Drives: An Electromagnetics Perspective offers a comprehensive introduction to electric machinery, variable speed drives, and motor control systems from an electromagnetics perspective. Covering topics such as induction machines, synchronous machines, power electronics, magnetic circuits, electromechanical energy conversion, and advanced control techniques, the book combines theoretical foundations with practical design and control applications. Enhanced with solution manuals and video animations, it is an essential resource for electrical engineering students, researchers, and professionals working in automation, automotive, aerospace, and power systems engineering.

Subject: General
Title: Essentials Of Assessing Infants, Toddlers, And Preschoolers
Author: Dale
Description: Essentials of Assessing Infants, Toddlers, and Pre-Schoolers is a practical, clinician-focused guide to assessing children from birth to five years of age. Covering developmental, psychological, and behavioral evaluations, the book provides up-to-date assessment techniques, intervention planning guidance, and strategies for working with children with complex medical or developmental conditions. Written in an accessible format with key points, illustrations, and practical tools, it is an essential resource for psychologists, pediatricians, speech-language pathologists, and early childhood assessment professionals.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: The Science Of Cooking: Understanding The Biology And Chemistry Behind Food And Cooking, 2Nd Edition
Author: Provost
Description: The Science of Cooking: Understanding the Biology and Chemistry Behind Food and Cooking uses food and cooking to make chemistry, biology, and biochemistry easy to understand through practical, real-world examples. Featuring hands-on experiments, inquiry-based cooking labs, recipes with scientific explanations, and interactive learning activities, the book helps readers explore the molecular science behind everyday cooking processes. Ideal for students, aspiring chefs, nutrition professionals, and science learners, it combines foundational scientific concepts with engaging culinary applications.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Toxicological Assessment Of Combined Chemicals In The Environment
Author: Pei
Description: Toxicological Assessment of Combined Chemicals in the Environment explores the behaviour, interaction, and toxic effects of chemical mixtures in the environment, focusing on pollutants such as heavy metals, pesticides, persistent organic pollutants, and pharmaceutical contaminants. The book covers exposure pathways, molecular toxicity mechanisms, toxicological research models, and computational hazard assessment methods, providing a multidisciplinary approach to environmental risk evaluation. It is an essential reference for researchers, toxicologists, students, and policymakers involved in environmental health, pollution control, and chemical safety assessment.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Green Technologies For Industrial Contaminants
Author: Kapoor
Description: Green Technologies for Industrial Contaminants explores sustainable and eco-friendly approaches for treating industrial pollutants and wastewater. Focusing on the combined use of biochar and microorganisms such as bacteria, algae, and fungi, the book examines innovative methods for removing dyes, heavy metals, and hazardous chemicals from industrial effluents. Addressing global challenges like water scarcity, pollution, and waste management, it provides valuable insights into sustainable contaminant remediation, bioenergy production, and green industrial practices for researchers, environmental scientists, and industry professionals.

Subject: General
Title: Essential Mass Communication: Convergence, Culture, And Media Literacy
Author: Dimarco
Description: Essential Mass Communication: Convergence, Culture, and Media Literacy provides a comprehensive introduction to mass communication, media literacy, and communication technologies while exploring how media shapes culture and society. Covering topics such as journalism, advertising, storytelling, filmmaking, strategic communication, ethics, and media convergence, the book combines theoretical concepts with practical career-focused insights and real-world examples. Designed for students and aspiring media professionals, it helps readers develop critical analysis skills and understand the evolving landscape of modern communication and digital media.

Subject: General
Title: The Handbook Of Mental Health Communication
Author: C. Yzer
Description: A comprehensive and transdisciplinary resource, The Handbook of Mental Health Communication explores how mass communication influences mental health awareness, promotion, and outcomes. Bringing together insights from public health, psychology, psychopathology, and communication studies, the book examines how people process mental health information and how media messages shape perceptions, behaviors, and stigma surrounding mental illness. With contributions from leading scholars, it provides evidence-based perspectives on mental health communication strategies, making it an essential reference for students, researchers, educators, and health communication professionals.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Problem Solving In Engineering: Analytical Mathematics And Numerical Analysis
Author: Glasgow
Description: Problem Solving in Engineering is a practical guide to analytical and numerical methods used in engineering and applied sciences. Covering differential equations, computational modelling, signal analysis, and problem formulation, the book combines mathematical principles with real-world engineering applications across multiple disciplines. With updated examples and interdisciplinary case studies, it is a valuable resource for students, researchers, and engineering professionals looking to enhance their technical problem-solving abilities.

Subject: General
Title: The Handbook Of International Crisis And Risk Communication Research, 2Nd Edition
Author: Schwarz
Description: A comprehensive and globally focused resource, The Handbook of International Crisis and Risk Communication Research explores how communication strategies shape the management of modern crises such as pandemics, climate disasters, political conflicts, and misinformation. Bringing together interdisciplinary perspectives from communication, public relations, and social sciences, the book examines crisis awareness, public response, resilience, and recovery across different cultural and international contexts. Updated with insights on COVID-19, climate change, the Ukraine war, and digital media challenges, it serves as an essential guide for researchers, students, crisis managers, and communication professionals navigating today’s complex global risks.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Quantum Dot Display Science And Technology
Author: Alivisatos
Description: Quantum Dot Display Science and Technology provides a comprehensive overview of quantum dot (QD)-based display technologies, covering the science, materials, fabrication methods, and applications behind next-generation displays. The book explores quantum dot photoluminescent and electroluminescent technologies used in LCDs, OLEDs, and μLEDs, while also examining QD synthesis, display panel processing, lighting, and photodetector applications. Supported with illustrations, tables, and process flow charts, it serves as a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, and professionals working in display technology, consumer electronics, and advanced materials science.

Subject: Science
Title: Applications Of Diamond-Like Carbon Coatings
Author: Zia
Description: Applications of Diamond-like Carbon Coatings provides a comprehensive overview of the latest developments and practical applications of diamond-like carbon (DLC) coatings in fields such as transportation, medicine, electronics, energy storage, and advanced manufacturing. Covering topics like tribology, green lubrication, biomedical implants, MEMS, optical devices, and sustainable engineering, the book also explores emerging trends, including AI-driven material informatics and carbon circularity. Blending cutting-edge research with real-world industrial insights, it serves as a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, scientists, and postgraduate students working in materials science and surface engineering.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Smart Cyber-Physical Power Systems, Volume 1 - Fundamental Concepts, Challenges, And Solutions
Author: Parizad
Description: A comprehensive guide to the evolving world of smart cyber-physical power systems, this book explores how advanced technologies such as AI, IoT, blockchain, edge computing, big data, and quantum computing are transforming modern energy infrastructure. Covering smart grids, microgrids, energy management, cybersecurity, resiliency, and distributed control strategies, it provides foundational concepts, major challenges, and innovative solutions for building intelligent, secure, and sustainable power systems. Ideal for researchers, engineers, and industry professionals, the book offers valuable insights into the future of adaptive and interconnected energy networks.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Cyber-Physical Systems For Innovating And Transforming Society 5.0
Author: Choudhury
Description: A forward-looking exploration of Society 5.0, this book examines how cyber-physical systems, artificial intelligence, and data-driven technologies are transforming modern society through the integration of cyberspace and the physical world. Covering areas such as image processing, natural language processing, computer vision, sentiment analysis, smart healthcare, intelligent infrastructure, legal systems, and innovative agriculture, it highlights practical applications and emerging technologies that promote inclusive growth and solve socio-economic challenges. Designed for researchers, engineers, and students, the book provides valuable insights into building a smarter, interconnected, and human-centered future.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Smart Factories For Industry 5.0 Transformation
Author: Nidhya
Description: A comprehensive guide to Industry 5.0 and smart factory technologies, this book explores how artificial intelligence, robotics, IoT, big data, and automation are transforming modern manufacturing and industrial operations. Covering design principles, operational strategies, sustainability, and human-machine collaboration, it highlights the role of smart factories in improving efficiency, quality, and innovation across industries. Combining technical insights with real-world applications and discussions on global supply chains and socioeconomic impact, the book is a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, technologists, and professionals working in advanced industrial systems.

Subject: General
Title: Sustainable Nanomaterials For Treatment And Diagnosis Of Infectious Diseases
Author: Chokkareddy
Description: A comprehensive exploration of sustainable nanotechnology in infectious disease management, this book examines how advanced nanomaterials can improve the diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of infectious diseases. Covering topics such as nano-drug delivery systems, nanotheranostics, vaccines, biosensors, green synthesis techniques, and biocompatibility, it highlights innovative approaches for tackling multidrug-resistant pathogens and enhancing treatment precision. Combining scientific foundations with practical healthcare applications, the book serves as a valuable resource for researchers, healthcare professionals, and students working in nanotechnology, medicine, and sustainable biomedical engineering.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Mobile Communication Systems For Private Networks
Author: Balasubramanian
Description: A practical guide to enterprise wireless communications, Mobile Communication Systems for Private Networks explores the design, deployment, and management of private 4G and 5G networks for businesses and industrial environments. Covering topics such as network architecture, IP connectivity, standards, cellular operations, and reliable indoor and outdoor connectivity, the book highlights the growing role of private mobile networks in modern enterprises. Designed for engineers, network operators, researchers, and technology professionals, it provides valuable insights into current developments and future challenges in private wireless communication systems.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Pharmacognosy And Phytochemistry: Principles, Techniques, And Clinical Applications
Author: Odoh
Description: A comprehensive introduction to plant-based medicines and natural product research, Pharmacognosy and Phytochemistry explores the principles, applications, and modern advancements in pharmacognosy and phytochemistry. Covering topics such as crude drug classification, extraction techniques, ethnopharmacology, herbal drug analysis, plant secondary metabolites, biotechnology, and molecular pharmacognosy, the book combines traditional medicinal knowledge with contemporary scientific approaches. Designed for students, researchers, and healthcare professionals, it serves as a valuable resource for understanding the therapeutic potential and scientific development of natural and plant-derived compounds.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Model-Based Product Line Engineering (Mbple) - The Feature-Based Path To Product Lines Success
Author: Forlingieri
Description: A practical and foundational guide to integrating model-based systems engineering (MBSE) with product line engineering (PLE), Model-Based Product Line Engineering (MBPLE) explores how model-centric approaches can improve system reuse, engineering efficiency, and digital transformation. Covering both technical and managerial perspectives, the book discusses MBPLE concepts, SysML v2 implementation, engineering processes, change management, and real-world deployment challenges. Featuring diagrams, code snippets, and industry-focused guidance, it serves as a valuable resource for system engineers, managers, and organizations adopting modern model-based engineering practices.

Subject: Science
Title: Decontamination And Device Processing In Healthcare, 2Nd Edition
Author: Mcdonnell
Description: A comprehensive guide to infection prevention and safe medical device reprocessing, Decontamination and Device Processing in Healthcare explores the principles, standards, and best practices involved in cleaning, disinfection, and sterilization within healthcare environments. Updated with the latest international regulations and guidelines, the book covers topics such as infection control, surgical suite management, microbiology, endoscopy, quality management, and patient safety. Designed for healthcare professionals, educators, and administrators, it serves as an essential resource for ensuring effective device processing and reducing healthcare-associated infections.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Fundamentals Of O-Ran
Author: Tripathi
Description: A comprehensive guide to Open RAN technology, Fundamentals of O-RAN explores the evolution, architecture, security, ecosystem, and future of open and virtualized radio access networks for 5G and beyond. Covering topics such as xApps, rApps, software-defined networking, virtualization, and O-RAN system integration, the book combines theoretical concepts with practical hands-on learning resources. Designed for students, researchers, and wireless network professionals, it provides valuable insights into building, testing, and managing next-generation open cellular network infrastructures.

Subject: General
Title: 4D Printing Technology - Principles, Materials And Application
Author: Samal
Description: A detailed guide to next-generation additive manufacturing, 4D Printing Technology: Principles, Materials and Applications explores how smart materials and advanced printing processes create adaptive, shape-changing structures. Covering topics such as smart polymers, reinforced materials, micro and nano-scale printing, stimuli-responsive systems, and applications in healthcare, aerospace, robotics, and photonics, the book provides both foundational knowledge and insights into recent innovations. Designed for researchers, engineers, materials scientists, and manufacturing professionals, it offers a comprehensive overview of the principles, technologies, and future potential of 4D printing.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Mathematics For Engineers
Author: Shrivastava
Description: A practical and accessible resource, Mathematics for Engineers provides a comprehensive foundation in essential mathematical concepts and their real-world engineering applications. Covering core topics with examples, practice problems, and application-based exercises, the book helps students and professionals strengthen problem-solving, analytical reasoning, and mathematical modelling skills. Designed for engineering and applied science learners, it serves both as a learning guide and a refresher for professionals seeking to enhance their technical mathematics proficiency.

Subject: General
Title: Communication Theory At The Crossroads
Author: Deetz
Description: Communication Theory at the Crossroads presents a fresh and integrated approach to understanding communication in an increasingly complex and divided world. Grounded in systemic constructionist and relational perspectives, the book explores how communication theory can help individuals and groups navigate social change, interdependence, technology-driven interactions, and contemporary conflicts. Combining theoretical insights with practical application, it encourages students to think critically, engage as active social scientists, and use communication concepts to foster productive, collaborative, and creative human interaction.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Undisrupted: Leadership Essentials On Business Transformation, Profitability And Future Readiness
Author: Khan
Description: Undisrupted: Leadership Essentials on Creating a Future-Ready Organization offers a practical roadmap for leaders seeking to build adaptable, resilient, and innovation-driven organizations in a rapidly changing world. Drawing on insights from futurist Ian Khan, the book introduces the Future Readiness Score™ and explores seven key pillars of organizational success, including engagement, learning, collaboration, culture, innovation, and execution. Combining strategic guidance with real-world insights, it helps business leaders, entrepreneurs, and managers prepare for uncertainty, embrace change, and create sustainable growth in the future economy.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Transforming The Construction Industry With Blockchain - Enhancing Efficiency, Transparency, And Collaboration
Author: Harty
Description: Transforming the Construction Industry with Blockchain explores how blockchain, along with emerging technologies like AI and machine learning, is reshaping the construction sector through improved transparency, security, and collaboration. The book explains blockchain fundamentals and demonstrates their practical applications in construction projects, including better data management, streamlined workflows, reduced subcontracting inefficiencies, and sustainable project practices. Supported by real-world case studies, it serves as a valuable guide for construction professionals, project stakeholders, and industry leaders looking to adopt smarter and more efficient digital solutions.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Accident Prevention And Investigation - A Systematic Guide For Professionals, Educators, Researchers, And Students
Author: Fukuoka
Description: Accident Prevention and Investigation provides a practical and systematic approach to understanding, preventing, and analyzing accidents in academic and research environments. Drawing on real-world case studies, the book explores accident theory, risk management, safety systems, and investigative techniques for accidents and near-misses. It also addresses the unique safety challenges faced by universities, laboratories, and fieldwork operations, offering checklists and actionable strategies to improve campus safety and reduce risks. Ideal for faculty, researchers, and advanced students, the book serves as a valuable guide to building safer research and educational environments.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Network Coding For Engineers
Author: Médard
Description: Network Coding for Engineers introduces the principles and practical applications of network coding, a technology that improves network throughput and efficiency by encoding and decoding data packets during transmission. Focusing on engineering applications such as random linear network coding (RLNC), the book explains how network coding enhances wireless, mesh, and multi-path networks while remaining compatible with legacy systems. Structured as a student-friendly guide, it covers implementation techniques, design principles, and future applications in areas like 6G and post-quantum cryptography, making it a valuable resource for engineering and computer science students studying advanced networking and communication systems.

Subject: Science
Title: People-Centred Pharmacy: Ethical Challenges In Everyday Practice
Author: Seedhouse
Description: People-Centred Pharmacy: Ethical Challenges in Everyday Practice explores the ethical dilemmas pharmacists encounter in daily professional practice and provides practical guidance for responsible decision-making in pharmaceutical care. Focusing on real-world scenarios, the book presents a people-centered and interdisciplinary approach to ethical reasoning that considers the interests of patients, pharmacists, and healthcare systems alike. With examples drawn from pharmacy practice and collaboration with pharmacy educators, it serves as a valuable resource for pharmacists, pharmacy staff, and students seeking to strengthen their ethical judgement and patient-care decision-making skills.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Storytelling with Data: A Data Visualization Guide for Business Professionals
Author: Cole Nussbaumer Knaflic
Description: A practical and engaging guide to effective data visualization, Storytelling with Data teaches readers how to transform complex data into clear, compelling, and impactful visual stories. Combining design principles with real-world examples, the book explains how to choose the right charts, eliminate unnecessary clutter, highlight key insights, and communicate data with clarity and purpose. Focused on audience understanding and storytelling techniques, it helps professionals, analysts, and students create presentations and visualizations that are both informative and memorable.

Subject: General
Title: Fact Forward: The Perils Of Bad Information And The Promise Of A Data-Savvy Society
Author: Gaylin
Description: Fact Forward: The Perils of Bad Information and the Promise of a Data-Savvy Society examines how the explosion of data has created both new opportunities and growing challenges related to misinformation, trust, and data literacy. The book explores the roles of data producers, communicators, and consumers in shaping a more informed society, emphasizing the need for transparency, responsible communication, and stronger data education. Combining insights from social science and data science, it provides a thoughtful guide for leaders, professionals, and readers seeking to better understand how data influences decision-making, public trust, and modern society.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Digital Mavericks
Author: Soon
Description: Digital Mavericks: A Guide to Web3, NFTs, and Becoming the Main Character in the Next Internet Revolution explores how blockchain technology, NFTs, and Web3 are reshaping careers, creativity, and digital communities. Combining practical guidance with real-world stories from entrepreneurs, artists, and innovators, the book explains the evolution of the internet, the fundamentals of Web3, and how individuals can successfully navigate this emerging digital ecosystem. Accessible and forward-looking, it serves as a valuable introduction for readers interested in blockchain innovation, digital ownership, online communities, and the future of the internet.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Bim Handbook - A Guide To Building Information Modeling For Owners, Designers, Engineers, Contractors, And Facility Managers, 4Th Edition
Author: Sacks
Description: A comprehensive guide to modern Building Information Modeling (BIM), BIM Handbook explores the latest tools, standards, and applications transforming the architecture, engineering, and construction industries. Covering BIM integration, digital twins, project management, simulation, and industry frameworks like ISO 19650, the book provides practical insights for owners, designers, engineers, contractors, and facility managers. Updated to reflect current industry practices, it serves as an essential resource for professionals and students involved in construction technology and digital building workflows.

Subject: General
Title: Adult Development And Aging: Biopsychosocial Perspectives, 8Th Edition Paperback With Eepub Access
Author: Whitbourne
Description: A comprehensive and engaging introduction to the aging process, Adult Development and Aging explores adult lifespan development through a biopsychosocial perspective, examining the interaction of biological, psychological, and social factors. Covering topics such as neuroscience, genetics, cognitive changes, mental health, relationships, and healthy aging, the book combines current research with real-world examples and interactive multimedia learning tools. Ideal for students, educators, and researchers, it offers an accessible and up-to-date understanding of adult development and aging across the lifespan.

Subject: General
Title: Health Psychology: Applying Psychological Science To Health And Wellness Paperback With Eepub Access
Author: Nevid
Description: A student-friendly introduction to the connection between psychology, health, and wellness, Health Psychology: Applying Psychological Science to Health and Wellness explores how thoughts, emotions, and behaviors influence physical and mental health. Covering topics such as stress, reproductive and sexual health, chronic illness, psychological wellbeing, and health-related decision-making, the book combines current research with relatable examples and practical applications. Enhanced with interactive multimedia tools, self-assessments, quizzes, videos, and animations, it provides an engaging learning experience that encourages students to apply health psychology concepts to their everyday lives.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Micro Electromechanical Systems (Mems): Practical Lab Manual
Author: Goel
Description: A practical and experiment-focused guide, Micro Electromechanical Systems (MEMS) provides comprehensive coverage of MEMS device design, fabrication, simulation, characterization, and real-world applications. The book combines foundational concepts with hands-on laboratory procedures, covering techniques such as photolithography, soft lithography, 3D printing, laser processing, and multi-physics modeling for MEMS sensors and microfluidic systems. It also explores advanced characterization methods including SEM, XRD, Raman spectroscopy, FTIR, and electrochemical analysis, while highlighting applications in wearables, energy harvesting, and microfluidics. Designed as a practical laboratory resource, it is highly valuable for students, researchers, academicians, and lab professionals working in MEMS, nanotechnology, and advanced manufacturing fields.

Subject: General
Title: The Psychology Of Diversity: Beyond Prejudice And Racism, 2Nd Edition
Author: Jones
Description: A comprehensive and research-driven exploration of diversity, inclusion, and intergroup relations, The Psychology of Diversity: Beyond Prejudice and Racism examines how diversity shapes individual behavior, social systems, and institutional structures. Fully updated to address contemporary issues such as political polarization, health disparities, racial bias, immigration, gender diversity, and debates around equity initiatives, the book combines psychological theory, historical context, neuroscience, and real-world case studies to provide a deeper understanding of prejudice and inclusion. With evidence-based strategies for reducing bias and fostering stronger communities, it serves as an essential resource for students, educators, researchers, and professionals working in social psychology, multicultural studies, education, healthcare, public policy, and organizational leadership.

Subject: Science
Title: Child And Adolescent Therapy: Science And Art, 3Rd Edition
Author: Shapiro
Description: A comprehensive and practice-oriented guide, Child and Adolescent Therapy: Science and Art introduces readers to major therapeutic approaches used in child and adolescent mental health treatment, including cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT), family systems therapy, transdiagnostic approaches, and modular psychotherapies. Rather than promoting a single method, the book emphasizes evidence-based decision-making and demonstrates how clinicians can match therapeutic strategies to individual client needs, diagnoses, and preferences. Integrating outcome research with real-world clinical reasoning, it provides practical examples of therapist-client interactions, treatment planning, and case conceptualization across common conditions such as anxiety and disruptive behavior disorders. Fully updated with contemporary research and new therapeutic models like Internal Family Systems (IFS), the book serves as an essential resource for graduate students, counselors, psychologists, social workers, and practicing mental health professionals seeking both theoretical understanding and practical therapeutic skills.

Subject: General
Title: Beyond Violence+: A Prevention Program For Criminal Justice-Involved Women And Gender-Diverse People Participant?S Workbook
Author: Covington
Description: Beyond Violence: A Prevention Program for Criminal Justice-Involved Women is a comprehensive, evidence-based intervention program developed specifically for women in correctional settings who have committed violent offenses. Structured around 20 interactive sessions, the program explores the personal, relational, social, and emotional factors connected to violence, helping participants understand how thoughts, feelings, trauma, relationships, and environmental influences contribute to violent behavior. Combining a detailed Facilitator Guide with a Participant Workbook, the program teaches practical life skills such as communication, conflict resolution, emotional regulation, decision-making, and calming techniques while encouraging self-reflection and group support.
