Designed for counselors, mental health professionals, and criminal justice program administrators, the program provides a trauma-informed and gender-responsive framework aimed at rehabilitation, personal growth, and violence prevention.

Subject: General
Title: The Handbook Of Linguistic Landscapes And Multilingualism
Author: Gorter
Description: The Handbook of Linguistic Landscapes and Multilingualism offers a comprehensive exploration of how linguistic landscapes, the visible use of language in public spaces such as signs, advertisements, schools, and urban environments, reflect and shape multilingualism in societies around the world. Combining theoretical perspectives, methodological approaches, and international case studies, the book examines the relationships between language, identity, space, politics, education, and social interaction. It covers emerging themes such as translanguaging, minority language ideologies, protest and conflict landscapes, and multilingual practices in cities and classrooms, while also highlighting innovative pedagogical applications for language learning and teaching. Bridging fields such as sociolinguistics, ethnography, discourse studies, education, and social geography, the handbook serves as an essential resource for graduate students, researchers, and scholars interested in multilingualism, language policy, and the social dynamics of language in public spaces.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Design And Forecasting Models For Disease Management
Author: Dutta
Description: Design and Forecasting Models for Disease Management explores how computational methods, artificial intelligence, and machine learning techniques can support smarter and more effective disease management. Comprising 13 chapters, the book examines statistical models, data analysis methods, hybrid machine learning approaches, and biomedical computing techniques used for early disease detection, prediction, diagnosis, and forecasting. It highlights applications such as image segmentation, cellular-level disease analysis, predictive modeling, and intelligent diagnostic systems designed to assist medical experts in identifying disease patterns and improving healthcare decision-making. Combining concepts from computational biology, bioinformatics, epidemiology, and information technology, the book serves as a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, and graduate students interested in AI-driven healthcare analytics and disease forecasting systems.

Subject: General
Title: Multifunctional Materials - Engineering And Biological Applications
Author: Tripathy
Description: Multifunctional Materials: Engineering and Biological Applications provides a comprehensive overview of advanced multifunctional materials and their growing role in engineering, technology, and biological applications. The book explores the chemistry, synthesis, characterization, properties, and practical uses of materials engineered at the molecular and atomic levels to achieve enhanced mechanical, electrical, thermal, and functional performance. Covering interdisciplinary concepts from materials science, chemistry, physics, and engineering, it highlights applications in areas such as biomedical devices, aerospace and defense systems, consumer electronics, renewable energy technologies, and advanced drug delivery systems. By combining foundational principles with emerging innovations, the book serves as a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, scientists, and graduate students interested in the rapidly evolving field of advanced multifunctional materials.

Subject: General
Title: Evaluation Essentials: Methods For Conducting Sound Research, 2Nd Edition
Author: Daponte
Description: Evaluation Essentials: Methods for Conducting Sound Research is a practical and comprehensive guide to program evaluation and applied research methods for nonprofit, public sector, educational, and policy-focused organizations. The book provides a step-by-step introduction to designing and conducting effective evaluations, covering essential topics such as developing evaluation questions, applying program theory, conducting literature reviews, collecting and analyzing qualitative and quantitative data, designing surveys, creating outcome measures, and grant writing. Updated to reflect current best practices, the second edition also addresses contribution analysis, organizational performance evaluation, and international evaluation contexts. Featuring more than 40 real-world examples from fields including public health, education, social work, labor, arts management, and international development, the book equips students, researchers, administrators, and practitioners with the tools needed to assess program effectiveness, demonstrate impact to stakeholders, and support evidence-based decision-making.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Guidelines For Preparing Process Equipment For Maintenance And Return To Service
Author: Ccps
Description: Process Equipment Preparation for Maintenance and Return to Service provides a comprehensive guide to safely preparing industrial process equipment for maintenance activities and recommissioning it for operational use. The book focuses on two critical phases of equipment handover: isolating and preparing equipment for maintenance, and safely returning equipment to service through operational readiness planning and pre-startup safety reviews (PSSR). It examines key industrial safety concerns such as hazard identification, decontamination procedures, de-energization methods, risk reduction strategies, and safe recommissioning practices. Supported by practical case studies, real-world examples, and incident investigation discussions, the book offers valuable guidance for engineers, maintenance personnel, plant operators, and safety professionals seeking to improve operational reliability, reduce maintenance risks, and prevent industrial incidents in manufacturing and process facilities.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Blockchain Technology For The Engineering And Service Sectors
Author: Vaishnavi
Description: Blockchain Technology for the Engineering and Service Sectors provides a comprehensive exploration of blockchain technology and its transformative impact across engineering, manufacturing, finance, and service industries. The book traces the evolution of blockchain from the emergence of Bitcoin to its integration with advanced technologies such as the Internet of Things (IoT), highlighting the growing role of decentralized systems in modern digital infrastructure. It examines blockchain frameworks, architectures, and practical applications in sectors including supply chain management, tourism, healthcare, banking, automation, agriculture, and manufacturing. The text also discusses current implementation challenges, security concerns, emerging trends, and future opportunities in decentralized finance and enterprise blockchain solutions. Combining technical foundations with industry-focused case studies, the book serves as a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, students, and professionals seeking to understand and apply blockchain technology in real-world environments.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Sustainable Operations Of Logistics And Supply Chain Management
Author: Mathiyazhagan
Description: Sustainable Logistics and Intelligent Supply Chain Management explores the evolving intersection of sustainability, intelligent technologies, and modern supply chain management. The book examines how artificial intelligence, machine learning, big data analytics, and digital technologies are transforming logistics operations by improving efficiency, optimizing transportation routes, reducing emissions, and enhancing resource utilization. It addresses critical industry challenges such as high implementation costs, operational resistance, supply chain disruptions, and the need for economically viable sustainable practices. Emphasizing resilience and circular economy principles, the book provides practical strategies for integrating reuse, recycling, waste reduction, and environmentally responsible resource management into supply chains. Supported by real-world applications and contemporary case studies, it serves as a valuable resource for logistics professionals, supply chain managers, policymakers, sustainability advocates, researchers, and students seeking innovative approaches to building resilient, efficient, and sustainable supply chain systems.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Understanding Electromagnetic Transients In Power Systems
Author: Zanetta
Description: Understanding Electromagnetic Transients in Power Systems provides a comprehensive and accessible introduction to electromagnetic transients and their critical role in electrical power systems. The book explains the theoretical foundations, mathematical principles, and practical implications of transient phenomena in power system design and operation. It covers key topics such as time-domain and frequency-domain analysis, electrical circuit transients, traveling waves, overvoltage phenomena, insulation coordination, and transform techniques, while emphasizing the relationship between theoretical calculations and their impact on power equipment and system reliability. Supported by numerous solved examples and practical explanations, the book serves as a valuable learning resource for graduate and advanced undergraduate students, as well as practicing electrical engineers and professionals working in utilities, power generation, transmission, and equipment manufacturing industries.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Functional Composites - Role In Modern Engineering
Author: Charkha
Description: Functional Composites: Role in Modern Engineering explores the latest developments in the mechanics, design, fabrication, and applications of advanced functional composite materials used in modern engineering systems. The book examines the vibration behavior and structural performance of complex components such as composite beams, plates, shells, curved membranes, and rings, with a strong emphasis on coupled mechanical and non-mechanical interactions. It covers advanced material systems including piezoelectric composites, magnetostrictive nanocomposites, active fiber composites, and smart structures, while discussing their characterization, fabrication techniques, sustainability considerations, and large-scale engineering applications. The text also addresses emerging technologies, production challenges, and future opportunities in multifunctional composite design. Combining theoretical concepts with practical engineering insights, the book serves as a valuable reference for researchers, students, materials scientists, and engineers working in aerospace, mechanical, civil, automotive, and smart materials engineering fields.

Subject: General
Title: Quantum Dot Display Science And Technology
Author: Chatterjee
Description: Optimization in Sustainable Energy: Methods and Applications provides a comprehensive exploration of modern optimization techniques used to improve the efficiency, reliability, and performance of sustainable energy systems. The book covers a wide range of topics including mathematical modeling, heuristic and metaheuristic algorithms, machine learning applications, renewable resource management, and energy storage optimization. It examines how advanced optimization methods can support the integration of renewable energy sources such as solar, wind, and hydroelectric power while addressing critical challenges related to energy distribution, resource allocation, carbon reduction, and energy security. The text also highlights the role of multi-criteria decision-making approaches that balance environmental, economic, and social considerations in sustainable energy planning. Combining theoretical foundations with practical examples and real-world applications, the book serves as a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, policymakers, industry professionals, and graduate students working in renewable energy, power systems, sustainability, and energy management.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: The Techno-Legal Dynamics Of Cyber Crimes In Industry 5.0
Author: Kaur
Description: The Techno-Legal Dynamics of Cyber Crimes in Industry 5.0 examines the growing challenges of cybercrime in advanced manufacturing environments shaped by Industry 5.0 technologies such as artificial intelligence, IoT, and robotics. The book explores the intersection of technology and law, highlighting emerging cyber threats, global legal frameworks, and regulatory strategies designed to improve cybersecurity and reduce risks in digitally connected industrial systems.

Subject: Science
Title: Deep Learning Applications In Medical Image Segmentation - Overview, Approaches, And Challenges
Author: Bhat
Description: Deep Learning Applications in Medical Image Segmentation provides a comprehensive overview of how deep learning techniques are transforming medical image segmentation for diagnosis, research, and treatment. The book covers foundational and advanced models such as FCN, UNet, SegNet, DeepLab, and GANs, while exploring applications in brain tumors, retinal imaging, lung disease detection, and other organ-specific analyses. It also discusses current challenges, recent advancements, and future trends in AI-driven medical image analysis, making it a valuable resource for researchers, biomedical engineers, and medical imaging professionals.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Explainable Artificial Intelligence In The Healthcare Industry
Author: Kumar
Description: Explainable AI in the Healthcare Industry explores the role of explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) in improving trust, transparency, and accountability in healthcare systems. The book examines how AI models can provide clear and interpretable explanations for medical decisions, helping healthcare professionals, patients, and regulators better understand AI-driven outcomes. Covering key principles, methodologies, and real-world healthcare applications, it serves as a valuable resource for researchers, healthcare practitioners, and AI professionals working in medical technology and digital healthcare.

Subject: General
Title: Forecasting Methods For Renewable Power Generation
Author: Singh
Description: Forecasting Methods for Renewable Power Generation provides a comprehensive guide to forecasting techniques used in renewable energy generation, demand response, and electricity pricing. The book explores AI and machine learning-based methods that help improve grid stability, optimize energy management, support electricity trading strategies, and enhance market efficiency. Covering practical forecasting models, algorithms, and real-world applications, it serves as a valuable reference for engineers, researchers, students, and professionals working in renewable energy and smart grid systems.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces For 6G And Beyond Wireless Networks
Author: Imoize
Description: Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces for 6G and Beyond Wireless Networks provides a comprehensive overview of reconfigurable intelligent surfaces (RIS) and their role in improving 6G and next-generation wireless networks. The book explores RIS design principles, signal processing techniques, channel modeling, energy and spectral efficiency, security frameworks, and AI/ML-based optimization methods for wireless communication systems. Covering applications in massive MIMO, terrestrial and non-terrestrial networks, and intelligent power control, it serves as a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, and professionals working in advanced wireless communications and 6G technologies.

Subject: Science
Title: The Respiratory System
Author: Peate
Description: The Respiratory System is a concise and accessible guide to the anatomy, physiology, and clinical care of the respiratory system. Designed for nursing and allied health students and practitioners, the book combines clear explanations with colorful illustrations to simplify complex concepts and support both academic learning and practical healthcare application. It also covers the management of respiratory conditions such as COPD, asthma, and lung cancer, helping readers develop the knowledge needed to deliver safe and effective patient care.

Subject: General
Title: The Sociology Of Boredom
Author: Finkielsztein
Description: The Sociology of Boredom explores boredom as a significant yet often overlooked human emotion through both macro- and micro-sociological perspectives. The book introduces a relational-expectational theory of boredom and examines its links to modern society, workplace experiences, consumer culture, marginalized groups, and the information age. Combining insights from sociology, social anthropology, and emotion studies, it provides a detailed analysis of how boredom is socially produced and experienced, making it a valuable resource for students, researchers, and scholars in sociology and related disciplines.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Inverse Differential Quadrature Method And Its Application In Engineering
Author: Ojo
Description: Inverse Differential Quadrature Method and its Application in Engineering provides the first comprehensive treatment of the inverse differential quadrature method (iDQM), an advanced numerical technique designed to solve higher-order differential equations while reducing numerical differentiation errors. The book explains the theoretical foundations, one- and two-dimensional formulations, approximation procedures, and error analysis of iDQM, alongside practical MATLAB-based implementation examples. Covering applications in structural analysis, heat transfer, fluid dynamics, wave propagation, and multiphysics problems, it serves as a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, and graduate students in mechanical, aerospace, civil, and chemical engineering.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Current And Future Cellular Systems - Technologies, Applications, And Challenges
Author: Chopra
Description: Current and Future Cellular Systems: Technologies, Applications, and Challenges provides a comprehensive overview of current and emerging cellular communication technologies, focusing on 5G and beyond. The book explores advanced topics such as millimeter-wave and THz communications, intelligent reflecting surfaces, IoT, AI/ML-assisted resource allocation, autonomous vehicles, quantum computing, and WiFi 6/7 technologies. Combining theoretical concepts, mathematical modeling, and simulation techniques, it serves as a valuable resource for graduate students, researchers, and industry professionals working in wireless communication and next-generation cellular systems.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Making Money Work: How To Rewrite The Rules Of Our Financial System
Author: Sekerke
Description: Making Money Work: How to Rewrite the Rules of Our Financial System explores the structure and functioning of modern fiat monetary systems, focusing on how money is created, regulated, and connected to the broader economy. The book examines the role of commercial banks, banking regulation, fiscal and monetary policy, and the relationship between banking and capital markets. It also proposes reforms to improve financial system stability, lending efficiency, and monetary policy effectiveness, making it a valuable resource for bankers, policymakers, finance professionals, researchers, and readers interested in economics and financial systems.

The book examines the environmental impacts, safety considerations, sustainability challenges, and engineering practices associated with large-scale EV battery production. Combining practical industry insights with technical knowledge, it serves as a valuable resource for engineers, students, researchers, and professionals entering the rapidly growing electric mobility sector.

The book explains sleep cycles, circadian rhythms, dreams, sleep deprivation, and common sleep disorders such as insomnia and sleep apnea, while also exploring the role of wearable sleep technology. Packed with science-backed advice and everyday strategies, it helps readers improve sleep quality, feel more refreshed, and develop healthier sleep habits for overall well-being.

Supported by practical examples, MATLAB resources, review questions, multimedia learning materials, and historical insights, it serves as a valuable resource for students, researchers, engineers, and professionals in electromagnetics and wireless communications.

Combining theoretical principles, engine performance analysis, component aerodynamics, and system integration with practical problem sets and modern sustainability topics, it serves as an essential resource for aerospace and mechanical engineering students, researchers, and aviation professionals.

Combining scholarly research, ethnographic observations, and vivid historical narratives, it provides an accessible and insightful understanding of the role of Sufism in the religious and social development of the world’s largest Muslim-majority region.
