It covers hardware, software, performance, security, and peripheral issues, while also explaining safe maintenance practices and Windows tools to keep a PC running smoothly, quickly, and reliably.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Agent-Powered Growth: Deploy Ai Agents That Build Your Marketing Pipeline 24/7
Author: Sjouwerman
Description: Agent-Powered Growth by Stu Sjouwerman explores how autonomous AI agents are transforming modern marketing. The book shows how marketers can shift from routine execution roles to strategic “AI orchestrators,” using agent-driven systems for content creation, campaign optimization, lead nurturing, and market research. It combines real-world case studies with practical frameworks for deploying AI tools responsibly, improving ROI, and adapting careers in an AI-driven business landscape.

Subject: General
Title: Degrees Of Change: What Ai Means For Education And The Next Generation
Author: Lavista Ferres
Description: Degrees of Change: What AI Means for Education and the Next Generation by Juan M. Lavista Ferres and contributing scholars explores how artificial intelligence is reshaping education, workforce readiness, and learning systems. It examines the role of AI literacy, ethics, and evolving teaching methods, while also analyzing how large language models are influencing students, educators, and knowledge-based jobs. The book provides research-driven insights and practical guidance for adapting education systems to an AI-driven future.

Subject: Economics
Title: Political Philosophy - The Fundamentals
Author: Brooks
Description: Political Philosophy: The Fundamentals introduces core concepts of political thought by engaging with key historical and contemporary philosophers, making it accessible even to readers without prior background. Across eight concise chapters, it explores major debates on freedom, equality, human rights, justice, global inequality, climate responsibility, and punishment, while connecting classical ideas to modern issues. It also highlights influential thinkers such as John Rawls and provides structured explanations and further readings to help readers form informed perspectives on political and ethical questions shaping today’s world.

Subject: General
Title: Aimé Césaire - Inventor Of Souls
Author: Hiddleston
Description: This is an introduction to Aimé Césaire, the influential Caribbean writer and political figure best known for Notebook of a Return to My Native Land and for shaping the concept of négritude—a cultural and philosophical movement centered on Black identity, creativity, and self-determination. Jane Hiddleston’s account presents Césaire as both a major literary voice and a long-serving political leader in Martinique, who used his roles as mayor and French National Assembly deputy to advocate for anti-colonial freedom and racial equality. The book highlights how his work blends poetry, politics, and resistance, showing that meaningful social change can emerge both through institutional power and cultural expression.

Subject: General
Title: Against Post-Liberalism: Why 'Family, Faith And Flag' Is A Dead End For The Left
Author: Kelly
Description: This book by Paul Kelly critically examines the rise of post-liberal political thought, which argues that liberalism is in crisis and proposes alternatives rooted in family, faith, and national identity. Kelly strongly rejects these claims, arguing that post-liberalism misdiagnoses modern social and economic problems and offers unrealistic or regressive solutions. Drawing on a range of thinkers associated with the movement, he contends that post-liberal ideas fail to address the realities of globalization and technological change, and may undermine values like equality, social mobility, and education. Ultimately, the book defends liberalism as the most viable and humane framework for addressing the challenges of contemporary politics.

Subject: General
Title: The New Empire Of Ai: The Future Of Global Inequality
Author: Adams
Description: This book by Rachel Adams argues that artificial intelligence is not just a technological revolution but a deeply political and economic one that risks widening global inequality. It highlights how AI development is currently concentrated in wealthy nations, reinforcing existing power imbalances and creating new forms of dependency for poorer regions. The author warns that these disparities could fuel migration pressures, political instability, and environmental harm linked to the AI supply chain. Ultimately, it calls for urgent action to decentralize AI development and reform its incentives so the technology can benefit all countries more equitably rather than deepening global divides.

Subject: Economics
Title: The Care Economy
Author: Jackson
Description: This book by Tim Jackson explores how care—despite being essential to human life, health, and wellbeing—is often undervalued in modern economic systems. It argues that care work is both socially vital and systematically underpaid, reflecting a deeper imbalance in capitalism where productivity is prioritized over human wellbeing. Drawing on history, economics, medicine, and philosophy, Jackson examines how this situation developed and why it persists, including the gendered nature of care work. He ultimately calls for a rethinking of economic priorities, proposing care and health as central organizing principles for a more humane and sustainable society.

Subject: Economics
Title: The Care Economy
Author: Jackson
Description: Tim Jackson’s The Care Economy examines how care—despite being essential for human life and wellbeing—is systematically undervalued in modern economic systems. The book argues that care work is vital but often underpaid and overlooked because economies prioritize productivity and profit over health and human needs. Through history, economics, philosophy, and gender analysis, Jackson explores how this imbalance developed and why it persists. He ultimately proposes rethinking society’s priorities by placing care and health at the center of economic design, calling for a more humane and sustainable model of prosperity.

Subject: Economics
Title: Institutional Economics: Theory And Practice
Author: Sturgeon
Description: This book offers a clear introduction to institutional economics, a field that studies how institutions—such as markets, governments, corporations, property systems, and labor structures—shape and control economic activity. It explains how these institutions influence key economic decisions about what is produced, how it is produced, and for whom it is produced. Across ten chapters, it combines theory, history, and real-world applications to explore topics like production, consumption, inequality, development, and value theory, while also examining the role of specific institutions in the modern economy. Designed for beginners, it serves as an accessible guide for understanding how institutional forces structure economic systems and outcomes.

Subject: General
Title: China'S Quest For Military Supremacy
Author: Wuthnow
Description: This book provides an accessible overview of China’s rapidly modernizing military and its growing ambitions to become a “world-class” force comparable to the United States. It examines the relationship between the Chinese Communist Party and the People’s Liberation Army, alongside key areas such as strategic thinking, military restructuring, conventional and nuclear modernization, and regional power projection. The book also explores China’s involvement in territorial disputes, its preparations for potential conflict scenarios, and its expanding global military presence. Finally, it assesses the limitations and challenges the Chinese military still faces, suggesting that while it is increasingly powerful in Asia, its global operational readiness is still evolving.

Subject: General
Title: China'S Quest For Military Supremacy
Author: Wuthnow
Description: This book offers a broad overview of China’s modern military transformation and rising global ambitions under Xi Jinping. It examines how the People’s Liberation Army has been modernized and reorganized to strengthen its regional power, particularly in relation to territorial disputes in Asia and strategic priorities such as Taiwan. The book also explores the political and strategic relationship between the Chinese Communist Party and the military, as well as developments in conventional and nuclear capabilities, military diplomacy, and global influence. While acknowledging China’s rapid progress, it also highlights existing limitations, suggesting that the PLA is still primarily focused on regional dominance rather than full global military parity with the United States.

Subject: General
Title: Also A History Of Philosophy, Volume 3: Rational Freedom. Traces Of The Discourse On Faith And Knowledge
Author: Habermas
Description: This book presents Jürgen Habermas’s final volume in his history of philosophy, offering interpretive readings of major thinkers who shaped modern and contemporary philosophical thought. It begins with Hume and Kant, then traces the development of post-metaphysical philosophy through German Idealism, historicism, and reactions to Hegel, culminating in analyses of Marx, Kierkegaard, and Peirce. Across these readings, Habermas develops and defends his own philosophical framework—centered on communicative rationality, intersubjectivity, and discourse-based theories of meaning, ethics, and politics. Rather than offering a simple survey of philosophers, the work presents a thematic and critical engagement that emphasizes philosophy as an ongoing, dialogical process aimed at understanding and justification.

Subject: Economics
Title: Socialism For Today - Escaping The Cruelties Of Capitalism
Author: Kotz
Description: This book by David Kotz presents a critical analysis of modern capitalism, arguing that it has produced deep and persistent problems such as inequality, housing and healthcare crises, educational debt, discrimination, and environmental degradation. Kotz suggests that these issues cannot be fully solved through reform alone and instead advocates for developing a new form of socialism suited to twenty-first-century conditions. Drawing lessons from past socialist systems—both their successes and failures—he outlines a vision for a democratic, participatory economic model aimed at achieving greater justice, equality, sustainability, and human well-being, along with a practical discussion of how such a transition might be pursued.

Subject: General
Title: Post-Liberalism
Author: Sleat
Description: This book by Matt Sleat offers a critical examination of post-liberalism, a growing intellectual movement within the contemporary New Right that challenges mainstream liberal political thought. It analyzes the ideas of key thinkers such as Patrick Deneen and Adrian Vermeule, exploring the movement’s theological influences and its relationship to broader currents like National Conservatism. Sleat argues that post-liberalism is based on a flawed understanding of modern social problems and suggests that its proposed “common good” politics is ultimately incoherent and potentially risks authoritarian outcomes. The book also reflects on how liberalism might respond to these challenges and what the rise of post-liberal thought signals for the future of Western political ideology.

Subject: General
Title: Ecocide In Ukraine: The Environmental Cost Of Russia?S War
Author: Tsymbalyuk
Description: This book by Darya Tsymbalyuk explores the environmental destruction caused by Russia’s war in Ukraine, alongside its human and ecological consequences. It describes how military operations have damaged forests, rivers, agricultural land, and biodiversity, including the loss and displacement of wildlife and the contamination of soil and water. A major focus is the concept of “ecocide,” illustrated through events such as large-scale flooding and habitat destruction. Blending personal reflection with environmental research, the book also captures the lived experience of Ukrainians coping with these changes and reflects on the emotional and cultural loss tied to damaged landscapes and ecosystems during the ongoing conflict.

Subject: General
Title: Ecocide In Ukraine: The Environmental Cost Of Russia?S War
Author: Tsymbalyuk
Description: This book by Darya Tsymbalyuk explores the environmental devastation caused by Russia’s war in Ukraine, focusing on the concept of “ecocide” and its long-term impact on ecosystems and biodiversity. It describes widespread destruction of forests, rivers, farmland, and wildlife, including contamination of soil and water and large-scale habitat loss such as the Kakhovka Dam disaster. Alongside this ecological damage, the book also reflects on the human experience of living through the war, combining environmental research with personal and emotional observations of a changing homeland and the loss of familiar natural landscapes.

Subject: General
Title: Urban Warfare In The Twenty-First Century
Author: King
Description: This book by Anthony King examines how modern warfare has increasingly shifted into cities, where major conflicts in places like Mosul, Aleppo, Mariupol, Bakhmut, and Gaza demonstrate the intensity of urban combat in the twenty-first century. It analyzes how battles in dense urban environments have evolved into fragmented “micro-sieges,” fought simultaneously at street level, underground, and in the air, often over individual buildings and districts. The book also explores how digital communication and social media broadcast these conflicts globally, sometimes involving outside audiences in the dynamics of war. Drawing on recent conflicts and historical comparisons, it provides an updated and interdisciplinary account of urban warfare and its human, strategic, and technological dimensions.

Subject: General
Title: The Rich And The Poor
Author: Kitcher
Description: This book by Philip Kitcher reflects on the growing separation between ethics and public policy, arguing that modern political and economic thinking has increasingly prioritized competition and national advantage over global moral responsibility. It contrasts today’s “dog-eat-dog” policy environment with earlier periods when improving global living standards was a central concern of serious policymakers. Kitcher proposes that addressing urgent global challenges like climate change requires reintegrating ethics into political and economic decision-making through inclusive, informed, and collaborative deliberation. Ultimately, the book warns that ignoring ethical inquiry in public policy risks deepening global inequality and creating long-term harm for future generations.

Subject: General
Title: At The Margins Of The Modern State: Critical Theory And Law
Author: Benhabib
Description: This book by Seyla Benhabib examines the current crisis in the post–World War II international order, marked by ongoing conflicts, weakened trust in human rights, and challenges to liberal democratic ideals. It argues that instead of abandoning universal principles in the face of global inequality and postcolonial critique, we should rethink and reconstruct them in a more inclusive and globally grounded way. Drawing on traditions such as Frankfurt School critical theory and liberal philosophers like Kant and Rawls, the book explores issues including sovereignty, international law, refugee rights, and climate governance. Ultimately, it defends the possibility of renewing universal human rights and global cooperation through more democratic and equitable forms of world-building.

Subject: General
Title: At The Margins Of The Modern State: Critical Theory And Law
Author: Benhabib
Description: This book by Seyla Benhabib examines the breakdown of the post–World War II international order and the rising skepticism toward democracy, human rights, and global cooperation amid ongoing conflicts in regions such as Ukraine, Gaza, Darfur, and the Congo. It critiques simplistic explanations like “the rise of the rest” and instead focuses on how universal moral and legal principles can be rethought in a more inclusive way that accounts for both the emancipatory and oppressive legacies of Western modernity. Drawing on critical theory and liberal traditions from thinkers like Kant, Rawls, and Dworkin, the book explores issues such as sovereignty, international law, refugee rights, and climate governance. It ultimately defends the idea that a more just and inclusive form of global universalism is still possible through renewed world-building and engagement with postcolonial perspectives.

Subject: General
Title: When Nobody?S Listening: Inside Sweden?S Drug Gangs Cloth
Author: Salihu
Description: This book by Diamant Salihu recounts the major breakthrough in European cybercrime policing when French authorities successfully infiltrated the encrypted messaging platform EncroChat. This operation exposed real-time communications between members of organised crime networks, including drug trafficking operations, murder plots, and coordinated criminal activity across multiple countries. The book follows how law enforcement agencies used this intelligence to carry out rapid arrests while carefully maintaining the secrecy of the surveillance. It also reveals the structure and reach of transnational criminal networks in Europe, highlighting key figures and rivalries within the drug trade, and showing how digital encryption both protected and ultimately exposed modern organised crime.

Subject: General
Title: Gaza: The Dream And The Nightmare
Author: Norman
Description: This book by Julie M. Norman and Maia Carter Hallward presents a grounded, people-centered account of Gaza, tracing its history from its early development through decades of occupation and into the current ongoing conflict. Rather than focusing solely on political leaders or abstract analysis, it highlights the lived experiences of ordinary Gazans—such as students, teachers, journalists, and aid workers—through firsthand testimonies. The book also engages with complex legal, moral, and humanitarian debates, including issues like war crimes, resistance, and the role of aid in conflict zones. Overall, it aims to move beyond simplified narratives by combining historical context with human stories to offer a more nuanced understanding of Gaza and its people.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Marketing - A Sociological Approach
Author: Mellet
Description: This book presents marketing from a sociological perspective, explaining it not just as a business tool but as a broader social practice that shapes markets, culture, and everyday life. It draws on decades of sociological research to examine how marketing works, who marketing professionals are, and how their activities influence consumption and economic behavior. The author introduces key concepts such as markets, organizations, culture, and consumption, showing how marketing operates according to specific social rules and contexts. Overall, it offers a clear and accessible introduction to how marketing functions as a powerful force in shaping both economic systems and society.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Marketing - A Sociological Approach
Author: Mellet
Description: This book offers a sociological introduction to marketing, presenting it as more than just a business function or set of techniques. It explores how marketing has become an influential force in modern life and examines the professionals behind it, what they do, and how their work shapes both markets and society. Drawing on forty years of sociological research, it introduces key concepts such as consumption, markets, organizations, and culture to explain how marketing operates within specific social rules and contexts. Overall, it shows marketing as a pervasive social activity that actively constructs economic systems, consumer behavior, and cultural meaning.

Subject: General
Title: Virtuous Hypocrisy
Author: Urbinati
Description: Hypocrisy is usually seen as a moral failure because it creates a gap between what people say and what they do, and that gap can undermine trust, which is essential for society, friendship, and love. In that sense, a consistent hypocrite can look like a quiet threat to the moral order. But Nadia Urbinati’s idea of “virtuous hypocrisy” complicates this view: not all inconsistency is harmful, because everyday life often requires restraint, tact, and selective honesty to maintain relationships and social peace. Speaking every thought without filter would damage cooperation just as much as deception does. So hypocrisy becomes morally problematic only when it turns into systematic deception or manipulation, rather than a limited, context-sensitive form of civility that helps social life function.

Subject: General
Title: Flawed Strategy: Why Smart Leaders Make Bad Decisions
Author: Heuser
Description: This book argues that major strategic failures—like Zelensky underestimating Russia in 2022, Chamberlain’s Munich agreement in 1938, or Israeli intelligence blind spots before the 2023 Hamas attack—often stem not simply from lack of information, but from “mirror-imaging”: decision-makers assume their adversaries think and behave according to similar values, logic, and constraints as themselves. When leaders interpret opponents through their own worldview, they tend to dismiss actions that don’t fit as irrational or unlikely, even when those actions are fully rational within a different belief system. The result is systematic misreading of intent, reinforced by bureaucracy, political pressure, and cognitive bias, which can turn understandable misjudgments into catastrophic strategic failures.

Subject: General
Title: Solidarity Between Species: Living With Animals Exposed To Pandemic Viruses
Author: Keck
Description: This book frames Covid-19 as a biopolitical crisis rooted in zoonosis—where a virus moving between animals and humans exposes the deep interconnection of species in global health. Drawing on Foucault’s ideas of biopolitics, it shows how modern states manage life through public health systems, surveillance, and long-term preservation of biological materials (“cryopolitics”) to anticipate future outbreaks. At the same time, it questions whether these technologies truly liberate or simply extend control over life. By focusing on animal–human transmission and “sentinel” species that signal ecological danger, it argues for a form of interspecies solidarity, where understanding disease requires breaking down the boundary between nature and society and recognizing shared vulnerability across living systems.

Subject: General
Title: Solidarity Between Species: Living With Animals Exposed To Pandemic Viruses
Author: Keck
Description: The book reads Covid-19 as a biopolitical crisis rooted in zoonosis, showing how a virus moving between animals and humans reveals deep interdependence across species. Using Foucault’s concept of biopolitics, it explains how modern public health systems regulate life through surveillance, containment, and even “cryopolitics,” where biological material is preserved to anticipate future threats. It also questions whether these systems are truly emancipatory or forms of extended control over life. Finally, it highlights interspecies “sentinels”—animals that signal environmental and disease risks—arguing for a broader understanding of solidarity that breaks down the boundary between humans and nature in responding to global threats like pandemics and climate change.

Subject: General
Title: Elect Women For A Change: The Path To Gender Parity In Politics
Author: Krook
Description: This book argues that although women have made progress in politics over recent decades, true gender parity is still far from achieved, both in numbers and in meaningful influence. It defines parity not just as equal representation, but as equal access, power, and recognition of diversity among women themselves. Drawing on global evidence, it shows that gender parity improves democratic legitimacy, policy responsiveness, and overall governance quality. The author identifies major barriers such as socialization, discrimination, and political backlash, and evaluates strategies used worldwide to overcome them. Ultimately, it calls for collective action, stressing that achieving political equality is essential for stronger and more inclusive democracies.

Subject: General
Title: Morality And Responsibility
Author: Scanlon
Description: This collection of essays presents T. M. Scanlon’s reflections on the development and defense of his contractualist moral theory and his account of moral responsibility and blame. Written over two decades, the essays clarify how his ideas relate to and respond to other major philosophers such as Parfit, Frankfurt, Watson, and Korsgaard, while also addressing key objections. Together, they offer a deeper understanding of Scanlon’s ethical framework and its place within contemporary moral, political, and legal philosophy.

Subject: General
Title: There Was No Revolution: Reflections On Property, Power And The Servile Condition
Author: Malabou
Description: Catherine Malabou argues that the French Revolution was only nominally revolutionary, since older structures of privilege and domination persisted beneath the appearance of change. Drawing on Proudhon’s critique of property as a form of domination (“property is theft”), she connects this failure of rupture to the enduring power of private property across history. Her analysis extends this insight to the present, showing how colonial legacies, labour relations, and political systems continue to reproduce inequality despite formal claims of emancipation. The book ultimately re-examines the link between property and domination in order to question whether genuine political transformation has ever fully occurred.

Subject: General
Title: There Was No Revolution: Reflections On Property, Power And The Servile Condition
Author: Malabou
Description: Catherine Malabou argues that the French Revolution was only a revolution in name, since the old structures of privilege and domination continued to shape everyday life despite formal political change. Drawing on Proudhon’s famous critique that “property is theft,” she links this failure of true rupture to the enduring power of private property as a system of domination. She extends this analysis to the present, showing how similar forms of inequality persist through colonial legacies, labour systems, and political institutions. The book ultimately reopens the question of whether genuine emancipation has ever been achieved and how domination continues to operate under modern conditions.

Subject: General
Title: Communicative Ai: A Critical Introduction To Large Language Models
Author: Coeckelbergh
Description: This book explores large language models like ChatGPT and LaMDA as both disruptive technologies and philosophical tools for rethinking language, communication, and intelligence. It uses LLMs to raise deeper questions about consciousness, authorship, truth, and whether machines can meaningfully be considered writers. Drawing on philosophy, linguistics, and communication theory, the authors analyze how these systems reshape our understanding of writing and meaning. The result is a critical yet accessible introduction to AI that highlights its technical impact while also probing its broader implications for human thought and communication.

Subject: General
Title: Communicative Ai: A Critical Introduction To Large Language Models
Author: Coeckelbergh
Description: This book presents large language models as both transformative technologies and philosophical provocations that force us to rethink language, intelligence, and communication. It explores questions such as whether AI systems can be considered authors, how they reshape ideas of consciousness and truth, and whether they signal a fundamental shift in writing itself. Drawing on philosophy, linguistics, and communication theory, it provides a critical introduction to LLMs and their impact on human thought and society.

Subject: General
Title: I Hate Myself: Overcome Self-Loathing And Realize Why You?Re Wrong About You
Author: Aguirre
Description: This book by Dr. Blaise Aguirre explores the serious issue of self-hatred, explaining how it can become deeply rooted in a person’s identity and why surface-level ideas like “self-love” are often not enough to address it. Drawing on clinical experience and patient stories, it examines the psychological origins of self-loathing, its links to conditions such as depression and borderline personality disorder, and its connection to suicidal thoughts and behaviors. The book also offers practical, evidence-informed strategies aimed at helping readers move from self-hatred toward self-compassion and healthier self-understanding, making it relevant for individuals struggling with these feelings, their families, and mental health professionals.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Stargazer: Unleashing The Brilliance Of Building Brighter Teams
Author: Desiderio
Description: The Stargazer: Unleashing the Brilliance of Building Brighter Teams by Katie P. Desiderio and Michael G. Frino is a leadership fable about building stronger, more connected teams through trust, growth, and collaboration. Through the story of Jack, a vineyard owner rediscovering leadership, the book introduces practical frameworks and reflective exercises to help leaders create supportive, high-performing team cultures.

Subject: computer science
Title: Virtual Reality Technology, Third Edition
Author: Burdea
Description: Virtual Reality Technology (3rd Edition) is a comprehensive and updated guide to the science, technology, and applications of virtual reality. Covering the evolution of VR from its origins to modern innovations, the book explores key topics such as input and output interfaces, rendering systems, object modeling, AI-driven virtual environments, and VR programming tools like Unity 3D. It also places strong emphasis on human factors, including user experience, cybersickness, and the broader societal impact of VR. With practical examples in healthcare, gaming, education, rehabilitation, and defense, the book serves as a valuable resource for students, researchers, and professionals interested in the rapidly growing field of virtual reality.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Structural Dynamics In Engineering Design
Author: Carrella
Description: Structural Dynamics in Engineering Design provides a comprehensive introduction to structural dynamics and its role in modern engineering design. Written by experts in the field, the book combines theoretical foundations with practical applications, covering vibration theory, experimental dynamics, numerical analysis methods, and finite element modeling. It further explores advanced topics such as system identification, nonlinear analysis, and model validation and updating. The final section demonstrates how these methods are applied in real engineering scenarios, including aerospace and aircraft design. Designed for researchers, practitioners, and graduate students, the book offers a multidisciplinary approach to integrating structural dynamics into advanced engineering design processes.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Plant Optimization In The Process Industries: Incorporating Equipment/Assets In The Decision-Making Process
Author: Moran
Description: Plant Optimization in the Process Industries explores how refining and petrochemical plants can improve profitability through an asset-focused approach to optimization. Rather than concentrating only on process operations, the book emphasizes optimizing the performance and financial value of industrial assets, where even small efficiency gains can produce major economic benefits. It introduces advanced methods such as constrained nonlinear optimization, statistical asset modeling, and Monte Carlo simulation, while comparing process-side and asset-side technologies. Supported by a real-world case study, the book is particularly valuable for engineers, operators, managers, and professionals working in hydrocarbon, chemical, and other asset-intensive industries.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Process Gas Chromatography - Advanced Design And Troubleshooting
Author: Waters
Description: Process Gas Chromatography: Advanced Design and Troubleshooting provides an in-depth guide to the advanced principles, optimization, and practical application of process gas chromatography (PGC) in industrial fluid processing. Building on the author’s earlier work on PGC fundamentals, this book focuses on improving system reliability, accuracy, and performance while offering extensive troubleshooting techniques and diagnostic test procedures for identifying and correcting malfunctions. Each chapter includes summaries and self-assessment questions, making the book valuable for both learning and practical reference. It is especially suited for process analyzer engineers, chemists, maintenance professionals, and troubleshooting specialists working in process industries.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Slow-Wave Microwave And Mm-Wave Passive Circuits
Author: Ferrari
Description: Slow-wave Microwave and mm-wave Passive Circuits explores the theory, design, and applications of slow-wave technologies used in microwave and millimeter-wave passive circuits. The book covers modern slow-wave structures, their electrical modeling, and circuit optimization techniques, showing how these concepts can reduce circuit size, improve performance, and lower material costs. It discusses applications in areas such as automotive radar, medical and security systems, and high-speed 5G and future 6G communications. With examples across CMOS, PCB, and nanowire technologies, the text explains transmission line behavior, energy storage concepts, and coupled-line performance, making it a valuable resource for graduate students, engineers, researchers, and applied physicists.

Subject: general
Title: Onshore And Offshore Wind Energy - Evolution, Grid Integration, And Impact
Author: Fthenakis
Description: Onshore and Offshore Wind Energy (2nd Edition) offers a detailed overview of the science, technology, and global development of wind energy, focusing on large onshore and offshore wind turbines and wind farms. It explains the fundamentals of wind energy conversion, turbine structures, offshore foundations, installation methods, grid integration, maintenance, and reliability, while also covering sustainability topics such as recycling, green hydrogen production, and climate impact reduction. Enhanced with illustrations, real-world examples, photographs, and chapter exercises, the book serves as both an educational resource and a practical guide for students, professionals, policymakers, and newcomers to the renewable energy sector.

Subject: general
Title: Classroom Assessment Techniques - Formative Feedback Tools For College And University Teachers, Third Edition
Author: Angelo
Description: Classroom Assessment Techniques (3rd Edition) is a practical, research-based guide to formative assessment in higher education, designed to help instructors evaluate and improve student learning in face-to-face, hybrid, and online classrooms. Drawing on decades of educational research and practice, the book provides over 50 adaptable classroom assessment techniques (CATs), along with case studies, implementation strategies, and tools for analyzing assessment data. It also introduces resources such as the Course Learning Outcomes Inventory (CLOI) and the Teaching Goals Inventory (TGI) to help educators identify and prioritize learning objectives. Written for busy educators and assessment professionals, the book offers clear, step-by-step guidance for integrating effective assessment into teaching practice across disciplines.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Materials And Methods For Industrial Wastewater And Groundwater Treatment
Author: Nataraj
Description: Materials and Methods for Industrial Wastewater and Groundwater Treatment provides an up-to-date overview of advanced materials and treatment technologies used to remove pollutants from industrial wastewater and groundwater. The book focuses on innovative and next-generation solutions, including emerging materials with strong potential for commercial and environmental applications. Combining cutting-edge research with practical case studies, it examines modern treatment methods, nanomaterial applications, and real-world implementation strategies. Designed for industrial chemists, environmental engineers, researchers, and wastewater treatment professionals, the book serves as a valuable resource for understanding current and future approaches to sustainable water treatment.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Impedance Source Matrix Converters And Control
Author: Liu
Description: Impedance Source Matrix Converters and Control presents advanced research and practical design approaches for modern power electronic converters used in motor drives, renewable energy systems, and industrial applications. The book explains the fundamentals, topologies, control strategies, and applications of impedance source matrix converters, with a focus on improving efficiency, reliability, compactness, and power quality. It covers topics such as converter modeling, modulation techniques, optimization control, LC filter integration, and sustainable energy conversion, while also discussing future challenges and research directions. Supported by tutorials, simulations, experimental results, and learning resources, the book is intended for researchers, postgraduate students, and engineers working in power electronics and renewable energy technologies.

Subject: Engineering
Title: First And Second Order Circuits And Equations - Technical Background And Insights
Author: O'Rourke
Description: First and Second Order Circuits and Equations is a detailed guide to understanding electric and electronic circuits that contain capacitors and inductors, the key energy-storage elements responsible for behaviors such as exponential decay, resonance, overshoot, and ringing. The book explains both time-domain and frequency-domain circuit responses while emphasizing the relationships between core concepts like voltage and current, capacitance and inductance, and series and parallel circuits. It also covers important topics such as damping, quality factor, bandwidth, root locus analysis, and Kirchhoff’s laws, presenting them in a clear and accessible format. Designed for students and industry professionals alike, the book serves as both a learning resource and a practical reference for circuit theory and related differential equations.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Design Of Shipboard Power System Grounding/Earthing
Author: Islam
Description: Design of Shipboard Power System Grounding/Earthing provides a comprehensive guide to grounding and earthing practices in modern shipboard power systems, addressing the increased complexity introduced by technologies such as variable frequency drives and electric propulsion systems. The book explains both traditional and common-mode grounding concepts, comparing terrestrial and marine grounding approaches while covering AC and DC system grounding, high-resistance grounding, and related international standards. Through step-by-step explanations, engineering drawings, and practical case studies, it offers engineers and marine professionals the tools needed to design reliable and safe shipboard electrical systems. It is also a valuable resource for shipbuilders, operators, regulatory organizations, and students in marine electrical engineering.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Incompressible Flow, Fifth Edition
Author: Panton
Description: Incompressible Flow (5th Edition) is a comprehensive textbook on fluid dynamics that balances physical intuition, mathematical analysis, and real-world flow behavior. It carefully develops the fundamental laws governing fluid motion, including the Navier–Stokes equations, while emphasizing dimensional analysis, scaling, and the effects of different Reynolds and Mach numbers. The book examines incompressible flow as a special case of fluid behavior and explores topics such as lubrication theory, flow stability, turbulence, and, in this edition, gas dynamics and compressible flow. Updated with new material on wind turbines, practical examples, end-of-chapter problems, and downloadable MATLAB codes, it serves as a valuable resource for advanced undergraduate and graduate students, engineers, and researchers in fluid mechanics.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Sensor Fusion Approaches For Positioning, Navigation, And Mapping: How Autonomous Vehicles And Robots Navigate In The Real World: With Matlab Examples
Author: Atia
Description: Sensor Fusion Approaches for Positioning, Navigation, and Mapping provides a comprehensive introduction to the theory and practical implementation of sensor fusion technologies used in modern navigation and mapping systems. The book explains how data from inertial sensors, radio positioning systems, cameras, radar, LiDAR, and depth sensors can be integrated to improve positioning accuracy and environmental awareness. It combines mathematical foundations—such as linear algebra, coordinate transformations, kinematics, probability, and signal processing—with practical applications in robotics, drones, wearable devices, and autonomous vehicles. Supported by case studies, solved problems, tutorials, and sample code, the book is designed for students, researchers, and engineers seeking both conceptual understanding and hands-on experience in sensor fusion and state estimation systems.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Leadership Pipeline: Developing Leaders In The Digital Age, 3Rd Edition
Author: Charan
Description: The Leadership Pipeline (3rd Edition) presents a practical framework for identifying, developing, and sustaining future leaders within an organization. Updated for the digital age, the book explains the responsibilities, skills, time priorities, and values required at each level of leadership, while showing how leadership development can be integrated with succession planning. Drawing on real-world examples from major companies, it offers tools for evaluating leadership potential objectively, reducing bias in talent selection, and building a continuous pipeline of capable leaders. Designed for managers, executives, and business leaders, the book serves as a strategic guide to strengthening long-term organizational leadership and stability.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Beauty Of Success: Start, Grow, And Accelerate Your Brand
Author: Bracken-Ferguso
Description: The Beauty of Success by Kendra Bracken-Ferguson is a motivational guide for entrepreneurs, aspiring business leaders, and corporate professionals seeking purpose-driven success. Through her own journey as a founder and the experiences of other leaders in the beauty industry, Bracken-Ferguson shares practical insights on aligning personal values—such as community, mentorship, education, and financial growth—with career and business goals. The book encourages readers to discover their strengths, reconnect with their passions, overcome barriers, and build a meaningful professional path guided by self-awareness and intentional growth.

Subject: general
Title: Geospatial Technology For Natural Resource Management
Author: Kanga
Description: This book explores how modern geospatial technologies such as Remote Sensing (RS), Geographic Information Systems (GIS), and Global Positioning Systems (GPS) are used to assess, manage, and protect natural resources. It emphasizes that sustainable resource planning begins with evaluating resource availability, monitoring environmental changes, and understanding long-term impacts before developing mitigation strategies. The text highlights how these technologies support surveying, mapping, environmental monitoring, disaster management, and sustainable development by providing accurate spatial data and analytical tools. Together, GPS, GIS, and remote sensing enable researchers and decision-makers to study complex environmental and resource-related challenges effectively at local, regional, and global scales.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Practical Control System Design - Real World Designs Implemented On Emulated Industrial Systems
Author: Medioli
Description: Practical Control System Design: Real World Designs Implemented on Emulated Industrial Systems combines control theory with hands-on industrial applications to help readers develop practical control engineering skills. Drawing from the authors’ real industrial projects, the book presents case studies as “Virtual Laboratories,” allowing readers to explore and experiment with systems such as servo mechanisms, wind power generation, boiler control, rolling mills, rocket dynamics, and fluid storage systems. Alongside these applications, it explains key control concepts including PID control, stability analysis, Laplace and Z-transforms, Kalman filters, state-space modeling, multivariable systems, and nonlinear control. Supported by accompanying software and practical exercises, the book serves as a valuable resource for engineering students, instructors, and practicing control system engineers.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Hydrogen Energetics - A Future Clean Energy Carrier
Author: Press
Description: Hydrogen Energetics provides a modern introduction to hydrogen as a clean and flexible energy source with significant potential for sustainable energy systems. The book explains the scientific foundations of hydrogen energy, including hydrogen production methods, fuel cell technologies, and the environmental impacts of hydrogen utilization. Alongside technical concepts, it also examines the economic, infrastructural, and practical challenges involved in adopting hydrogen on a large scale. Combining theoretical knowledge with current research and applications, the book serves as a valuable resource for students, researchers, and professionals working in renewable energy, energy storage, sustainability, and environmental science.

Subject: computer science
Title: Pen Testing From Contract To Report
Author: Basta
Description: Pen Testing from Contract to Report provides a step-by-step guide to penetration testing, the practice of evaluating computer systems and applications by simulating cyber-attacks to identify security vulnerabilities. Organized around the concept of the “Penetration Testing Life Cycle,” the book walks readers through each phase of a professional pen test, from planning and execution to analysis and reporting. It also covers techniques related to bypassing security defenses such as intrusion detection systems, firewalls, and honeypots, while aligning its content with certifications like CompTIA PenTest+. Supported by practice software, case studies, and review questions, the book is designed for cybersecurity professionals and advanced students seeking practical skills in ethical hacking and system security assessment.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Effective Business Communication For Dummies
Author: Schiefelbein
Description: Effective Business Communication For Dummies is a practical guide to improving communication skills in professional and everyday settings. Written in the approachable “For Dummies” style, the book covers essential topics such as active listening, assertive speaking, conflict resolution, virtual communication, presentations, interviews, and intercultural communication. It helps readers learn how to build stronger relationships, communicate clearly in emails and meetings, and handle challenging conversations with confidence. Suitable for both team members and leaders, the book offers straightforward strategies for becoming a more effective and confident communicator in all aspects of work and life.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Iot Signal Detection
Author: Han
Description: IoT Signal Detection provides a clear and structured introduction to signal detection techniques used in Internet of Things (IoT) communication systems. Combining theoretical foundations with practical applications, the book explains key topics such as device activity detection, sparse and collided signal detection, delay estimation, and backscatter communication. It covers advanced methods including variational inference, compressive sensing, ML, ZF, MMSE detection, and interference cancellation techniques, while also presenting recent research developments in the field. Designed for graduate students and wireless communication engineers, the book offers an accessible pathway from basic concepts to advanced IoT signal processing and estimation methods.

Subject: general
Title: Time Series Forecasting, 3Rd Edition
Author: Montgomery
Description: Time Series Analysis and Forecasting (3rd Edition) provides a comprehensive introduction to the statistical methods used for analyzing time-oriented data and making accurate forecasts. The book explains how time series techniques can be applied to predict trends and outcomes in areas such as business, economics, demographics, and data science. Updated with modern tools and examples using JMP, SAS, and R, it combines theoretical concepts with practical applications through exercises, algorithms, datasets, and programming examples. Covering both foundational and advanced forecasting methods, the book serves as a valuable resource for students, researchers, and practicing data scientists working with predictive analytics and statistical forecasting.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Principles Of Combustion, Third Edition
Author: Kirkpatrick
Description: Principles of Combustion (3rd Edition) is a comprehensive textbook on chemically reacting flow systems and combustion science, covering both fundamental theory and practical applications. The book explains key topics such as chemical kinetics, reaction mechanisms, laminar and turbulent flames, droplet combustion, and solid fuel combustion, while integrating concepts from thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, and chemistry. Updated with new material on hydrogen, renewable fuels, zero-carbon combustion, and climate-related emissions, it also includes modern application examples, end-of-chapter problems, and MATLAB-based learning tools. Designed for upper-level undergraduate and graduate students, as well as engineers and researchers, the book serves as both an educational resource and a reference for understanding real-world combustion processes and sustainable energy challenges.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Supply Chain Management: A Global Perspective, Fourth Edition Paperback With Eepub Access
Author: Sanders
Description: Supply Chain Management: A Global Perspective (4th Edition) by Nada R. Sanders provides a comprehensive introduction to the principles, strategies, and operations of modern supply chain management. The book examines key areas such as sourcing, logistics, forecasting, demand planning, operations management, and supply chain design, while emphasizing the interconnected and global nature of SCM. Updated with current business practices, emerging technologies like AI, and discussions on trade policies, tariffs, data protection, and national security, it combines theory with real-world case studies and practical examples from companies across industries. Designed for university students and business professionals, the text serves as both an academic resource and a practical guide to understanding today’s rapidly evolving global supply chains.

Subject: computer science
Title: Realizing The Metaverse - A Communications And Networking Perspective
Author: Lim
Description: Realizing the Metaverse explores the technologies, challenges, and future possibilities behind the development of the Metaverse—an immersive version of the internet where interactions occur in real time within virtual environments. The book examines key areas such as infrastructure, network access, artificial intelligence, security, augmented reality, and haptic feedback, while discussing the technical barriers that must be overcome to make large-scale Metaverse systems practical and reliable. Combining current research with discussions of future developments, the book provides one of the earliest comprehensive academic treatments of the Metaverse and is intended for students, researchers, and professionals working in wireless communication, networking, and emerging digital technologies.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Unnoticed Entrepreneur, Book 3 - How Entrepreneurs Can Leverage Their Authenticity To Build Brands
Author: James
Description: The UnNoticed Entrepreneur is a practical guide that helps entrepreneurs build brand recognition by leveraging authenticity, adaptability, and modern marketing strategies. Drawing from the experiences of 50 entrepreneurs worldwide, the book shares real-world case studies, actionable insights, and technology tools that helped businesses move from obscurity to visibility. It emphasizes genuine leadership, customization, and the integration of technology with traditional business principles, while offering easy-to-implement “strategy smoothies” at the end of each chapter. Designed for business owners seeking sustainable growth, the book provides practical lessons in marketing, branding, and public relations from people who have successfully applied them in real businesses.

Subject: computer science
Title: Microsoft Sharepoint Premium In The Real World - Bringing Practical Cloud Ai To Content Management
Author: Sanford
Description: Microsoft SharePoint Premium in the Real World: Bringing Practical Cloud AI to Content Management is a hands-on guide to using AI-powered features in Microsoft SharePoint for document management and automation. Written by experienced Microsoft AI consultants, the book explains how organizations can apply low-code and no-code approaches to classify, organize, and process documents using SharePoint Premium and Azure Cognitive Services. It covers practical applications such as forms processing, document understanding, image analysis, metadata search, and content assembly, while also demonstrating how to use both custom and pre-built AI models. Designed for developers, information managers, and business users alike, the book focuses on reducing manual document handling and improving enterprise content management through cloud-based AI solutions.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Synergy Of Bio-Chemical Processes For Photocatalytic And Photoelectrochemical Wastewater Treatment
Author: Pandey
Description: This book explores the development and applications of photocatalytic fuel cells as sustainable solutions for wastewater treatment, renewable energy production, and environmental remediation. It examines how technologies such as microbial fuel cells and photocatalysis can simultaneously treat industrial wastewater, generate electricity, and support clean hydrogen production. The text discusses advanced materials, photocatalytic and photoelectrocatalytic processes, hydrogen evolution, carbon dioxide reduction, and system optimization for improved sustainability and energy efficiency. Covering both theoretical concepts and practical applications—including process scaling, chemical synthesis, and green energy generation—the book is designed for researchers, graduate students, engineers, and environmental professionals working in sustainable chemistry, nanotechnology, fuel cells, and water treatment technologies.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Intelligent Spectrum Management - Towards 6G
Author: Iyer
Description: Intelligent Spectrum Management: Towards 6G examines advanced techniques for spectrum sharing and resource management in 5G, beyond 5G, and future 6G wireless networks. The book explores how technologies such as cognitive radio, artificial intelligence, reinforcement learning, deep learning, blockchain, and reconfigurable intelligent surfaces can improve the efficiency, security, and flexibility of wireless communication systems. It also discusses emerging 6G applications like digital twins, Industry 5.0, holographic telepresence, and extended reality (XR), along with challenges related to dynamic spectrum management and machine learning-driven optimization. Combining theoretical concepts with practical approaches such as network slicing and decentralized resource allocation, the book serves as a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, regulators, and professionals working in next-generation wireless communications.

Subject: general
Title: Automating Building Energy Management For Accelerated Building Decarbonization - System Architecture And The Network Layer
Author: Kempf
Description: Automating Building Energy Management for Accelerated Building Decarbonization provides a detailed guide to modern building energy management systems and their role in reducing energy consumption and carbon emissions. The book explains the architecture of cloud-based energy management systems, networking technologies, communication protocols, and application-layer standards such as BACnet, Zigbee, Bluetooth Low Energy, and Matter for commercial and residential buildings. It also covers API-based HVAC control, integration of renewable energy resources like solar panels and battery storage, and utility communication standards for demand response systems. With a strong emphasis on cybersecurity, the book discusses protocols such as IPsec, TLS, and OAuth2.0 for securing operational technology networks. Combining practical implementation guidance with technical depth, it serves as a valuable resource for engineers, energy managers, researchers, and professionals working in smart buildings, renewable energy, and building decarbonization.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Practical Process Design For Chemical Engineers
Author: Marchildon
Description: Practical Process Design for Chemical Engineers offers a comprehensive guide to the principles, methods, and real-world applications of modern chemical engineering process design. Covering topics such as reactors, separations, heat exchange, solids processing, mixing systems, process integration, and operational efficiency, the book combines theoretical foundations with practical industry-focused solutions. It emphasizes innovation, sustainability, environmental responsibility, and safety while exploring advanced technologies and modern engineering practices. With discussions on process intensification, solvent and reactor technologies, and project estimation techniques, the book equips students and professionals with the knowledge and practical skills needed to tackle complex challenges in chemical engineering and industrial process design.

Subject: computer science
Title: Cybersecurity In The Transportation Industry
Author: Shah
Description: This book explores the growing cybersecurity challenges facing modern transportation systems and infrastructure. It examines how industries such as shipping, trucking, automotive production, and logistics are increasingly vulnerable to cyberattacks, ransomware, and disruptions that can impact global supply chains and public safety. The text presents a risk-based cybersecurity framework that organizations can use to strengthen security management, protect communication systems, and improve resilience across dynamic vendor ecosystems. Combining practical strategies with insights into emerging threats, the book focuses on safeguarding passengers, cargo, transportation networks, and critical infrastructure while helping organizations develop stronger and more effective cybersecurity programs.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Integrated Devices For Artificial Intelligence And Vlsi - Vlsi Design, Simulation And Applications
Author: Raj
Description: This book explores the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) with Very Large-Scale Integration (VLSI) design, highlighting how AI techniques are transforming semiconductor and computer system development. It explains the role of advanced CAD tools such as Synopsys and Mentor Graphics in automating VLSI design processes, while also discussing the limitations of traditional automation approaches. The book focuses on how AI-driven methods, including expert systems and knowledge-based techniques, improve efficiency and problem-solving across various stages of VLSI design such as RTL design, physical design, and static timing analysis (STA). Combining concepts from microelectronics, semiconductor technology, and AI, the text provides insights into emerging opportunities and modern practices shaping the future of the VLSI industry.

Subject: general
Title: Microwave Photonics
Author: Yao
Description: Microwave Photonics provides a comprehensive study of the technologies and techniques that combine microwave engineering with photonics for advanced communication and signal processing applications. Covering developments from the past three decades, the book explains topics such as photonic generation, processing, and distribution of microwave and ultra-wideband (UWB) signals, beamforming for phased array antennas, microwave frequency measurement, analog-to-digital conversion, and quantum microwave photonics. It also discusses important components and concepts including optical fibers, modulators, photodetectors, fiber Bragg gratings, opto-electronic oscillators, and radio-over-fiber networks. Designed for graduate students and researchers, the book offers both theoretical foundations and practical insights into modern microwave photonic systems and their applications in communication, sensing, and signal processing technologies.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Chemical Engineering Analysis And Optimization Using Matlab
Author: Xie
Description: Chemical Engineering Analysis and Optimization Using MATLAB® introduces modern computer-aided analysis and optimization techniques for chemical engineering using MATLAB. The book explains how optimization methods are applied in engineering design and manufacturing to improve efficiency, reduce resource use, and maximize performance. It covers programming, mathematical modeling, simulation, and optimization techniques through practical examples, case studies, and MATLAB code implementations. Combining theoretical concepts with hands-on applications, the text helps students and engineering professionals develop valuable skills in process analysis, computational problem-solving, and engineering design optimization.

Subject: computer science
Title: Reinforcement Learning For Cyber Operations: Applications Of Artificial Intelligence For Penetration Testing
Author: Rahman
Description: Reinforcement Learning for Cyber Operations: Applications of Artificial Intelligence for Penetration Testing explores how reinforcement learning (RL) and artificial intelligence can be applied to modern cybersecurity operations and penetration testing. The book explains concepts such as attack path analysis, sensor placement, automated penetration testing, and network defense strategies using reinforcement learning models, Markov Decision Processes, and multi-agent techniques. Through simulated and real-world cybersecurity scenarios, it demonstrates how AI can improve both offensive and defensive cyber operations by identifying vulnerabilities, predicting adversarial behavior, and strengthening organizational security posture. Designed for cybersecurity professionals, researchers, network administrators, and information security teams, the book provides both theoretical foundations and practical applications of AI-driven cyber defense and penetration testing methodologies.

Subject: science
Title: The Advanced Practitioner In Pathophysiology And Diagnostics
Author: Phipps
Description: The Advanced Practitioner in Pathophysiology and Diagnostics is a comprehensive guide designed to help healthcare professionals develop a deeper understanding of disease processes and clinical diagnostics. The book explains how functional changes in the body occur due to illness or injury and shows how practitioners can use patient history, physical examinations, diagnostic tests, and evidence-based approaches to make informed clinical decisions. Covering topics such as differential diagnosis, pathophysiological mechanisms, and diagnostic reasoning, it provides a multidisciplinary and patient-centered perspective suitable for advanced practice healthcare professionals and postgraduate students studying advanced clinical practice and pathophysiology.

Subject: science
Title: How To Read A Paper, The Basics Of Evidence-Based Healthcare, Seventh Edition
Author: Greenhalgh
Description: How to Read a Paper (7th Edition) is a practical and accessible guide to understanding and evaluating clinical and scientific research papers in evidence-based healthcare. The book explains how research studies are designed, presented, and critically appraised, helping students and healthcare professionals build confidence in interpreting medical literature. Updated with modern topics such as big data, artificial intelligence, consensus methods, systematic reviews, meta-analyses, and qualitative research, it balances the principles of evidence-based medicine with real-world clinical practice. Designed for medical, nursing, and allied health professionals, the book serves as an essential resource for learning how to assess research quality and apply scientific evidence effectively in healthcare decision-making.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Signal Processing: An Applied Decomposition Approach
Author: Candy
Description: Signal Processing: An Applied Decomposition Approach provides a practical and mathematically guided introduction to signal decomposition techniques used in modern signal processing. The book explains how complex signals can be broken down into individual components for analysis, noise reduction, and system modeling. It covers a wide range of decomposition methods from statistical, spectral, and model-based perspectives, including techniques such as PCA, ICA, MUSIC, SSP, and Singular Spectrum Analysis. Combining theory with real-world applications, MATLAB examples, case studies, and problem sets, the text explores both time-domain and frequency-domain signal analysis, system estimation, and modal decomposition. Designed for graduate students, researchers, and engineering professionals, the book serves as a comprehensive resource for understanding and applying advanced signal processing techniques.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Smart Antennas For 5G+
Author: Sánchez-Hernánd
Description: Smart Antennas for 5G+ explores the evolution of advanced antenna technologies designed for 5G and beyond wireless communications. The book covers key concepts such as beamforming, beam-steering, MIMO systems, mmWave antennas, metasurfaces, and reconfigurable intelligent surfaces, along with 3GPP/ITU standards, RF testing, and propagation models. It also discusses modern antenna integration techniques, flexible and 3D-printed antennas, thermal management, and performance optimization for next-generation wireless networks, making it a valuable resource for students, researchers, and engineers in 5G communications.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Nanonetworks - The Future Of Communication And Computation
Author: Lau
Description: Nanonetworks: The Future of Communication and Computation provides a clear and accessible introduction to nanoscale communication and computation systems, one of the fastest-growing interdisciplinary fields in modern technology. The book explores the foundations, applications, network paradigms, machine learning integration, and security aspects of nanonetworks, while also covering their history and key theories. With practical examples and easy-to-understand explanations, it serves as an ideal resource for students, researchers, and professionals interested in the future of communication and nanotechnology.

Subject: computer science
Title: Computational Intelligence - Theory And Applications
Author: Kumar
Description: Computational Intelligence explores advanced information processing techniques inspired by human intelligence and biological systems. The book covers key computational intelligence methods such as Artificial Neural Networks, Fuzzy Logic Systems, and Genetic Algorithms, highlighting their applications in machine learning, optimization, bioinformatics, diagnostics, and manufacturing systems. It also discusses computer vision and agile manufacturing technologies that improve flexibility, efficiency, adaptability, and productivity, making the book a valuable resource for students, researchers, and professionals in artificial intelligence and modern manufacturing.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Aircraft Design - A Systems Engineering Approach, Second Edition
Author: Sadraey
Description: Aircraft Design: A Systems Engineering Approach provides a comprehensive introduction to modern aircraft engineering by integrating aerodynamics, propulsion, flight dynamics, structures, and systems engineering into a unified design process. Covering everything from conceptual to detailed aircraft design, the book explores advanced topics such as all-electric aircraft, VTOL systems, winglets, structural and spoiler design, aircraft modernization, and current FAA requirements. With a practical and holistic approach, it serves as an essential resource for aerospace students, researchers, and engineers involved in aircraft and air vehicle design.

Subject: computer science
Title: Model Optimization Methods For Efficient And Edge Ai: Federated Learning Architectures, Frameworks And Applications
Author: Chelliah
Description: Model Optimization Methods for Efficient and Edge AI provides a comprehensive overview of AI model engineering, optimization, and deployment across cloud, edge, and hybrid environments. The book covers key AI applications such as computer vision and natural language processing, while focusing on federated learning techniques and platforms like PySyft, TensorFlow Federated, and FATE. It also explores AI model compression, edge AI for IoT systems, data privacy and security, parallel processing, and protection against cyberattacks, making it a valuable resource for students, researchers, and professionals working in AI, machine learning, and edge computing.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Analysis And Design Of Analog Integrated Circuits, 6Th Edition
Author: Gray
Description: Analysis and Design of Analog Integrated Circuits provides a comprehensive and accessible introduction to analog circuit design, covering the fundamental concepts and advanced techniques used in modern integrated circuits. The book includes detailed discussions on amplifier design, frequency response, stability, noise analysis, replica biasing, pole-zero doublets, and MOS transistor noise performance. Updated with new circuit concepts and practical design methods, it serves as an essential resource for electrical and computer engineering students, researchers, and analog IC designers.

Subject: general
Title: The Economics Of Climate Resilience In Power Infrastructure
Author: Arabnya
Description: The Economics of Climate Resilience in Power Infrastructure explains how power systems can be protected and adapted against climate-related disasters such as wildfires, hurricanes, and heat waves through engineering, economic, and resilience planning strategies. The book covers risk management, climate adaptation investments, disaster operations, post-disaster recovery, microgrid applications, and resilience-focused asset management, while using practical models and numerical examples to demonstrate how utilities and infrastructure owners can improve operational and financial stability in the face of climate challenges.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Probabilistic Forecasts And Optimal Decisions
Author: Krzysztofowicz
Description: Probabilistic Forecasts and Optimal Decisions explores how mathematical probability and decision theory can be used to improve decision-making under uncertainty. The book introduces probability concepts, continuous and discrete modeling methods, and forecasting techniques that help optimize decisions when outcomes are uncertain and influenced by multiple variables. Supported by real-world case studies, mini-projects, and exercises, it provides a practical and theoretical foundation for students, engineers, scientists, and professionals working in predictive analytics and decision analytics.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Wireless Semantic Communications - Concepts, Principles, And Challenges
Author: Sun
Description: Wireless Semantic Communications introduces the emerging field of semantic communications, where only the essential meaning of information is transmitted instead of large amounts of raw data, enabling faster and more efficient wireless communication with lower data usage and reduced ambiguity. The book explains the foundations of semantic theory, semantic encoding and decoding, transceiver design, resource management, and optimization methods, while also exploring future applications and the role of semantic communication in next-generation wireless networks. It is designed for researchers, engineers, and professionals working in wireless and communication technologies.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Smart Electric And Hybrid Vehicles: Advancements In Materials, Design, Technologies, And Modeling
Author: Kumar
Description: Smart Electric and Hybrid Vehicles: Advancements in Materials, Design, Technologies, and Modeling provides a broad, interdisciplinary overview of electric and hybrid vehicle technologies, covering design, materials, modeling, and system integration from both mechanical and electrical engineering perspectives. It also discusses batteries, electric machines, power electronics, emissions reduction, regulatory and market factors, and emerging technologies like AI and digital twins, making it a comprehensive reference for engineers, researchers, and students in automotive and energy systems.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Dynamical Behaviors Of Multiweighted Complex Network Systems
Author: Wang
Description: Dynamical Behaviors of Multiweighted Complex Network Systems studies the behavior, control, and synchronization of complex dynamical and neural network systems with multiple weighted interactions, including delayed and derivative couplings. It focuses on advanced topics such as finite-time synchronization, passivity, H-infinity control, and stability under disturbances or attacks, using methods like PID and PD controllers. The book is aimed at researchers and graduate-level readers working in neural networks, complex systems, and control theory.

Subject: computer science
Title: Machine Learning And Metaheuristic Computation
Author: Cuevas

Built around Python-based implementation, it explains key concepts such as clustering, dimensionality reduction, and evolutionary algorithms like differential evolution. The book gradually guides readers from foundational ideas to advanced AI applications, making it suitable for students, researchers, and professionals interested in optimization and intelligent computation.

Subject: Engineering
Title: 3D Printing In The Healthcare Industry - Novel Applications
Author: Malviya
Description: 3D Printing in Healthcare: Medical Applications, Personalized Medicine and Surgical Innovation explores how additive manufacturing is transforming modern healthcare by enabling patient-specific solutions such as custom prosthetics, implants, surgical guides, and personalized drug delivery systems. The book covers the evolution of 3D printing, its medical applications across various diseases, and its role in surgical planning, tissue engineering, and hospital practice, while also addressing regulatory and technological challenges.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Polypropylene Cable Insulation
Author: Du
Description: Polypropylene Cable Insulation is a technical guide on the use of polypropylene as an environmentally friendly insulation material for high-voltage AC and DC power cables. It explains its dielectric properties, space charge behavior, polarization, and degradation mechanisms such as partial discharge and electrical treeing, while also presenting experimental data and applications relevant to modern power transmission systems. The book is intended for power engineers, researchers, and professionals involved in cable design, installation, and maintenance.

Subject: general
Title: Comptia Network+ Study Guide: Exam N10-009 6E

Written by experienced networking professionals, it combines practical instruction with exam preparation tools like practice questions, flashcards, and glossaries, making it suitable for aspiring network administrators and support technicians aiming to build foundational networking skills and earn industry certification.

Title: Python All-In-One For Dummies, 3Rd Edition
Author: Shovic
Description: Python All-in-One For Dummies is a beginner-to-advanced reference guide that teaches Python programming from the ground up while also serving as a practical resource for experienced developers. It covers applications such as web development, data analysis, and large-scale programming, along with modern tools like AI-assisted coding. Designed as an all-in-one handbook, it helps readers build strong coding skills and apply Python effectively in real-world projects and career development.

Title: Principles And Applications Of Blockchain Systems: How To Overcome The Cap Trilemma In Consortium Blockchain
Author: Li
Description: Principles and Applications of Blockchain Systems provides a comprehensive overview of consortium blockchain systems, covering their architecture, consensus mechanisms, and smart contract layers along with practical applications in secure storage and network management. It explains key concepts such as peer-to-peer networks, distributed consistency, Byzantine fault tolerance, and the evolution of consensus algorithms from proof-based to voting-based and hybrid models, while also addressing scalability challenges like the CAP trilemma. The book is designed for students, researchers, engineers, and professionals working in blockchain and distributed systems.

Title: Creating A Business Plan For Dummies, 2Nd Edition
Author: Curtis

It covers key steps such as validating business ideas, analyzing competition, building budgets and financial forecasts, managing risk, and designing growth strategies, while also incorporating modern tools like AI. The book is designed to make business planning simple, structured, and accessible for startups and established businesses alike.

Title: Food Coatings And Preservation Technologies
Author: Sen

It explains how biopolymer-based coatings help prevent microbial contamination, extend shelf life, and improve food stability, while also covering formulation methods, encapsulation techniques, and sustainability considerations. The book also reviews safety, regulatory, and industrial applications, making it useful for food scientists, technologists, and researchers working in food preservation and packaging innovation.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Hands-On Real Time Communications: A Practical Guide To Rtc Protocols In Non-3Gpp, 3Gpp 4G/5G/6G And Iot Networks
Author: Herrero
Description: Hands-On Real Time Communications is a practical guide to real-time communication systems, covering VoIP and modern RTC protocols used in 5G, IoT, and internet-based networks.
