Title: The Warren Buffett Way, 30Th Anniversary Edition
Author: Hagstrom

Written by Robert Hagstrom, it outlines Buffett’s core investment principles, psychological discipline, and decision-making strategies, while also including updated insights, academic references, and historical Berkshire Hathaway portfolio data. The book is aimed at investors seeking to apply Buffett’s methods to real-world investing.

Subject: computer science
Title: Internet Of Things In Bioelectronics - Emerging Technologies And Applications
Author: Murthy
Description: IoT-Enabled Bioelectronics: Applications in Healthcare and Smart Medical Systems explores how the convergence of Internet of Things (IoT) and bioelectronics is transforming healthcare through connected wearable and implantable devices. It explains how these systems enable real-time health monitoring, remote diagnostics, and personalized treatment, while also addressing data security and integration with AI, machine learning, and big data. The book is aimed at researchers, students, and professionals working in healthcare technology, IoT, and biomedical engineering.

Title: Artificial Intelligence-Based System Models In Healthcare
Author: Anand
Description: Artificial Intelligence-Based System Models in Healthcare provides a comprehensive overview of how artificial intelligence is transforming healthcare systems through advanced decision support, predictive analytics, machine learning, and medical imaging technologies. It explores the integration of AI into clinical workflows, biomedical text processing, diagnostics, and healthcare management, while also addressing future directions such as chatbots, secure health records, and intelligent patient monitoring. The book is aimed at researchers, postgraduate students, healthcare professionals, and policymakers interested in AI-driven medical innovation.

Subject: computer science
Title: Knowledge Management And Industrial Revolution 4.0 - Practices And Challenges
Author: Kumar
Description: Knowledge Management and Industrial Revolution 4.0 examines how knowledge management practices integrate with Industry 4.0 technologies to improve industrial efficiency, automation, and decision-making. It highlights the role of technologies like AI, deep learning, blockchain, and IoT in optimizing operations, enabling remote monitoring, enhancing HR systems, and improving knowledge sharing across organizations. The book also includes case studies that demonstrate how digital transformation drives innovation, reduces costs, and improves productivity across modern industries.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Busy Is A Four-Letter Word: A Guide To Achieving More By Doing Less

Description: Audacious Optimism is a motivational memoir that follows Shanna Adamic’s journey from a successful corporate career and former cheerleader to surviving a life-threatening brain tumor and ultimately making a remarkable recovery. Through her experience, she shares how resilience, willpower, and a conscious choice to remain optimistic can help people overcome extreme adversity. The book offers insights on embracing uncertainty, cultivating inner strength, and applying optimism across all areas of life. Overall, it is an inspiring story aimed at helping readers find hope and courage during difficult challenges.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Go-To-Market Cheat Code - The Secret To Unlocking B2B Growth
Author: Gray
Description: The Go-to-Market Cheat Code by Justin Gray and Josh Wagner explores how trust and relationships form the foundation of successful B2B growth strategies. The book explains why modern business success depends less on short-term tactics and more on building authentic, long-term trust with customers. It introduces practical frameworks like the “digital mask,” customer journey design, and the alignment of culture, customers, and context to improve business relationships and drive sustainable growth. Overall, it serves as a guide for executives and founders looking to scale their organizations by strengthening trust-driven go-to-market strategies.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Through Shifts And Shocks: Lessons From The Front Line Of Technology And Change
Author: Vamos
Description: Through Shifts and Shocks by Steve Vamos is a leadership guide focused on helping individuals and organizations navigate constant change and disruption. Drawing on his experience with major tech companies like Microsoft, Apple, IBM, and Xero, the book offers practical tools and real-world insights for aligning teams, strategy, and execution during periods of uncertainty. It emphasizes developing the right leadership mindset, prioritizing effectively, fostering psychological safety, and making tough decisions to drive innovation and growth. Overall, it serves as a practical playbook for leaders aiming to manage change and improve organizational performance.

Subject: Economics
Title: The China Business Conundrum: Ensure That "Win-Win" Doesn'T Mean Western Companies Lose Twice
Author: Wilcox
Description: The China Business Conundrum by Ken Wilcox is a firsthand account of the challenges Western companies face when attempting to operate in China. Based on the author’s experience leading a joint venture between Silicon Valley Bank and Chinese partners, the book explores cultural, regulatory, and political complexities that can complicate business collaborations. It highlights misunderstandings, operational difficulties, and hard-earned lessons from working within China’s business environment. Overall, it serves as a cautionary guide for executives and entrepreneurs seeking to navigate international partnerships in China with realistic expectations and better preparation.

Subject: computer science
Title: A Beginner'S Guide To Web Application Penetration Testing
Author: Abdollahi
Description: A Beginner’s Guide to Web Application Penetration Testing by Ali Abdollahi is an introductory cybersecurity book that teaches how to identify, test, and understand vulnerabilities in web applications. It follows OWASP standards and covers common security issues like broken access control, injection flaws, cryptographic weaknesses, and misconfigurations. The book also walks readers through the full penetration testing process—from reconnaissance and scanning to exploitation and reporting—using practical tools like Nmap and Sublist3r. Overall, it’s designed to help beginners build foundational skills in web application security and ethical hacking.

Subject: computer science
Title: Sql Essentials For Dummies
Author: Blum
Description: SQL Essentials For Dummies is a concise, quick-reference guide to the fundamentals of SQL, the standard language used to manage relational databases. It focuses on core concepts without unnecessary detail, making it useful for beginners, students, or professionals needing a refresher. The book covers essential skills for creating, managing, and manipulating databases, and serves as a practical desk reference for learning, exam preparation, or everyday work use.

Subject: computer science
Title: Java Essentials For Dummies
Author: Lowe
Description: Java Essentials For Dummies is a concise reference guide that covers the fundamental concepts of the Java programming language. It focuses on core skills needed to understand and use Java for building desktop, web, and mobile applications, without unnecessary detail or lengthy explanations. Designed as a quick refresher or learning aid, it helps readers strengthen coding basics, review key concepts, and get clear answers while working or studying.

Subject: computer science
Title: Data Science Essentials For Dummies
Author: Pierson
Description: Data Science Essentials For Dummies is a concise reference guide that covers the core concepts of data science, including data collection, cleaning, processing, and visualization. Designed for quick learning and review, it helps readers strengthen foundational skills without unnecessary detail. The book is ideal for students and professionals who want a practical refresher on key data science topics, whether for study, certification, or everyday work use.

Subject: computer science
Title: Artificial Integrity
Author: Mann
Description: Artificial Integrity by Hamilton Mann introduces the concept of building AI systems that prioritize integrity-led outcomes rather than just intelligence. It argues that traditional ethical frameworks are not enough for modern AI challenges and proposes a new “code of design” to ensure AI aligns with human values, cultural contexts, and societal norms. The book offers practical strategies for designing and implementing AI that enhances human decision-making while maintaining trust, fairness, and human agency. Overall, it serves as a guide for leaders, developers, and policymakers working toward responsible, human-centered AI systems.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Your Ai Roadmap: Actions To Expand Your Career, Money, And Joy
Author: Bajorek
Description: Your AI Roadmap by Dr. Joan Palmiter Bajorek is a practical career and finance guide designed to help readers navigate job uncertainty in the age of AI. It combines career strategy with personal finance and income-building advice, offering frameworks for financial literacy, goal setting, personal branding, networking, and resilience during job disruptions. Drawing on the author’s own experience in AI layoffs and building multiple income streams, the book provides actionable tools to help individuals adapt, grow, and achieve financial stability in an AI-driven economy.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Work Backwards - The Revolutionary Method To Work Smarter And Live Better
Author: Duggan
Description: Work Backwards by Tim Duggan is a practical and inspiring guide that challenges conventional thinking about work and career satisfaction. It encourages readers to rethink their approach to work-life balance by exploring global perspectives and research on happiness and productivity. The book offers actionable strategies for using flexible work models, improving wellbeing, and designing a more fulfilling career and life, helping readers move from simply coping with work to genuinely enjoying it.

Subject: computer science
Title: C++ Essentials For Dummies
Author: Mueller
Description: C++ Essentials For Dummies is a concise reference guide that covers the core concepts of the C++ programming language. It is designed for quick learning and review, helping readers understand fundamental coding concepts, refresh existing knowledge, and apply C++ in practical projects. With minimal explanations and no unnecessary detail, it serves as a handy resource for students and professionals who need a fast, reliable refresher on C++ basics for study, certification, or everyday work.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The 9% Edge Planner: A Quarterly Guide To Help You Maintain Clarity, Stay Focused, And Keep You On Track For Success
Author: Valentino
Description: The 9% Edge Yearly Planner by Candy Valentino is a practical companion to her main book that focuses on helping entrepreneurs and business leaders apply proven growth strategies in real time. It provides structured worksheets, checklists, and tools to guide users through every stage of business development—from improving revenue and customer growth to managing costs and scaling efficiently. Designed as a hands-on planning system, it helps readers turn business concepts into actionable steps for building and expanding successful enterprises.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Leading The Sustainable Business Transformation - A Playbook From Imd
Author: Binder
Description: Leading the Sustainable Business Transformation: A Playbook from IMD is a research-based guide that explains how organizations can integrate sustainability into corporate strategy while still achieving strong financial performance. Drawing on IMD research and executive case studies, it provides a practical framework for implementing environmental and social initiatives across businesses. The book addresses key challenges such as organizational barriers, performance measurement, and effective sustainability marketing, while also highlighting the role of AI and data in driving impact. Overall, it serves as a strategic playbook for leaders aiming to build sustainable, future-ready organizations.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Noise - Living And Leading When Nobody Can Focus
Author: Mccormack
Description: Noise: Living and Leading When Nobody Can Focus by Joseph McCormack explores how modern distractions—especially digital devices and constant information overload—are weakening attention spans in work and life. It offers practical techniques to help leaders and managers improve focus, reduce distractions, and prioritize effectively. Drawing on real-world examples from major organizations, the book shows how attention can be strengthened even in a hyper-connected world and how doing so improves leadership, communication, and decision-making.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Unapologetically Adhd: A Step-By-Step Framework For Everyday Planning On Your Terms
Author: Kinzer
Description: Unapologetically ADHD by Nikki Kinzer and Pete D. Wright is a practical and supportive guide designed to help people with ADHD manage planning, organization, and long-term goals in a flexible, realistic way. It explains how the ADHD brain works, including challenges like all-or-nothing thinking and rejection sensitivity, while offering coaching strategies, exercises, and tools to improve daily functioning. The book also focuses on reducing shame and building self-acceptance, helping readers create structure that works with their strengths rather than against them.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: How To Breathe While Suffocating: A Story Of Overcoming Addiction, Recovering From Trauma, And Healing My Soul
Author: Brackett
Description: How To Breathe While Suffocating by Bruce W. Brackett is an inspirational and reflective book that encourages readers to overcome feelings of hopelessness, self-doubt, and emotional struggle. Drawing from the author’s personal experiences with trauma and recovery, it combines affirmations, storytelling, and insight to help readers reconnect with purpose and resilience. The book addresses themes like loneliness, mental health, and post-crisis disconnection, ultimately guiding readers toward self-acceptance, positivity, and the motivation to keep moving forward in life.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Capitalism Created The Climate Crisis And Capitalism Will Solve It: The Market Forces Catalyzing A Climate Technology Renaissance
Author: Kawamori
Description: Capitalism Created the Climate Crisis and Capitalism Will Solve It by Kentaro Kawamori examines the relationship between free-market systems and global warming, arguing that capitalism both contributed to the climate crisis and has the potential to drive its solution. The book explores how governments, finance, and fossil fuel industries have shaped environmental outcomes, while highlighting how climate technology, innovation, and market incentives can help reverse damage. It also discusses the future role of energy systems and climate-focused stakeholders in accelerating change, positioning capitalism as a potential driver of large-scale climate solutions.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Power Of Going All-In - Secrets For Success In Business, Leadership, And Life
Author: Bornancin
Description: The Power of Going All-In by Brandon Bornancin is a leadership and business guide focused on building high-performing teams and achieving large-scale success. Drawing on the author’s entrepreneurial experience, it offers practical strategies for motivating employees, defining vision and purpose, eliminating poor leadership habits, and improving team performance through coaching and data-driven insights. The book serves as a hands-on toolkit for entrepreneurs and managers who want to strengthen leadership skills, inspire teams, and drive stronger business results.

Subject: general
Title: The Warren Buffett Way, 30Th Anniversary Edition
Author: Hagstrom
Description: The Warren Buffett Way (30th Anniversary Edition) by Robert Hagstrom is a detailed exploration of Warren Buffett’s investment philosophy and long-term wealth-building approach. It breaks down Buffett’s core principles of business-driven investing, including how to evaluate companies, build a strong portfolio, and think with a long-term mindset. The book also explores investor psychology, common mistakes to avoid, and insights from Charlie Munger’s “worldly wisdom,” along with historical Berkshire Hathaway portfolio data. Overall, it serves as a practical guide for investors who want to apply Buffett’s proven strategies to their own investing decisions.

Subject: general
Title: On A Discourse That Might Not Be A Semblance - The Seminar Of Jacques Lacan, Book Xviii
Author: Lacan
Description: This passage describes Jacques Lacan’s psychoanalytic theory of sexuality as formalized by Jacques-Alain Miller. It argues that human sexual relations are not purely biological but structured through “semblance” (social and symbolic displays) and an underlying real dimension of jouissance that differs between men and women. Lacan suggests that masculine sexuality is organized around the phallic function and symbolic display, while feminine jouissance is less fully contained within these structures, making it harder to systematize. The text ultimately points toward a formal, logical approach to sexuality—using symbolic and mathematical notation—to explain Lacan’s controversial idea that “there is no sexual relationship” in the sense of a fully harmonious, universal structure between sexes.

Subject: general
Title: First Writings
Author: Lacan
Description: The passage explains that Jacques Lacan’s early work as a psychiatrist already shows signs of his later psychoanalytic theory. His approach focused on the uniqueness of each patient case (“singularity”) and careful attention to detailed clinical observations, even when complex or difficult to follow. It also highlights early traces of his later ideas, such as using “structure” instead of developmental explanations, analyzing patients’ writings, and linking psychological symptoms with literature and creative expression.

Subject: general
Title: Distant Freedom: Essay On Chekhov
Author: Rancière
Description: Chekhov’s stories, as interpreted by Jacques Rancière, suggest that life is not fixed or repetitive—“another life is possible” always exists within ordinary moments. His fiction captures brief openings where change, freedom, or a different way of living can emerge against everyday monotony. For Rancière, literature doesn’t just convey knowledge; it reshapes how we see reality by revealing these possibilities and encouraging us to imagine and strive toward more liberated forms of existence.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Biobanks In Healthcare - From The Collection Of Biological Samples To Digital Health
Author: Arrighi
Description: Biobanks are now crucial institutions in modern healthcare, responsible for managing and governing valuable biological samples needed for research and medical innovation. With advances in biotechnology, precision medicine, and the rise of large-scale digital health data, organized access to human biological materials has become essential for identifying biomarkers, developing biological drugs, and ensuring reproducible scientific results. Biobanks in Healthcare highlights how these systems are transforming medical research by improving data quality and supporting the growth of precision medicine in the digital era.

Subject: general
Title: Thermal Properties Measurement Of Materials, 2Nd Edition
Author: Jannot
Description: Energy efficiency in industry and buildings is a major driver of the global energy transition, making it essential to use advanced insulating materials and high-performance components. This requires precise understanding and measurement of thermal properties to select the right materials and apply accurate testing methods. Thermal Properties Measurement of Materials (2nd Edition) serves as a practical guide to help users choose and implement appropriate measurement techniques for characterizing materials, making it useful for both thermal engineers and non-specialists involved in energy and materials science.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Biomedical Devices And Sensors
Author: Molimard
Description: Medical device design is a highly regulated field that sits at the intersection of engineering, healthcare, and ethics. While modern digital health tools like smartphones and wearables make body monitoring seem simple, true medical devices require strict compliance with safety, quality, and legal standards to protect human life. This book introduces biomedical students to the field by covering its historical and economic background, key design principles, and essential EU and US regulations. It also explains sensor technologies used in human health monitoring and highlights the importance of clinical evaluation and risk management. Overall, it provides a structured overview of how medical devices are responsibly designed, tested, and validated in real-world healthcare systems.

Subject: general
Title: Geopolitics And Energy Transition 2 - From One Continent To Another, Contrasting Situations
Author: Favennec
Description: The book Geopolitics and Energy Transition 2 explores how the global energy landscape is changing as countries move from fossil fuel dependence toward cleaner energy systems. It highlights the central challenge of reducing CO₂ emissions while still relying on fossil fuels, since renewable energy remains intermittent and limited in share. The book also compares regional energy situations across continents—such as North America’s resource wealth, Europe’s import dependence, Africa’s access challenges, and Asia’s rising demand—showing how geopolitics, resources, and energy transition are deeply interconnected worldwide.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Some Complex Phenomena In Fluid And Solid Mechanics
Author: Saouab
Description: Some Complex Phenomena in Fluid and Solid Mechanics brings together research themes explored by Abdelhak Ambari, focusing on areas such as rheology of complex fluids, hydrodynamic interactions, low Reynolds number flows, porous media characterization, instabilities, solid mechanics, and electrochemical metrology. The book is aimed at researchers and advanced students, offering both theoretical and experimental insights, while also serving as an accessible introduction to key methods and approaches in fluid and solid mechanics.

Subject: computer science
Title: Digital Presences Of Organizations
Author: Pinède
Description: Digital Presences of Organizations examines how organizations communicate in today’s digital ecosystem, where websites, social networks, and data-driven platforms shape how information is delivered and perceived. It highlights key challenges in creating effective online communication strategies, especially in multicultural contexts. The book explores three main areas: differentiated content and interaction strategies based on organization type and users, the cultural dimensions of local versus global communication, and the role of storytelling in building online narratives.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Design And Evaluation Of Ad Hoc Routing Protocol
Author: Wahl
Description: Design and Evaluation of Ad Hoc Routing Protocol focuses on vehicle-based ad hoc communication systems in environments without fixed network infrastructure. It explores the challenges of supporting driver assistance services under such conditions and presents a routing protocol designed by the authors as a case study. The book explains its design methodology, functional requirements, and performance evaluation, helping readers understand how such protocols work and are assessed. It is intended for beginners in protocol engineering and intelligent transport systems, while also offering useful insights and methods for more advanced researchers.

Subject: general
Title: Bioreactor Implementation In The Agro-Food Industries - Technology, Kinetics And Modelization
Author: Ghoul
Description: Bioreactor Implementation in the Agro-Food Industries explains the growing use of bioreactors in producing food ingredients under controlled, efficient, and environmentally friendly conditions. It covers key engineering principles such as thermodynamics, material and heat balances, microbial kinetics, and process optimization needed to design and run bioreactors effectively. The book is structured into theory and applications, first introducing core bioreactor concepts and then providing practical examples of producing valuable food ingredients and metabolites, making it useful for students, researchers, and industry professionals.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Emotional Intelligence 2E
Author: Hasson
Description: Emotional Intelligence (2nd Edition) by Gill Hasson explains that success and well-being depend more on how well we understand and manage emotions than on intelligence or talent alone. The book provides practical guidance on developing emotional intelligence (EQ) to improve happiness, confidence, and performance in both personal and professional life. It teaches readers how to manage stress and anxiety, communicate clearly, become more assertive, and handle workplace challenges like conflict, politics, and bullying more effectively.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Stahlschlüssel - Key to Steel, 2025 Edition
Author: V.S. Wegst
Description: Stahlschlüssel - Key to Steel, 2025 Edition is a comprehensive steel cross-reference guide that helps users identify, compare, and find equivalent steel grades and material designations worldwide. Covering over 75,000 standards and steel grades from hundreds of manufacturers, this trilingual reference (English, French, and German) is an essential resource for engineers, designers, materials specialists, and technical professionals.

Subject: Food
Title: Food Production Operations - Fourth Edition
Author: Dr Parvinder S. Bali
Description: Food Production Operations is a practical guide for aspiring chefs and hospitality professionals, covering essential kitchen operations, cooking techniques, food commodities, pastry basics, and Indian cuisine. Enriched with real-world insights and chef’s tips drawn from sixteen years of experience with Oberoi Hotels and Resorts, the book bridges the gap between culinary theory and professional kitchen practice.

Subject: Hotel Management
Title: Hotel Housekeeping
Author: G Raghubalan & Smritee Raghubalan
Description: Hotel Housekeeping: Operations and Management is a comprehensive guide to housekeeping operations and management in the hospitality industry. Fully aligned with the NCHMCT Accommodation Operations syllabus, it serves as an essential resource for students, educators, and hotel professionals, earning its reputation as the “Housekeeper’s Bible.”

Subject: Hotel Management
Title: Hotel Front Office - Third Edition
Author: Jatashankar R. Tewari
Description: Hotel Front Office is a comprehensive guide to front office operations and management, designed for hotel management students and hospitality professionals. Featuring updated industry practices, real-world examples, case studies, and project assignments, the book offers both theoretical knowledge and practical insights into the functioning of a hotel's front office department.

Subject: Food
Title: Food and Beverage Service - Third Edition
Author: R. Singaravelavan
Description: Food and Beverage Service is a comprehensive guide to food service operations, menu planning, beverages, bar management, and ancillary functions. Updated to reflect current industry practices, the book combines theoretical knowledge with practical insights, making it an essential resource for hotel management students and hospitality professionals alike.

Subject: Hotel Management
Title: Hospitality Management Series (4-Book Set)
Author: Set of 4
Description:
Build a strong foundation in hospitality education with this comprehensive four-book series covering the core operational areas of the hotel industry: Food Production Operations, Food and Beverage Service, Hotel Front Office, and Hotel Housekeeping. Designed for diploma, undergraduate, and postgraduate students of hotel management, these books combine academic rigor with practical industry insights, real-world examples, case studies, and professional best practices. Together, they provide a complete understanding of hotel operations and serve as valuable reference resources for both students and hospitality professionals.

Special Offer: Purchase the complete series and enjoy 25% discount on the entire set.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: CompTIA SecAI+ Study Guide: Exam CY0-001
Author: Mike Chapple, Fred Nwanganga
Description: Prepare for the CompTIA SecAI+ CY0-001 certification with comprehensive coverage of AI cybersecurity concepts and exam objectives. Written by cybersecurity experts Mike Chapple and Fred Nwanganga, this guide covers AI fundamentals, securing AI systems, AI-driven security techniques, and governance, risk, and compliance topics.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Rewired: McKinsey's Playbook on How Leading Companies Win with Technology and AI, 2nd Edition
Author: Eric Lamarre, Kate Smaje, Robert Levin, Alex Singla, Alexander Sukharevsky
Description: Rewired: The McKinsey Playbook on How Leading Companies Win with Technology and AI (Second Edition) is a practical guide to successful Tech & AI transformation. Featuring proven frameworks, cutting-edge AI insights, and more than 30 real-world case studies, it helps leaders build the capabilities, talent, and operating models needed to scale technology and AI initiatives and deliver measurable business value.

Subject: Chemistry
Title:
Poucher’s Perfumes, Cosmetics and Soaps — Volume 1
The Raw Materials of Perfumery
Author: W. A. Poucher
Description: First published in 1923, Poucher’s Perfumes, Cosmetics and Soaps is a renowned reference work on perfumery and cosmetic science. This edition of Volume I focuses on raw materials, presenting information in a more structured format to help readers trace the history, identification, and nomenclature of compounds. It also includes reference details such as CAS numbers where available, making it easier to identify ingredients and supporting the growing need for standardized ingredient labeling in the fragrance and cosmetics industries.

Subject: General
Title: Sustainability in Manufacturing
Author: Günther Seliger
Description: This book presents the outcomes of 12 years of research by the Collaborative Research Center (Sfb) 281 at the Technical University Berlin, focused on sustainable manufacturing and resource recovery. It explores innovative technologies, product lifecycle management, disassembly systems, and circular economy concepts aimed at improving resource productivity. Combining ecological, economic, and socio-political perspectives, the book highlights methods for sustainable product development, information systems, and recycling processes, while emphasizing the importance of global collaboration in addressing sustainability challenges.

Subject: Science
Title: Clinical Forensic Medicine
Author: Margaret M. Stark
Description: This updated edition of clinical forensic medicine provides comprehensive guidance for healthcare professionals working in general forensic and sexual offence medicine. Fully revised to reflect modern multidisciplinary practice, it covers key developments such as forensic photography, strangulation and torture assessment, bite mark injuries, Novel Psychoactive Substances, changing patterns of deaths in custody, and evolving drug-driving legislation. Written by leading experts, the book serves as an essential resource for doctors, nurses, paramedics, emergency physicians, pediatricians, and gynaecologists involved in forensic healthcare.

Subject: Science
Title: Facial-Oral Tract Therapy (F.O.T.T.)
Author: Ricki Nusser-Müller-Busch, Karin Gampp Lehmann
Description: This book provides a comprehensive guide to assessing and treating disorders of facial expression, oral movement, swallowing, breathing, voice, and speech caused by developmental and acquired neurological conditions such as stroke, trauma, and tumors. It presents the Facial-Oral Tract Therapy (F.O.T.T.) approach, developed by Kay Coombes, which emphasizes meaningful daily activities rather than isolated exercises to improve function and participation. Covering nutrition, oral hygiene, nonverbal communication, speech, tracheostomy management, and caregiver training, the book combines interdisciplinary expertise and evidence-based practice to support effective rehabilitation and patient-centered care.

Subject: Science
Title: OCT Imaging in Glaucoma
Author: Ki Ho Park, Tae-Woo Kim
Description: This book offers a practical and up-to-date guide to the use of Optical Coherence Tomography (OCT) in glaucoma diagnosis and management. Featuring high-quality clinical images of glaucoma patients, suspects, and healthy individuals, it explains how OCT findings are interpreted and applied in everyday practice. The book highlights the role of OCT in early detection, monitoring disease progression, assessing optic nerve and retinal structures, and evaluating vascular changes associated with glaucoma. It serves as a valuable resource for ophthalmologists, glaucoma specialists, and trainees seeking to enhance their understanding and use of OCT imaging.

Subject: Health
Title: The Business of Building and Managing a Healthcare Practice
Author: Neil Baum , Marc J. Kahn , Jeffery Daigrepont
Description: This book provides a practical guide to building, managing, and growing a successful healthcare practice in the modern medical landscape. Designed for physicians at all career stages, it covers essential business concepts such as accounting, contracts, financial management, and leadership, while offering guidance on starting a practice and exploring career paths including solo practice, group practice, and academic medicine. Updated with insights on technology, telemedicine, cybersecurity, and marketing, the book equips healthcare professionals with the knowledge and skills needed to navigate the business side of medicine and achieve long-term success.

Subject: Science
Title: Forensic Analysis of Fire Debris and Explosives
Author: Kenyon Evans-Nguyen, Katherine Hutches
Description: This book provides practical training in the key tools and methodologies used in forensic laboratories for the analysis of fire debris and explosives evidence. Designed for students and professionals entering forensic science or forensic chemistry, it covers both disciplines in detail while also exploring their connections with other forensic fields such as DNA and fingerprint analysis. Written by an international team of experts from academia and leading forensic agencies, the book combines real-world laboratory techniques with end-of-chapter exercises to help readers build a strong foundation in forensic chemistry and evidence analysis.

Subject: Science
Title: Monoclonal Antibodies
Author: Vincent Ossipow, Nicolas Fischer
Description: This updated edition of Monoclonal Antibodies: Methods and Protocols provides comprehensive, step-by-step guidance on the generation, expression, purification, characterization, and application of monoclonal antibodies. Covering modern techniques from antigen design and epitope mapping to quality control of purified antibodies, the book is organized into four sections addressing antibody production, purification, modification, and practical applications. Written in the Methods in Molecular Biology format, it includes detailed laboratory protocols, required materials, troubleshooting tips, and reproducible methods, making it an essential resource for researchers working in antibody development and molecular biology.

Subject: General
Title: Solid Waste Management
Author: Ramesha Chandrappa , Diganta Bhusan Das
Description: This book provides a comprehensive overview of the theory and practice of solid waste management, addressing one of the world's oldest and most pressing environmental challenges. It examines how economic growth, industrialization, technological advancements, and societal changes have influenced waste generation and management across different regions. Covering the environmental, social, economic, and public health dimensions of waste, the book highlights global approaches, challenges, and innovations in waste management. Drawing on extensive experience and relevant literature, it serves as a valuable resource for students, researchers, engineers, policymakers, and professionals involved in sustainable waste management.

Subject: Science
Title: Health Information Systems
Author: Alfred Winter , Reinhold Haux , Elske Ammenwerth , Birgit Brigl , Nils Hellrung , Franziska Jahn
Description: This book provides a comprehensive introduction to hospital and health information systems, highlighting their critical role in delivering high-quality, cost-effective healthcare. Written by leading experts in medical informatics, it explores the design, architecture, management, and organizational aspects of healthcare information systems, including the processes used to acquire, manage, and utilize clinical and administrative data. Serving as an essential resource for medical informatics professionals, the book offers valuable insights into the strategies and structures required for effective hospital information management in modern healthcare environments.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Sustainable Issues in Transportation Engineering
Author: Louay Mohammad, Ragaa Abd El-Hakim
Description: This volume of the GeoMEast 2019 proceedings presents a collection of international research and practical studies on recent advances in pavement design, modeling, performance, and sustainable infrastructure development. Addressing the challenges posed by aging transportation systems, the book highlights innovative materials, technologies, and engineering practices that support infrastructure sustainability, green energy integration, and long-term performance. It serves as a valuable resource for researchers, engineers, and policymakers focused on developing resilient and sustainable transportation infrastructure.

Subject: Science
Title: Handbook of Biometrics for Forensic Science
Author: Massimo Tistarelli, Christophe Champod
Description: This comprehensive handbook explores the emerging forensic challenges and security threats of the digital age, along with advanced computing technologies designed to address them. It examines topics such as identity verification, security system attacks, and the detection of abnormal or dangerous behavior through surveillance systems. Combining insights from forensic science and biometric technologies, the book provides an in-depth review of current methods, real-world forensic data analysis, and future developments in automated and semi-automated forensic investigations. It serves as a valuable resource for researchers, practitioners, and professionals working in digital forensics, cybersecurity, and biometric identification.

Subject: General
Title: The Digital Twin
Author: Noel Crespi, Adam T. Drobot, Roberto Minerva
Description: This book provides a comprehensive overview of Digital Twin technology, bringing together insights from ninety experts to explore its evolution, applications, and future potential. Covering concepts, enabling technologies, implementation strategies, and real-world use cases across sectors such as manufacturing, energy, transportation, communications, healthcare, smart cities, and life sciences, it demonstrates how Digital Twins can improve efficiency, reduce costs, and enhance decision-making throughout the lifecycle of products and services. Serving as both a practical guide and an academic resource, the book is valuable for executives, policymakers, technical professionals, researchers, and students interested in digital transformation and innovation.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Breakthroughs in Statistics
Author: Samuel Kotz, Norman L. Johnson
Description: This passage reflects on the value of knowledge and scientific discovery beyond immediate practical benefits. Through observations by economists and scientists, it argues that society often focuses too narrowly on personal gain, utility, and material well-being while neglecting curiosity, innovation, and the pursuit of great ideas. The text emphasizes that scientific progress and intellectual exploration are essential to human advancement and warns that a lack of enthusiasm for knowledge and higher ideals can contribute to broader societal challenges and setbacks.

Subject: General
Title: Vehicle Dynamics
Author: Dieter Schramm , Manfred Hiller , Roberto Bardini
Description: This book provides a comprehensive introduction to the fundamentals and mathematical modeling of automobile dynamics, covering a range of vehicle models from simple single-track representations to advanced three-dimensional multi-body systems. It emphasizes the development of accurate mathematical models based on real vehicles and the validation of simulation results through practical applications. The second edition includes updated corrections, additional examples of standard driving maneuvers, and a new section on the design, implementation, and use of driving simulators, reflecting their growing importance in automotive research and industry.

Subject: Economics
Title: Industrial and Labor Economics
Author: Saibal Kar , Debabrata Datta
Description: This book explores the unique characteristics of labor markets and industrial relations in developing and transition economies, addressing issues often overlooked in traditional labor economics texts focused on developed countries. It examines topics such as training decisions, labor contracts, migration, information asymmetries, and risk-sharing arrangements between workers and employers, supported by empirical evidence and modern research findings. Combining intuitive explanations with mathematical and graphical analysis, the book provides a comprehensive understanding of labor economics in the developing world and serves as a valuable resource for graduate students, researchers, and professionals in labor economics, industrial economics, and human resource management.

Subject: Science
Title: Project Management for Healthcare Informatics
Author: Susan M. Houston , Lisa Anne Bove
Description: This book introduces project management principles specifically for nurses and healthcare informatics professionals, demonstrating how project management can be applied to a wide range of nursing activities, from patient care planning and staff development to technology implementation and healthcare improvement initiatives. It explains the project management process, project life cycle phases, and the tools needed for successful project execution, using practical clinical examples and real-world case studies. Designed as both a learning resource and a professional reference, the book equips nurses, managers, educators, researchers, and informaticians with the skills needed to effectively lead and manage healthcare projects.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Engineering Design
Author: Robert Matousek
Description: This book is designed for engineering students who seek to understand and develop true design skills rather than simply replicate existing solutions. Drawing on extensive teaching experience, the author presents a clear and systematic approach to engineering design, guiding beginners through a structured problem-solving process that reduces reliance on trial and error. Focused on general mechanical engineering, the book emphasizes the principles and thought processes behind effective design while encouraging confidence, creativity, and analytical thinking. To maintain clarity and accessibility, detailed reference tables and data commonly found in engineering handbooks have been omitted.

Subject: General
Title: Optimization Models and Methods for Equilibrium Traffic Assignment
Author: Alexander Krylatov , Victor Zakharov , Tero Tuovinen
Description: This book provides an in-depth exploration of the traffic assignment problem, examining its mathematical foundations, practical significance, and formulation as a nonlinear optimization problem. It presents innovative approaches, methods, and algorithms developed by the authors, emphasizing solutions that leverage the structural characteristics and real-world meaning of traffic networks rather than relying solely on conventional optimization techniques. Combining theoretical insights with practical applications, the book serves as a valuable resource for civil engineering students, traffic engineers, researchers, and developers involved in transportation planning and traffic modeling.

Subject: General
Title: Emerging Technologies for Electric and Hybrid Vehicles
Author: Christopher H. T. Lee
Description: This book explores the latest theoretical advancements and technological innovations in electric and hybrid vehicle systems. It covers emerging topics such as advanced electric machine designs, modern control algorithms, power converter technologies, energy management strategies, enhanced charging methods, innovative energy storage solutions, and wireless power transfer systems. Combining cutting-edge research with practical insights, the book serves as a valuable resource for researchers, educators, engineers, and anyone interested in the future of electric and hybrid vehicle technology.

Subject: Science
Title: Medical Quality Management
Author: Angelo P. Giardino, Lee Ann Riesenberg, Prathibha Varkey
Description: This comprehensive textbook provides an up-to-date overview of medical quality management, combining theoretical foundations with practical strategies for improving healthcare quality and patient outcomes. Covering key topics such as quality improvement, healthcare data analytics, care coordination, utilization management, healthcare economics, accreditation, legal considerations, and ethics, it equips healthcare professionals with the knowledge needed to lead quality initiatives in modern healthcare settings. Enhanced with case studies, updated learning objectives, and insights into emerging healthcare technologies, the book serves as an essential resource for physicians, healthcare administrators, educators, students, and quality management professionals.

Subject: General
Title: Theory and Applications of Viscous Fluid Flows
Author: Radyadour Kh. Zeytounian
Description: This book provides a broad introduction to the theory and applications of viscous fluid flows, expanding on the authors’ earlier work on nonviscous fluids. It explores the behavior of real fluids through three key models: the Navier model for incompressible flows, the Navier–Stokes model for compressible viscous gases, and the Navier–Stokes–Fourier model for heat-conducting gases. Designed as a foundation for advanced study, the book combines theoretical concepts with practical applications in fluid dynamics.

Subject: Science
Title: Frontiers in Superconducting Materials
Author: Anant V. Narlikar
Description: Frontiers in Superconducting Materials provides a comprehensive overview of the latest advances in superconducting materials and related phenomena. Featuring contributions from leading international experts, the book covers key research developments in physics and materials science, offering valuable insights for researchers, industry professionals, engineers, and advanced students. It serves as an important reference for anyone interested in the science, applications, and future potential of superconductivity.

Subject: General
Title: Aviation Fuel Problems and Solutions: From Refinery to Wingtip
Author: Moshe Rabaev, Joanna M Bauldreay, Frederick Passman, Raj Shah, Liel Mazar
Description: This book is a comprehensive guide to aviation fuel management, covering fuel handling, quality control, troubleshooting, and remediation practices. Written by international experts, it examines the entire fuel lifecycle—from crude oil production to aircraft operation—addressing topics such as fuel quality, additives, storage and transportation issues, filtration, contamination, compatibility, laboratory testing, military applications, and fuel system failure analysis. The book also explores sustainable aviation fuels and their role in the transition to a low-carbon future, making it an essential reference for engineers, researchers, and aviation industry professionals.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Numerical Methods in Physics with Python
Author: Alex Gezerlis
Description: This comprehensive textbook combines Python programming, numerical methods, and physics applications to provide a practical introduction to computational physics. It covers essential techniques such as linear algebra, differential equations, optimization, interpolation, integration, Fourier transforms, machine learning, and partial differential equations, with clear explanations, derivations, and Python/NumPy code examples. Featuring over 60 complete programs, 140 new problems, and hands-on physics projects, it helps students quickly learn Python while mastering the computational tools widely used in modern physics and scientific computing.

Subject: Computer science
Title: Twenty Lectures on Algorithmic Game Theory
Author: Tim Roughgarden
Description: This concise introduction to algorithmic game theory explores the intersection of computer science and economics, focusing on how self-interested individuals interact in complex computational systems. Developed from a Stanford University course, the book explains key concepts and models used to solve problems in areas such as resource allocation, network management, auctions, and online markets. With short, focused chapters and real-world case studies on online advertising, wireless spectrum auctions, kidney exchange, and network management, it provides an accessible and practical foundation for students and newcomers to the field.

Subject: General
Title: Academic Vocabulary in Use Edition with Answers
Author: Michael McCarthy
Description: Academic Vocabulary in Use (Second Edition) is a practical guide for students who use English in academic settings. Designed for intermediate to advanced learners, it helps build essential academic vocabulary through 50 easy-to-follow units featuring clear explanations, reading passages, and practice exercises. Updated to reflect modern education, technology, and communication trends, the book includes a comprehensive answer key and pronunciation support, making it ideal for self-study, classroom learning, IELTS preparation, and university entrance exams.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Analysis and Design of Data Converters
Author: Behzad Razavi
Description: This comprehensive textbook provides a practical and intuitive introduction to data converter design, guiding readers from fundamental concepts to advanced architectures used in modern electronics. Written by Behzad Razavi, it covers key topics such as sampling circuits, comparators, DACs, flash converters, SAR and pipelined architectures, time-interleaved converters, and oversampling systems. Featuring step-by-step transistor-level designs, simulations, over 250 worked examples, and 350+ end-of-chapter problems, the book bridges theory and real-world design, making it an excellent resource for senior undergraduate students, graduate students, and practicing engineers seeking expertise in data converter analysis and design.

Subject: Economics
Title: Financial Econometrics
Author: Oliver Linton
Description: This comprehensive textbook offers a thorough introduction to financial econometrics, combining fundamental principles with modern developments in finance and econometric modeling. Written by one of the field’s leading experts, it covers key topics such as asset pricing, financial markets, and market microstructure while linking theory to real-world financial applications. Featuring worked exercises, empirical examples, and insights drawn from internationally taught courses, the book provides students in economics, finance, statistics, mathematics, and engineering with a practical and up-to-date understanding of financial econometric methods.

Subject: Science
Title: Social Dominance
Author: Jim Sidanius, Felicia Pratto
Description: This book explores the roots of social oppression and discrimination, examining why group-based inequalities such as racism, classism, and patriarchy emerge and persist across societies. Using the framework of Social Dominance Theory, it argues that humans have a tendency to create and maintain hierarchical social structures, leading to enduring patterns of intergroup conflict and inequality. Offering a cross-cultural perspective on race and ethnic relations, the book provides a comprehensive explanation of how social power operates and how group-based hierarchies are formed, reinforced, and sustained over time.

Subject: General
Title: Modern British Drama: The Twentieth Century
Author: Christopher Innes
Description: This comprehensive guide traces the development of modern British drama throughout the twentieth century, examining major playwrights, influential plays, and key theatrical movements from George Bernard Shaw to Patrick Marber and Sarah Kane. Combining literary analysis with historical context, it explores the social, political, and cultural forces that shaped British theatre while discussing important productions and their critical reception. Enhanced with chronologies, photographs, bibliographies, and reference lists, the book serves as an accessible and valuable resource for students, theatre enthusiasts, and theatre historians seeking a deeper understanding of modern British drama.

Subject: Engineering
Title: A History of Aerodynamics
Author: John D. Anderson
Description: This acclaimed book provides a unique and engaging history of aerodynamics, tracing the development of aerodynamic theory and its profound influence on the evolution of flying machines. Written by renowned aerospace historian and engineer John D. Anderson Jr., it explores the contributions of pioneering scientists, engineers, and inventors while placing their work within its historical context. Blending technical insight with historical narrative, the book bridges the gap between a textbook and a history book, making it accessible to both aviation professionals and general readers. Richly illustrated with nearly 200 figures and photographs, it offers a comprehensive understanding of how aerodynamic science shaped the development of modern aviation.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Mathematics of Public Key Cryptography
Author: Steven D. Galbraith
Description: This comprehensive text provides a rigorous introduction to the mathematical foundations of public key cryptography, making it ideal for students, researchers, and professionals in mathematics, computer science, and electrical engineering. It combines essential cryptographic concepts with the underlying mathematics, covering topics such as digital signatures, cryptographic algorithms, algebraic structures, isogenies, hyperelliptic curves, and advanced techniques like the Pollard algorithms and Maurer reduction. Enriched with historical insights, detailed proofs, worked examples, exercises, and open research problems, the book serves both as an advanced textbook and a valuable reference for deeper study in modern cryptography.

Subject: Computer science
Title: Computer Vision
Author: Simon J. D. Prince
Description: This modern and self-contained introduction to computer vision uses probabilistic models, learning, and inference as a unified framework for understanding and analyzing image data. Designed for advanced students and practitioners, it covers key topics such as machine learning, graph cuts, multiple-view geometry, camera calibration, face recognition, and object tracking, while building from fundamental probability concepts to real-world vision applications. Featuring detailed explanations of over 70 algorithms, extensive full-color illustrations, and all necessary mathematical background, the book provides both a strong theoretical foundation and practical guidance for developing computer vision systems.

Subject: Economics
Title: Grow the Pie
Author: Alex Edmans
Description: This award-winning book by Alex Edmans challenges the notion that businesses must choose between profit and social responsibility. Drawing on rigorous research and real-world examples, it argues that the most successful companies are driven by purpose—creating value for society while delivering sustainable profits for investors. The book explores how organizations can embed purpose into their core strategy, work effectively with stakeholders, and use practices such as executive compensation, shareholder activism, and share buybacks responsibly. Offering a balanced perspective on both business and society, it presents a practical framework for building companies that are both profitable and a force for good.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: High-tech Internet Start-ups in India
Author: H. S. Krishna
Description: This book examines the growth and development of high-tech start-ups, with a particular focus on the Indian entrepreneurial ecosystem and emerging economies. Combining case studies, empirical research, and practical insights, it explores the entrepreneurial, organizational, and environmental factors that influence the survival, scaling, and long-term success of technology ventures. The book clarifies the distinction between start-ups and small businesses, presents a multidimensional framework for understanding the start-up life cycle, and offers valuable guidance for entrepreneurs, researchers, and policymakers seeking to foster innovation-driven economic growth.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: The Theory of Homogeneous Turbulence
Author: G. K. Batchelor
Description: This classic work by George K. Batchelor provides a rigorous and foundational treatment of turbulent motion, one of the most important topics in fluid mechanics. Originally published in 1953, the book explores the theory of homogeneous turbulence, its mathematical representation and kinematics, as well as linear problems, turbulence decay, equilibrium theory, and the behavior of energy-containing eddies. Renowned for its depth and clarity, it remains a widely cited reference for researchers and students in fluid mechanics, atmospheric science, astrophysics, and planetary science, offering essential insights into the complex dynamics of turbulent flows.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Strategic Decisions
Author: Anna Muni, Marcel Planellas
Description: Strategic Decisions by Marcel Planellas and Ignasi M. Muni is a practical guide to effective strategic decision-making for managers, consultants, and MBA students. Built around the authors’ “Strategic Decisions Circle” framework, the book helps readers navigate the decision-making process and select the most appropriate strategic tools for different business situations. It presents 30 widely used strategy models—including Porter's Five Forces, Ansoff Matrix, Blue Ocean Strategy, and Open Innovation—with clear explanations, practical applications, and visual illustrations, making it a valuable resource for strategic planning and business analysis.

Subject: Economics
Title: Seven Deadly Economic Sins
Author: James R. Otteson
Description: This engaging book by James R. Otteson explores seven common economic fallacies that often seem convincing but can lead to poor decisions, waste, and reduced prosperity. Written in accessible, non-technical language, it introduces fundamental economic principles, connects them to important moral and social issues, and explains how wealth is created and sustained. By challenging misconceptions about economics, the book helps readers develop a clearer understanding of how sound economic thinking can contribute to greater prosperity and well-being.

Subject: Computer science
Title: Data Mining and Data Warehousing
Author: Parteek Bhatia
Description: This comprehensive textbook introduces the core concepts of data mining and data warehousing in a clear and practical manner. Covering topics such as classification, clustering, association mining, decision trees, Naïve Bayes, data marts, and operational data stores, it combines theoretical foundations with hands-on implementation using Weka and R. The book also explores advanced areas including big data analytics, relational data models, and NoSQL databases, making it an ideal resource for undergraduate students in computer science, engineering, and information technology. Practical examples, exercises, unsolved problems, and multiple-choice questions further enhance learning and understanding.

Subject: Computer science
Title: Mobile Computing Principles
Author: Reza B'Far
Description: This comprehensive guide to mobile application development focuses on the fundamental concepts, architectures, and challenges involved in building mobile software across different platforms. Written by Reza B'far, the book covers essential topics such as UML, XML, mobile user interface design, wireless connectivity, location-based services, data synchronization, security, and mobile application testing. Combining platform-independent principles with practical examples and case studies, it provides developers with both the theoretical foundation and hands-on knowledge needed to design and implement robust mobile applications.

Subject: Science
Title: Quantum Computation and Quantum Information
Author: Michael A. Nielsen Isaac L. Chuang
Description: Quantum Computation and Quantum Information by Michael A. Nielsen and Isaac L. Chuang is the definitive textbook on quantum computing and quantum information science. Widely regarded as a foundational resource in the field, it introduces the principles of quantum mechanics and computer science before exploring quantum algorithms, quantum teleportation, quantum cryptography, quantum error correction, and quantum information theory. With extensive background material, numerous figures, and hundreds of exercises, the book provides a comprehensive and accessible guide for graduate students, researchers, and anyone seeking a deep understanding of quantum technologies.

Subject: Computer science
Title: Integer Linear Programming in Computational and Systems Biology
Author: Dan Gusfield
Description: This book provides a practical introduction to Integer Linear Programming (ILP) through the lens of computational and systems biology, demonstrating how powerful optimization methods can be applied to complex biological problems. Using examples from genomics, phylogenetics, protein folding, network analysis, cancer research, ecology, DNA sequencing, and more, it teaches both the theory and practical workflow of modeling and solving biological problems with ILP. Accessible to readers with no prior background in biology, computation, or programming, the book includes hundreds of examples, exercises, and companion software, making it an excellent resource for students, researchers, and practitioners interested in biological data analysis and optimization.

Subject: Economics
Title: Managing Portfolio Credit Risk in Banks
Author: Arindam Bandyopadhyay
Description: This book provides a practical and comprehensive guide to credit risk measurement and management, focusing on the challenges faced by banks and financial institutions, particularly in emerging markets such as India. It explains the mathematical and statistical techniques used to assess portfolio credit risk, while also exploring industry best practices developed in the wake of the 2008 financial crisis. Supported by real-world case studies and data from Indian banks, the book offers valuable insights into key credit risk drivers, risk management frameworks, and strategies for maintaining financial stability and solvency.

Subject: General
Title: The Dravidian Model
Author: Kalaiyarasan A, Vijayabaskar M
Description: This book examines the unique development trajectory of Tamil Nadu, exploring how efforts to address caste-based inequalities have contributed to the state's social and economic progress. Drawing on fresh data, policy analysis, and an innovative interpretive framework, it argues that the redistribution of access to economic opportunities—rather than growth alone—has enabled more inclusive and sustained development. By linking social justice, democratization of opportunities, and economic modernization, the book offers valuable insights into regional development and inequality in the Global South, while contributing to broader debates on caste, inclusion, and development policy.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Digital Prism
Author: Mikkel Flyverbom
Description: This thought-provoking book explores how digital technologies, big data, artificial intelligence, and online platforms are reshaping privacy, transparency, and visibility in modern society. Challenging conventional views of transparency, it argues that the management of what is seen, hidden, and monitored has become a powerful force influencing individuals, organizations, and governments in a data-driven world. By examining the social, political, and organizational consequences of digital transformation, the book provides a fresh framework for understanding how technology affects governance, power, and everyday life, making it a valuable resource for students, researchers, and policymakers.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Algebraic Groups
Author: J. S. Milne
Description: This comprehensive textbook provides a modern introduction to the theory of algebraic group schemes over fields, bridging classical group theory with contemporary algebraic geometry. Covering fundamental topics such as algebraic groups, Lie algebras, finite group schemes, reductive and solvable groups, and key results like the Barsotti–Chevalley Theorem and Borel–Chevalley Structure Theory, the book develops the structure theory of algebraic groups in a clear and accessible manner. With minimal prerequisites and a focus on modern geometric methods, it serves as an excellent resource for graduate students, researchers, and non-specialists seeking a solid foundation in algebraic group theory.

Subject: General
Title: Regulating Assisted Reproductive Technologies
Author: Amel Alghrani
Description: This book explores the legal, ethical, and regulatory challenges posed by emerging reproductive technologies beyond traditional IVF. Examining innovations such as uterus transplantation, artificial wombs, and other advances in assisted reproduction, it analyzes how these technologies could transform human reproduction and raise new questions about parenthood, gestation, and medical regulation. By combining insights from law, science, and ethics, the book provides a practical and forward-looking framework for understanding how future reproductive technologies may be governed and regulated as they move from scientific possibility to reality.

Subject: History
Title: A History of the Fedon Rebellion: Encounters with Empire
Author: Kit Candlin
Description: A compelling account of the 1795 Fédon Rebellion in Grenada, revealing how this overlooked revolution challenged British colonial power and shaped Caribbean history. Through rich storytelling and historical analysis, Kit Candlin explores the people, politics, and lasting consequences of a defining moment in the Age of Revolution.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: AI Trust, Risk, and Security Management: Framework, Principles, and Practices
Author: R. Karthick Manoj, S. Senthilnathan, S. Arunmozhi Selvi, T. Ananth Kumar, S. Balamurugan
Description: A practical guide to AI Trust, Risk, and Security Management (AI TRiSM), helping organizations build secure, transparent, and responsible AI systems. Covering governance, compliance, bias mitigation, and risk management, it provides proven frameworks and real-world insights for deploying trustworthy AI in high-stakes environments.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Clever Girl Millionaire: A Guide to Redefining What’s Possible, Turning Ambitions into Actions, and Dreams into Millions
Author: Bola Sokunbi
Description: Clever Girl Millionaire is an inspiring and practical guide to wealth creation by Bola Sokunbi. Packed with actionable strategies, exercises, and financial insights, it helps readers develop a millionaire mindset, overcome setbacks, invest wisely, and build a sustainable path to long-term financial freedom.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Coaching Salespeople Into Sales Champions: A Tactical Playbook to Ignite Change, Transform People, and Achieve Results, 2nd Edition
Author: Keith Rosen
Description: The updated edition of Coaching Salespeople Into Sales Champions equips leaders with practical coaching strategies to boost sales performance, improve team engagement, and build a culture of accountability. Featuring Keith Rosen’s proven L.E.A.D.S. Coaching Framework, it offers a roadmap for leading successful teams in today's rapidly changing business environment.

Subject: Geography
Title: Cities and Agriculture
Author: Gwenn Pulliat
Description: Urban and peri-urban agriculture currently attracts considerable attention from citizens, policymakers and researchers. Perceived positively for its contributions to urban sustainability and food systems, it nonetheless faces significant challenges, including urban pressure on agricultural land, the economic viability of farms, and environmental pollution, among other concerns. Cities and Agriculture examines intra- and peri-urban agricultural spaces and the stakeholders driving them, analyzing the socio-spatial dynamics currently at play. Drawing on a variety of case studies from contrasting geographic contexts, it demonstrates how issues manifest locally yet recur globally, transcending the North-South divide. This book thus offers insights into the making of urban regions through the lens of their agricultural spaces.

Subject: Economics
Title: Climate Smart Agriculture
Author: Anitha Velu, Prasanth Aruchamy, Raghu Ramamoorthy, Rajesh Kumar Dhanaraj, Seifedine Kadry
Description: This book explores the application of deep reinforcement learning in climate-smart agriculture, providing advanced AI techniques for precision farming, yield optimization, crop management, and environmental monitoring. It also addresses key challenges in data privacy, security, and scalability, offering a roadmap for building intelligent and sustainable agricultural systems.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Computational Bioinorganics: From Description to Prediction
Author: Jean-Didier Maréchal
Description: Computational Bioinorganics: From Description to Prediction explores state-of-the-art computational methods for studying metal-containing biological systems. Covering bioinformatics, quantum chemistry, molecular dynamics, and AI-driven approaches, it provides essential insights into metalloenzymes, metallodrugs, metal-binding proteins, and predictive bioinorganic research.

Subject: Law
Title: Cyber Laws and IT Protection
Author: Harish Chander, Gagandeep Kaur
Description: A comprehensive guide to Information Technology Law, Cyber Law, and E-Commerce in India, covering the IT Act, electronic records, cybercrimes, intellectual property rights, digital evidence, and online transactions. Ideal for law, IT, and computer application students seeking a clear understanding of the legal framework governing cyberspace.

Subject: General
Title: Essential Concepts in Sociology, 4th Edition
Author: Anthony Giddens, Philip W. Sutton
Description: Essential Concepts in Sociology offers a clear and engaging introduction to the key ideas that shape sociological thought. Covering topics such as identity, capitalism, deviance, globalization, digital society, and intersectionality, this updated fourth edition provides an essential guide to understanding contemporary social life and change.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Engineering Air Quality Solutions: Measurement, Control, and Infrastructure Innovations
Author: Sneha Gautam, Ajay Taneja
Description: Engineering Air Quality Solutions explores advanced technologies and engineering strategies for monitoring, modeling, and reducing air pollution. Covering air quality sensors, AI-driven analytics, indoor air quality, smart-city systems, emissions control, and regulatory compliance, it provides practical solutions for creating healthier and more sustainable urban environments.

Subject: Science
Title: Essentials of Cognitive Neuroscience, 3rd Edition
Author: Bradley R.  Postle
Description: Essentials of Cognitive Neuroscience offers a clear and engaging introduction to how the brain supports perception, memory, decision-making, language, emotion, and consciousness. Updated with the latest computational and theoretical advances, this third edition is an essential resource for students of psychology, neuroscience, and neurology.

Subject: Health
Title: Forensic Neuroscience and Violence
Author: John Matthew Fabian
Description: Forensic Neuroscience and Violence explores how brain function, neurological disorders, and psychological factors influence violent behavior and criminal responsibility. Combining neuroscience, forensic psychology, and legal analysis, it provides practical insights into violence risk assessment, neuropsychological evaluation, expert testimony, and the application of neuroscience in the courtroom.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Generative AI for Communications Systems: Fundamentals, Applications, and Prospects
Author: Diep N. Nguyen, Dinh Thai Hoang, Octavia A. Dobre, Dusit Niyato, Petar Popovski, Nam H. Chu
Description: Generative AI for Communications Systems explores the application of generative artificial intelligence in modern communication networks. Covering network optimization, AI-driven architectures, cybersecurity, privacy, and applications across networking layers, it provides a comprehensive guide to the future of intelligent communications and networking technologies.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Generalized Fractional Brownian Motion
Author: Mounir Zili
Description: Generalized Fractional Brownian Motion introduces the Zili Generalized Fractional Brownian Motion (ZgfBm), a flexible stochastic process that extends classical fractional Gaussian models. Covering stochastic calculus, parameter estimation, generalized Itô formulas, and stochastic differential equations, it offers a comprehensive guide to advanced stochastic analysis and modeling.

Subject: Psychology
Title: Group Dynamics and Team Interventions: Understanding and Improving Team Performance, 2nd Edition
Author: Timothy M. Franz
Description: Group Dynamics and Team Interventions combines theory, research, and practical tools to help readers assess and improve team effectiveness. Covering team performance, culture, inclusion, assessment methods, and intervention strategies, this second edition provides an evidence-based framework for developing high-performing teams and organizations.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Growth Engineering: How to Build Systems That Drive Product Success in an AI-Driven World
Author: Rita Okonkwo
Description: Growth Engineering shows how technical decisions, experimentation frameworks, and data-driven systems can accelerate product growth and user engagement. Covering A/B testing, feature rollouts, analytics, scalable data pipelines, and growth-focused engineering practices, it offers a practical guide for building successful software products in the AI era.

Subject: General
Title: Good Girl, Goodbye: Rewrite Your Story, Reclaim Your Power, Build a Life You Love
Author: Caitlin Judd
Description: Good Girl, Goodbye helps women overcome people-pleasing, perfectionism, and self-limiting patterns. Through practical tools, positive psychology insights, and a transformative framework for change, Caitlin Judd empowers readers to set boundaries, embrace their strengths, and create a life defined by their own version of success.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Harvesting Data: Blockchain, AI and Advanced Innovations in Agriculture
Author: Narayanan Ganesh, Kanak Kalita
Description: Blockchain for Sustainable Agriculture examines how blockchain technology can enhance transparency, traceability, and efficiency in agricultural systems. Covering food safety, supply chain management, smart contracts, fair trade, and market access, it provides practical insights into building a more sustainable and profitable future for agriculture.

Subject: Emerging Technologies
Title: Hydrogen Energy Systems: Advancing Sustainable Power Solutions
Author: Krishan Arora, Himanshu Sharma, Suman Lata Tripathi, Sandesh S. Chougule
Description: Green Energy: Technologies, Innovations, and Sustainable Transitions explores the renewable energy solutions shaping a sustainable future. Covering solar, wind, hydro, geothermal, biomass, energy storage, smart grids, and energy efficiency, it provides practical insights into the technologies, policies, and innovations driving the global clean energy transition.

Subject: Emerging Technologies
Title: Hydrogen Technologies in Decarbonizing Industries and Energy Systems
Author: Vikas Khare, Sanjeet K. Dwivedi, Monica Bhatia, K. K. Pant
Description: Hydrogen Technologies in Decarbonizing Industries and Energy Systems explores the transformative role of green hydrogen in reducing carbon emissions across industry and energy sectors. Covering hydrogen production, storage, transportation, industrial applications, policy frameworks, and economic impacts, it provides a comprehensive guide to advancing sustainable energy and industrial decarbonization.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: How to Do More with Less: Future-Proofing Yourself in an AI-driven Economy
Author: Sharon Gai
Description: This practical guide helps readers understand how artificial intelligence is transforming the workplace and future careers. Combining real-world examples with actionable strategies, it shows professionals, leaders, and parents how to adapt, develop future-ready skills, leverage AI effectively, and thrive in an increasingly AI-driven world.

Subject: Physics
Title: High Energy Physics Essentials: A Guide to HEP Research
Author: Christopher Aubin
Description: High Energy Physics Essentials: A Guide to HEP Research prepares students for theoretical particle physics research by covering the foundations of relativity, quantum mechanics, electrodynamics, quantum field theory, and quantum chromodynamics. With practical problem sets and a research-focused approach, it helps bridge the transition from coursework to active participation in high-energy physics research.

Subject: general
Title: Language, Madness, and Desire: On Literature
Author: Michel Foucault
Description: This volume collects previously unpublished lectures and talks in which Michel Foucault explores the relationship between literature, language, madness, truth, and desire.
