Featuring discussions of major literary figures and themes, it offers valuable insights into a lesser-known but significant dimension of Foucault’s thought and intellectual development.

Subject: Education, Literacy
Title: Literacies in the Age of AI: Teaching and Learning in the Digital Era
Author: Gabriela C. Zapata, Bill Cope, Mary Kalantzis
Description: Literacies in the Age of AI explores how digital technologies and generative AI are transforming literacy, teaching, and learning. Combining theory, practical strategies, and ethical considerations, it helps educators develop critical, creative, and inclusive learning environments for the digital era.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Linear and Nonlinear Methods of Matrix Structural Analysis
Author: Ann E. Jeffers
Description: Linear and Nonlinear Methods of Matrix Structural Analysis offers a practical guide to modern structural analysis techniques, covering both linear and nonlinear methods for analyzing trusses, beams, frames, and complex structural systems. Featuring computational approaches, worked examples, and advanced topics, it is an essential resource for structural and civil engineering students and professionals.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Mathematics in Cybersecurity
Author: Alfred Basta, Stephan Delong, Stavros Basta
Description: Mathematics in Cybersecurity introduces the essential mathematical concepts behind modern cybersecurity, including logic, number systems, matrices, probability, graph theory, and cryptography. With practical examples and real-world applications, it helps learners build strong analytical skills for solving cybersecurity challenges.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Machine Learning: A Concise Introduction, 2nd Edition
Author: Steven W. Knox
Description: Machine Learning offers a comprehensive introduction to core concepts, algorithms, and applications in machine learning. Covering classification, regression, clustering, and model evaluation, it combines theory with practical Python and R examples to support both students and practitioners.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Machine-Learning-Based Hyperspectral Image Processing
Author: Bing Zhang
Description: Machine-Learning-Based Hyperspectral Image Processing explores advanced machine learning techniques for hyperspectral data analysis, covering denoising, classification, unmixing, target detection, and change detection. It provides both theoretical foundations and practical insights for researchers and students in remote sensing and image processing.

Subject: Psychology
Title: Collected Papers of the Pioneer of Constructivist Psychology: George A. Kelly
Author: David A. Winter, Nick Reed
Description: This collected volume presents the key writings of George Alexander Kelly, founder of personal construct theory, covering his work in psychology, psychotherapy, education, and human behavior. It provides essential insight into constructivist psychology and Kelly’s influential “person as scientist” framework.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: Natural Surfactants: Science, Technology, and Industrial Aspects
Author: Guido Bognolo, Krister Holmberg
Description: Natural Surfactants: Science, Technology, and Industrial Aspects explores the production, properties, and applications of biosurfactants from microorganisms and natural sources. Covering industrial uses, analytical methods, and future opportunities, it is a key reference for researchers and industry professionals in surfactant science and biotechnology.

Subject: Agriculture
Title: Next-Generation Fertilizers for Sustainable Agricultural Practices and Food Security
Author: Shah Fahad, Shah Saud, Taufiq Nawaz, Sajid Fiaz, Iqbal Munir
Description: Next-Generation Fertilizers for Sustainable Agricultural Practices and Food Security examines advanced fertilizer technologies such as controlled-release systems, bio-based formulations, and precision nutrient delivery. It highlights innovations that improve crop yield, soil health, and environmental sustainability while supporting global food security.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: J.K. Lasser's New Rules for Estate, Retirement, and Tax Planning, 7th Edition
Author: Stewart H. Welch III
Description: New Rules for Estate, Retirement, and Tax Planning is a comprehensive guide to managing wealth, taxes, and inheritance. Covering wills, trusts, retirement planning, and recent tax law updates, it offers practical strategies to help individuals protect assets and secure their financial legacy.

Subject: Education, Literacy
Title: Practice Learning Through Simulation: A Guide for Nursing Academics and Facilitators
Author: Nicole Pollock
Description: Practice Learning Through Simulation provides practical guidance for integrating simulation-based learning into nursing education. Covering scenario design, debriefing, virtual reality, teamwork, and experiential learning, it helps educators develop students’ clinical skills, critical thinking, and confidence in realistic healthcare settings.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Pioneers in AI: OpenAI and the Builders of Artificial Intelligence
Author: Miquel Noguer Alonso
Description: Pioneers in AI: OpenAI and the Builders of Artificial Intelligence chronicles the rise of OpenAI and the innovators behind its success. Combining technical insights, biographies, and industry analysis, it explores the breakthroughs, challenges, and societal impact of modern artificial intelligence.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Database Management Systems
Author: Narang, Rajesh
Description: Master the foundations and advanced concepts of Relational Database Management Systems with confidence. Designed for undergraduate and postgraduate students in Computer Science, Information Technology, MCA, MSc (IT), and MSc (Computer Science), this enhanced second edition presents database concepts in a clear, structured, and practical manner. Featuring SQL implementations using Oracle, MS SQL Server, and Sybase, the book bridges theoretical understanding with real-world applications. From Entity-Relationship modeling, normalization, relational algebra, and database architectures to cutting-edge topics such as partitioning, replication, mirroring, security, auditing, and high availability, it offers a complete learning resource for the next generation of database professionals. Recommended by Guwahati University, Assam, this text serves as both an academic companion and a practical reference for modern database technologies.

Subject: General
Title: Design Thinking With Problem-Solving Techniques
Author: Narassima, M.S. Vasudevan, Shriram K. Pulari, Sini Raj
Description: This book provides a practical and comprehensive introduction to Design Thinking, a human-centred approach to innovation and problem-solving. Covering the core stages of Empathise, Define, Ideate, Prototype, and Test, it equips readers with essential tools, frameworks, and techniques for developing creative, user-focused solutions. Featuring real-world case studies from leading global organisations, the book demonstrates how empathy, collaboration, and iterative thinking can drive meaningful innovation. Ideal for students, professionals, and entrepreneurs, it offers actionable guidance for solving complex challenges and creating lasting impact.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Discrete Mathematical Structures
Author: Somasundaram, R. M.
Description: Discrete Mathematical Structures provides a comprehensive introduction to the fundamental concepts of discrete mathematics essential for computer science and engineering students. Covering topics such as logic, set theory, recursion, combinatorics, recurrence relations, algebraic structures, graphs, trees, and automata theory, the book combines clear explanations, worked examples, and practical exercises to build strong analytical and problem-solving skills. It serves as an ideal foundation for understanding the mathematical principles underlying modern computing and information systems.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Data Communications And Computer Networks
Author: Gupta, Prakash C.
Description: This comprehensive text provides a clear and practical understanding of Data Communications and Computer Networks, covering concepts from data transmission fundamentals to modern networking technologies and applications. Updated with the latest developments, it explores key topics such as IPv6, MPLS, Wireless LANs, Network Security, Quality of Service (QoS), Gigabit Ethernet, IPSec, SSL, and Multicasting. Richly illustrated with examples and standard protocols, the book effectively bridges theory and practice, making it an invaluable resource for students of Engineering, Computer Science, IT, and Computer Applications, as well as networking professionals seeking a strong foundation in contemporary communication networks.

Subject: Mathematics & Statistics
Title: Gas Dynamics7Th Edition
Author: Rathakrishnan, E
Description: This revised and updated seventh edition offers a comprehensive yet accessible introduction to Gas Dynamics and Compressible Flow, emphasizing fundamental concepts, physical principles, and problem-solving techniques. Covering topics such as supersonic flows, gas dynamic processes, high-speed aerodynamics, flow visualization, rarefied gas dynamics, and high-temperature gas dynamics, the book bridges theory and practical applications through clear explanations, illustrations, and worked examples. Enhanced with new content on the Compressible Bernoulli Equation, additional examples, and exercise problems, it serves as an essential resource for undergraduate and postgraduate students, as well as engineers and researchers working in aerospace, mechanical, and related fields.

Subject: General
Title: Earthquake Resistant Design Of Structures
Author: Agarwal, Pankaj,Shrikhande, Manish
Description: This comprehensive text provides a clear and practical introduction to the principles of Earthquake Resistant Design of Structures. Covering topics from structural dynamics fundamentals to seismic analysis, design codes, evaluation, and retrofitting of reinforced concrete and masonry buildings, the book adopts a systematic step-by-step approach for effective learning. Supported by numerous diagrams and solved examples, it helps readers understand and apply seismic design concepts in real-world situations. Ideal for civil engineering students, practicing engineers, architects, and educators, the book serves as a valuable resource for designing safer and more resilient structures in earthquake-prone regions.

Subject: General
Title: Electronic Devices And Circuits.
Author: Nagrath, I. J.
Description: This comprehensive text provides a clear and practical introduction to Earthquake Resistant Design of Structures, covering structural dynamics, seismic analysis, design principles, seismic codes, and retrofitting techniques for reinforced concrete and masonry buildings. Richly supported with diagrams and solved examples, the book bridges theoretical concepts with practical applications, enabling readers to effectively understand and implement seismic design practices. Ideal for undergraduate and postgraduate civil engineering students, as well as practicing engineers, architects, and educators, it serves as a valuable guide for designing safe and resilient structures in earthquake-prone regions.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Indian Models Of Economy, Business And Management
Author: Kanagasabapathi, P.
Description: This extensively researched and updated third edition offers a comprehensive analysis of economic, business, and management models from an Indian perspective. Drawing on field studies, empirical research, and authoritative national and international sources, the book examines India's historical strengths, contemporary challenges, and future potential in the global economy. By comparing Indian models with leading international approaches, it provides valuable insights into policy, management practices, and economic development. Ideal for postgraduate students of Management, Economics, and Commerce, as well as professionals and researchers, the book serves as a thought-provoking guide to understanding India's evolving economic and business landscape.

Subject: Law
Title: Introduction To The Constitution Of India
Author: Sharma, Brij Kishore
Description: This comprehensive and student-friendly text provides a clear understanding of the Indian Constitution, its framework, fundamental principles, and key constitutional provisions. Written in simple and accessible language, it incorporates important constitutional developments, leading cases, and landmark judicial decisions, making complex legal concepts easy to grasp. Designed for LL.B. and LL.M. students, as well as aspirants of Judicial Services and Civil Services examinations, the book serves as a reliable resource for both objective and descriptive preparation, offering a strong foundation for academic success and competitive examinations.

Subject: General
Title: Intellectual Property Rights
Author: Pandey, Neeraj,Dharni, Khushdeep
Description: This comprehensive text provides a clear and practical understanding of Intellectual Property Rights (IPR) and their growing significance in today's knowledge-driven economy. Covering key forms of intellectual property, including patents, copyrights, trademarks, industrial designs, integrated circuits, and geographical indications, the book explains the legal frameworks, protection mechanisms, and regulatory procedures associated with each. It also explores creativity, innovation, commercialization, and the strategic management of intellectual property, supported by analytical case studies for practical insight. Ideal for undergraduate and postgraduate students of management, technology, and law, the book serves as a valuable guide to understanding and managing intellectual assets effectively.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Fundamentals Of Computer
Author: V.Rajaraman
Description: This extensively revised sixth edition provides a clear and comprehensive introduction to the fundamentals of computer systems, covering both hardware and software concepts. From data representation, input/output devices, memory organization, and processor operations to programming languages, operating systems, and communication protocols, the book explains core concepts in an easy-to-understand manner. Updated with contemporary topics such as wireless communication, WiFi, WiMAX, multimedia processing, peer-to-peer computing, grid computing, and cloud computing, it offers a balanced blend of foundational knowledge and emerging technologies. Ideal for students of Computer Applications, Engineering, Computer Science, Management, and working professionals, the book serves as a valuable guide to understanding modern computing systems.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Microsoft Excel 2019 Data Analysis and Business Modeling
Author: Wayne L.Winston
Description: This practical and hands-on guide helps readers master business modeling, data analysis, and decision-making using Microsoft Excel 2019 and Office 365. Through real-world scenarios and over 800 practice problems, the book demonstrates how to transform data into meaningful business insights using powerful Excel tools such as Power Query, Power Pivot, Solver, Pivot Tables, forecasting, regression analysis, Monte Carlo simulation, and advanced charting techniques. Covering both foundational and advanced analytics concepts, it equips students, analysts, managers, and business professionals with the skills needed to solve complex problems, optimize decisions, and drive data-informed results in today's competitive business environment.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Control Systems
Author: Kumar, A. Anand
Description: This comprehensive and student-friendly text provides a clear understanding of the fundamentals and principles of Control Systems. Designed for undergraduate engineering students, it offers a balanced treatment of theory and applications, helping readers develop a strong understanding of system behaviour and the control of continuous-time systems. The second edition presents concepts in a simple and accessible manner, supported by numerous classroom-tested solved and unsolved problems that reinforce learning and problem-solving skills. Ideal for students of electronics, electrical, telecommunication, instrumentation, mechanical, and biomedical engineering, the book also serves as a valuable resource for self-study and competitive examinations.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Computer–Based Industrial Control
Author: Kant, Krishna
Description: This comprehensive second edition provides a thorough introduction to computer-based control of industrial processes, covering the principles, technologies, and architectures used in modern automation systems. The book explores classical and advanced control techniques, including adaptive control, self-tuning control, expert systems, and fuzzy logic, along with key components such as sensors, actuators, SCADA systems, programmable controllers, and distributed control systems. Featuring real-time operating systems, industrial case studies, and contemporary technological developments, it offers both theoretical foundations and practical insights. Ideal for students, professionals, and researchers in instrumentation and electronics engineering, the book serves as a valuable resource for understanding modern industrial automation and process control.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Classic Data Structures
Author: Samanta, Debasis
Description: This revised second edition provides a clear and comprehensive introduction to Data Structures, designed specifically for undergraduate engineering students. Maintaining a strong focus on readability and conceptual understanding, the book presents fundamental data structures and algorithms in a simple, systematic manner. With improved explanations and enhanced algorithmic discussions, it helps students develop a solid foundation in modern programming techniques and problem-solving. Covering all major data structures used in contemporary software development, the book serves as an essential resource for learning efficient data organization, storage, and retrieval methods.

Subject: General
Title: Communication Skills For Professionals
Author: Konar, Nira
Description: Communication Skills for Professionals is a practical and comprehensive guide designed to help students, academicians, and working professionals develop effective communication skills for academic, professional, and personal success. Covering the essential aspects of verbal, written, interpersonal, and professional communication, the book equips readers with the confidence and competence needed to communicate clearly, collaborate effectively, and excel in today's competitive environment. With its practical approach and easy-to-understand style, it serves as a valuable resource for enhancing workplace effectiveness and career growth.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Big Data Analytics
Author: Kulkarni,  ParagJoshi, Sarang Brown, Meta S.
Description: This comprehensive text explores the principles and applications of Unstructured Data Mining and Big Data Analytics, with a strong focus on machine learning techniques and data-driven decision-making. Covering key topics such as data mining models, clustering, incremental learning, multi-label association, knowledge representation, and business analytics, the book provides a practical understanding of how valuable insights can be extracted from large and complex datasets. Designed for senior undergraduate and postgraduate students of Computer Science and Engineering, it serves as an essential resource for understanding modern data analytics and emerging research opportunities in the field.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Cryptography And Network Security
Author: Gupta, Prakash C.
Description: This comprehensive and self-contained text provides a thorough introduction to the principles and practices of Network Security. Designed for undergraduate and postgraduate students of Computer Science, Information Technology, and Master of Computer Applications (MCA) programs, the book covers essential concepts, techniques, and security mechanisms in a structured and accessible manner. Organized into well-defined sections and supported by numerous examples, illustrations, and end-of-chapter exercises, it helps readers build a strong conceptual foundation while reinforcing learning through practical problem-solving. An ideal academic resource, the book serves as a valuable guide to understanding and implementing modern network security practices.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Data Structures In C
Author: Nair, Achuthsankar S. Mahalekshmi, T.
Description: This student-friendly text provides a comprehensive introduction to Data Structures using the C programming language. Covering fundamental and user-defined data structures such as arrays, linked lists, stacks, queues, trees, graphs, and files, the book combines clear explanations with practical implementations and memory-based illustrations. It explores both static and dynamic data structures, graph traversal techniques, binary tree operations, and efficient searching and sorting algorithms through visual examples and programming applications. Ideal for undergraduate students of Computer Science and Information Technology, the book serves as a practical guide to mastering data organization and algorithmic problem-solving.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Cloud Computing
Author: Rao, M.N.
Description: This introductory textbook provides a clear and comprehensive understanding of Cloud Computing, covering its fundamental concepts, architectures, service models, and deployment strategies. Designed for undergraduate and postgraduate students of Computer Science and Computer Applications, the book presents cloud technologies in a simple and structured manner, combining theoretical foundations with practical insights. It examines various cloud service providers, their advantages and limitations, and explores modern cloud services and architectures widely used in industry today. An ideal academic resource, the book equips readers with the knowledge required to understand and effectively utilize contemporary cloud computing technologies.

Subject: Engineering
Title: A Textbook Of Chemical Engineering Thermodynamics
Author: Narayanan, K. V.
Description: This comprehensive and classroom-tested second edition provides an in-depth understanding of Chemical Engineering Thermodynamics, combining fundamental principles with practical engineering applications. Covering the laws of thermodynamics, thermodynamic property relationships, solution thermodynamics, phase equilibria, and chemical reaction equilibria, the book presents complex concepts in a clear and systematic manner. Enriched with numerous illustrations, over 200 worked examples, 400+ practice problems, and objective-type questions, it helps students develop strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Ideal for undergraduate students of Chemical Engineering and related disciplines, the book serves as a valuable resource for mastering both the theory and application of thermodynamics in engineering practice.

Subject: General
Title: Managing Innovations And New Product Development : Concepts And Cases
Author: Chaturvedi, Mukesh Kumar , Aseem Rahul , Manmohan
Description: This comprehensive book explores the principles of innovation and new product development, demonstrating how creative ideas can be transformed into successful market offerings. It presents a structured framework for product development, covering key concepts, processes, and innovation strategies applicable to organizations of all sizes. The book highlights contemporary innovation theories, particularly in the service sector, and introduces practical tools such as sales estimation models for new products. Enriched with real-world case studies from the Indian market, it provides valuable insights into enhancing product value, fostering innovation, and achieving sustainable competitive advantage. Ideal for students, entrepreneurs, managers, and professionals interested in innovation and product strategy.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Materials Science And Engineering : A First Course
Author: Raghavan, V.
Description: This widely adopted sixth edition offers a comprehensive and accessible introduction to Materials Science, covering the fundamental principles that govern the structure, properties, and performance of engineering materials. The book explores key topics such as crystal structures, phase diagrams, diffusion, phase transformations, mechanical behaviour, electrical and magnetic properties, semiconductors, superconductivity, and dielectric materials. Updated with a dedicated chapter on Nanomaterials, it highlights recent advancements in this rapidly evolving field. Supported by clear explanations, illustrative diagrams, worked examples, and practical applications, the book serves as an invaluable resource for undergraduate engineering students and postgraduate learners in Physics, Chemistry, and Materials Science.

Subject: General
Title: Operations Research
Author: Panneerselvam, R.
Description: This comprehensive third edition provides a thorough understanding of Operations Research as a powerful tool for decision-making, optimization, and productivity improvement. Covering both theoretical foundations and practical applications, the book explores key techniques such as linear programming, transportation and assignment models, queuing theory, inventory control, project management, game theory, decision theory, network analysis, dynamic programming, nonlinear programming, and goal programming. Enriched with carefully designed numerical examples, it simplifies complex mathematical concepts and demonstrates their relevance to real-world managerial challenges. Ideal for students, researchers, and professionals, the book serves as a valuable resource for mastering analytical and optimization techniques in modern decision support systems.

Subject: General
Title: Introduction To Indian Knowledge System : Concepts And Applications
Author: Mahadevan, B. Bhat, Vinayak Rajat Nagendra Pavana R.N.
Description: This comprehensive textbook provides a structured introduction to the Indian Knowledge System (IKS), aligning with the requirements of AICTE and the National Education Policy (NEP). Designed primarily for students of Engineering and Science, it explores the foundational concepts, epistemology, and ontology of IKS while demonstrating their relevance to contemporary academic disciplines. The book presents applications of traditional Indian knowledge across science, engineering, medicine, management, and liberal arts, enabling students to appreciate its historical significance and modern-day relevance. Supported by pedagogical features and a companion website, it serves as an engaging and accessible resource for understanding India's rich intellectual and knowledge traditions.

Subject: Science
Title: Fundamentals Of Tribology
Author: Basu, S.K. Sengupta, S. N. Ahuja, B. B.
Description: This comprehensive and student-friendly text provides a clear understanding of the fundamental principles of Tribology, covering surface behaviour, contact phenomena, friction, lubrication, and wear. The book explains the characterization, measurement, and standardization of surface irregularities and their impact on tribological performance, while also discussing various lubricants and their selection for engineering applications. Enriched with real-world industrial insights, worked examples, and practice exercises, it effectively bridges theory and practical application. Ideal for undergraduate and postgraduate students of Mechanical, Production, and Design Engineering, the book also serves as a valuable reference for practicing engineers seeking to enhance their understanding of tribological systems.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Financial Markets, Institutions, And Financial Services
Author: Gomez, Clifford
Description: This book provides a concise yet comprehensive overview of financial markets, institutions, and financial services. Designed for students, professionals, and finance enthusiasts, it explores the key components of the modern financial system and explains their growing importance in an era shaped by liberalization, globalization, and rising consumer spending.

Subject: Management
Title: Production And Operations Management
Author: Panneerselvam, R.
Description: This comprehensive third edition provides the latest concepts and techniques in production and operations management, helping students and professionals improve productivity and profitability. Covering key topics such as capacity planning, vendor evaluation, quality function deployment, enterprise resource planning, and contract laws, the book combines theory with practical applications, making it an essential resource for management and engineering students as well as industry practitioners.

Subject: Management
Title: Management : Text And Cases
Author: Satya Raju, R. Parthasarathy, A.
Description: This comprehensive third edition offers a clear and practical understanding of management principles and practices, enriched with real-life examples and case studies. Covering topics from the evolution of management and communication to modern quality improvement tools such as Quality Management Systems (QMS) and Six Sigma, the book serves as a valuable resource for students, educators, and professionals seeking to enhance organizational performance and managerial effectiveness.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Mechanism And Machine Theory
Author: Ambekar A.G.
Description: This comprehensive text on kinematics and mechanism design is tailored for undergraduate and postgraduate engineering students. Combining conceptual clarity with practical problem-solving, it covers both traditional graphical methods and modern analytical approaches, including mechanism synthesis, function and path generation, and kinematic chain analysis. Richly illustrated with solved examples and practice problems, it is also an excellent reference for GATE and Engineering Services Examination (IES) aspirants.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Mechanical Workshop Practice
Author: John, K. C.
Description: Designed for first-year engineering students, this book provides a clear introduction to workshop practice and manufacturing processes. It covers engineering materials, tools, equipment, machining operations, metal joining, heat treatment, and essential hand-working practices such as fitting, welding, carpentry, foundry, and blacksmithy. With a strong emphasis on safety, practical skills, and workshop procedures, it serves as a comprehensive guide for developing fundamental engineering workshop competencies.

Subject: Research
Title: Research Methodology
Author: Panneerselvam, R.
Description: This comprehensive second edition provides a thorough understanding of research methodology concepts and statistical tools essential for academic and professional research. Designed for MBA, MCom, MA, PhD scholars, and researchers, it serves as both a textbook and a practical reference for conducting research projects, consultancy assignments, and data-driven studies across various disciplines.

Subject: Marketing
Title: Services Marketing : The Indian Context
Author: Srinivasan, R.
Description: This updated fourth edition provides a comprehensive understanding of services marketing in today's dynamic and globalized business environment. Featuring contemporary concepts, recent industry trends, and Indian case studies from hospitality, tourism, aviation, finance, and IT sectors, the book bridges theory and practice. It is an invaluable resource for management students, aspiring marketers, and practicing professionals seeking to master modern services marketing strategies.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Theory Of Computer Science : Automata, Languages And Computation
Author: Mishra, K.L.P. Chandrasekaran, N.
Description: This well-established third edition offers a comprehensive introduction to theoretical computer science, covering automata theory, formal languages, computability, and computational complexity. With clear explanations and essential mathematical foundations, it provides students with a cohesive understanding of the core concepts that underpin modern computer science. Ideal for undergraduate and postgraduate computer science courses.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Web Engineering
Author: Chopra, Rajiv
Description: This comprehensive guide to Web Engineering covers the complete web development life cycle, from analysis and design to coding, testing, and maintenance. Written in a clear and accessible style, it introduces key web technologies, client-side and server-side programming, security concepts, and scripting languages such as JavaScript and VBScript. Ideal for undergraduate and postgraduate students of Computer Science, IT, MCA, and related disciplines, the book combines theoretical concepts with practical web application development.

Subject: Engineering
Title: Textbook Of Mechanical Vibrations
Author: Dukkipati, V. Rao,Srinivas, J.
Description: This comprehensive second edition provides a clear and practical introduction to the theory of mechanical vibrations, covering both its mathematical foundations and physical principles. Featuring a new chapter on nonlinear vibrations, the book explains analytical techniques, vibration models, and solution methods through numerous solved and unsolved problems. It is an ideal resource for undergraduate and postgraduate students of mechanical engineering seeking a strong understanding of vibration analysis and applications.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Python Programming : Basics To Machine Learning
Author: Kumari, Lalita,Shyam, Radhey
Description: This comprehensive textbook provides a structured and practical introduction to Python programming, guiding readers from fundamental concepts to advanced applications in machine learning. Covering topics such as data types, control structures, functions, file handling, object-oriented programming, modules, data visualization, and machine learning techniques, the book equips students with the skills needed to develop robust Python applications and implement intelligent data-driven solutions. Ideal for undergraduate and postgraduate students of Computer Science and Information Technology.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Software Quality & Testing : A Concise Study
Author: Sa Kelkar
Description: This concise and practical guide explores the core principles of software quality and software testing, combining theoretical foundations with real-world applications. Covering quality assurance throughout the software development life cycle, the book focuses on error prevention, detection, correction, and tolerance strategies to help develop reliable and high-quality software systems. It is an ideal resource for students of Computer Science, Information Technology, and MCA, as well as professionals seeking a solid understanding of software testing and quality management.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: So You Want to Start a Hedge Fund: Lessons for Managers and Allocators
Author: Ted Seides
Description: So You Want to Start a Hedge Fund is a practical guide for aspiring hedge fund managers and investment professionals navigating the challenges of launching and growing a successful fund. Drawing on real-world experiences, it covers fundraising, investment strategy, team building, performance management, and allocator expectations, while offering valuable insights into avoiding common pitfalls and building a sustainable investment business.

Subject: Agriculture
Title: Sustainable Agriculture Production Using Blockchain Technology
Author: Rekh Ram Janghel, Rajesh Doriya, Jaykumar Lachure, Yogesh Kumar Rathore
Description: This book explores how blockchain and artificial intelligence are transforming modern agriculture through smarter, more transparent, and efficient supply chains. Covering applications in traceability, data management, smart farming, and ethical AI, it provides practical insights into improving agricultural productivity, sustainability, and trust from production to distribution.

Subject: Language
Title: Spanish All-In-One For Dummies: Book + Audio Online, 2nd Edition
Author: Gail Stein, Cecie Kraynak
Description: Spanish All-In-One For Dummies, 2nd Edition is a practical, beginner-friendly guide to learning Spanish for everyday communication. Covering speaking, reading, writing, grammar, verb conjugations, pronunciation, and conversation skills, it includes plenty of practice exercises and online audio to help learners build confidence and fluency at their own pace.

Subject: General
Title: Sociology, 10th Edition
Author: Anthony Giddens, Philip W. Sutton
Description: This fully revised tenth edition provides a comprehensive and accessible introduction to sociology, examining the theories, methods, and key concepts that shape the discipline. Covering topics such as class, gender, race, globalization, politics, digital society, and climate change, it helps readers understand contemporary social issues through a global and critical perspective. Ideal for introductory sociology courses, it combines academic rigor with engaging, real-world examples.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Towards a Tokenized Economy: The Convergence of Finance, Technology and Media in the Digital Economy
Author: Yoshitaka Kitao
Description: Towards a Tokenized Economy explores how blockchain, cryptocurrencies, stablecoins, and tokenization are transforming the future of global finance. Combining financial expertise with practical insights, the book explains key concepts such as Bitcoin, Web3, decentralized finance (DeFi), smart contracts, NFTs, and tokenized assets, while examining their impact on investment strategies, capital markets, and the evolving digital economy. It is an essential guide for investors, business leaders, and finance professionals navigating the next generation of financial innovation.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The 5 Voices: How to Communicate Effectively with Everyone You Lead
Author: Jeremie Kubicek, Steve Cockram
Description: 5 Voices is a practical leadership guide that helps readers discover their unique communication and leadership style while learning to understand and work effectively with others. Using five distinct “voices ”Pioneer, Connector, Creative, Guardian, and Nurturer the book explains how different people naturally approach decisions, relationships, and influence. Like learning the instruments in an orchestra, readers gain a shared vocabulary for reducing conflict, improving communication, building stronger teams, and increasing emotional intelligence in both professional and personal relationships.

Subject: General
Title: Trans Lives: Social Realities Across the Globe
Author: Raewyn Connell
Description: This insightful book examines the diverse realities of transgender lives across the world, moving beyond media stereotypes to explore the social, economic, political, and cultural forces that shape gender diversity. Covering the experiences of trans women, trans men, hijra, travesti, and other communities, it offers a thoughtful analysis of contemporary debates, inequality, healthcare, and activism while providing a global perspective on gender and social change.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: The Follower Effect: A Fable About Flipping the Script on Leadership
Author: Tony A. Bridwell
Description: The Follower Effect challenges traditional leadership thinking by showing that exceptional leaders first learn the art of effective followership. Through an engaging business story and practical insights, the book explores how emotional intelligence, humility, collaboration, and the courage to follow can strengthen leadership, build high-performing teams, and create purpose-driven organizations. It offers actionable strategies for professionals, managers, and leaders seeking to inspire lasting influence and meaningful workplace relationships.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Tastes Like Money: RumChata and the Making of a Beloved Brand
Author: Tom E. Maas
Description: Tastes Like Money is the inspiring entrepreneurial story of Tom Maas, founder of RumChata, who turned a simple kitchen experiment into a globally recognized brand. Blending personal memoir with practical business lessons, the book explores branding, sales, product quality, trademark protection, distributor relationships, and the resilience needed to build a successful business from the ground up. It is an insightful read for entrepreneurs, business leaders, and anyone interested in brand-building and innovation.

Subject: Education, Literacy
Title: The Wiley Blackwell Companion to the City, Retail, and Consumption
Author: Daniel Paiva, Pedro Guimaraes
Description: The Wiley Blackwell Companion to the City, Retail and Consumption is a comprehensive reference exploring the changing relationship between urban spaces, retail, and consumer behavior in a rapidly evolving world. Featuring contributions from international experts, it examines topics such as digital retail, omnichannel strategies, shopping districts, consumption patterns, urban governance, and sustainable city development through interdisciplinary perspectives. An essential resource for students, researchers, urban planners, and retail professionals, it provides global insights into the future of cities and commerce.

Subject: Chemistry
Title: The Fundamentals of Clinical Research: A Universal Guide for Implementing Good Clinical Practice
Author: P. Michael Dubinsky, Karen A. Henry
Description: This practical guide explains the fundamentals of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) and their application in planning, conducting, and analyzing clinical trials. Covering quality-by-design principles, regulatory requirements, ethical considerations, and the roles and responsibilities of clinical research professionals, it also features chapter-end review questions, visual summaries, and online training resources, making it an ideal resource for students and clinical trial practitioners.

Subject: Business Management, Finance & Accounting
Title: Why Moats Matter: The Morningstar Approach to Stock Investing
Author: Heather Brilliant, Elizabeth Collins
Description: Why Moats Matter is a practical guide to long-term value investing based on identifying companies with sustainable competitive advantages, or economic moats. Drawing on Morningstar's proven investment philosophy, the book explains how to evaluate businesses using key sources of competitive advantage, fundamental valuation, and cash flow analysis. It provides investors with a clear framework for identifying high-quality companies, assessing intrinsic value, and making informed investment decisions for long-term portfolio growth.

Subject: Engineering
Title: 6G Security: New Frontiers in Mobile Network Technologies and Verticals
Author: Luiz A. DaSilva
Description: 6G Security provides a comprehensive guide to the security challenges and technologies shaping next-generation 6G communication networks. Covering topics such as network slicing, blockchain, federated learning, non-terrestrial networks, millimeter-wave communications, and AI-enabled applications, the book offers practical insights into securing future mobile networks and emerging digital ecosystems. It is an essential resource for researchers, network engineers, cybersecurity professionals, and students in telecommunications.

Subject: Computer Science
Title: Untangling AI: Driving Business Success Through Enterprise Automation and AI Agents
Author: Matt Kesby
Description: Untangling AI: Driving Business Success Through Enterprise Automation and AI Agents is a practical guide to implementing artificial intelligence across modern organizations. It explains how to develop an AI adoption strategy, automate business processes with AI agents, and build the essential foundations of successful AI transformation strategy, people, execution, and technology. Ideal for business leaders and technology professionals, the book provides actionable insights for improving efficiency, fostering innovation, and adopting AI responsibly with a focus on ethics, privacy, and security.

Subject: Psychology
Title: When Life Happens: The Mindset Shift You Need to Manage Stress, Build Confidence, and Break Free
Author: Rachel L. Goldman, Pilar Guzman
Description: When Life Happens: The Mindset Shift You Need to Manage Stress, Build Confidence, and Break Free is a practical self-help guide by clinical psychologist Dr. Rachel L. Goldman that empowers readers to build resilience, manage stress, and overcome self-limiting beliefs. Blending psychological insights with actionable strategies, the book offers tools for navigating burnout, life transitions, and personal setbacks while fostering confidence, emotional well-being, and lasting personal growth. It is an invaluable resource for anyone seeking a healthier, more resilient mindset.

Subject: Economics
Title: World Inequality Report 2026
Author: Lucas Chancel, Ricardo Gomez-Carrera, Rowaida Moshrif, Thomas Piketty
Description: The World Inequality Report 2026 presents a comprehensive analysis of global trends in income, wealth, gender, taxation, and climate inequality. Drawing on research from over 200 experts worldwide, it provides authoritative data and insights into the economic, social, and political forces shaping inequality today. An essential resource for policymakers, researchers, students, and anyone seeking to understand the challenges and implications of inequality in the modern world.

Subject: Engineering
Title: 6G to Build a Sustainable Future
Author: Mikko A. Uusitalo, Patrik Rugeland, Mauro Boldi, Ahmad Nimr
Description: 6G to Build a Sustainable Future provides a comprehensive overview of the technologies, architectures, and research driving the development of next-generation 6G mobile networks. Drawing on findings from the European Hexa-X-II and related research projects, the book explores AI-native networks, sustainable system design, advanced radio technologies, end-to-end security, and future communication services. It is an essential reference for researchers, engineers, policymakers, and students interested in the future of wireless communications and sustainable digital infrastructure.
